yoruba: Àwọn onímọ̀ ìjìnlẹ̀ sáyẹ̀nsì láti ilé ìkẹ́ẹ̀kọ́ gíga ti ìsègun Stanford lọ́jọ́ ajé ti kéde ìdásílẹ̀ irinṣẹ́ ìwádìí tuntun tí ó le tó nǹkan lẹ́sẹẹsẹ pẹ̀lú bí wọ́n bá se rí: irinṣẹ́ kéreké tí a lè tẹ̀ jáde pẹ̀lú lílo irinṣẹ́ ìtẹ̀wé ìgbàlódé pẹ̀lú owó ẹyọ fún ọ̀kọ̀ọ̀kan.
english: On Monday, scientists from the Stanford University School of Medicine announced the invention of a new diagnostic tool that can sort cells by type: a tiny printable chip that can be manufactured using standard inkjet printers for possibly about one U.S. cent each.

yoruba: Olùwádìí àgbà sọ pé èyí le è tètè mú kí wọn tètè mọ nípa jẹjẹrẹ, ikọ́ fee HIV, àti ibà lára àwọn aláàrẹ̀ ni àwọn orílẹ̀ èdè tí kòní owó púpọ̀, ní ibi tí àwọn tí wọ́n ń borí àìsàn bíì jẹjẹrẹ ọyàn jẹ bí ìlàjì àwọn olówó ní ìlú náà.
english: Lead researchers say this may bring early detection of cancer, tuberculosis, HIV and malaria to patients in low-income countries, where the survival rates for illnesses such as breast cancer can be half those of richer countries.

english: 28-year-old Vidal had joined Barça three seasons ago, from Sevilla.

yoruba: Ìflrúnú hàn bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní ago mọ́kànlá (UTC+1) ní Whitehall ní wájú ẹnu ọ̀nà ilé iṣẹ́ ọlọ́pàá sí òpópónà Downing, ilé àrẹ orílẹ̀ èdè.
english: The protest started around 11:00 local time (UTC+1) on Whitehall opposite the police-guarded entrance to Downing Street, the Prime Minister's official residence.

english: Just after 11:00, protesters blocked traffic on the northbound carriage in Whitehall.

yoruba: Ní aago mọ́kànlá kọjá ogún ìsẹ́jù, iléeṣẹ́ ọlọ́ọ̀pá tí ní káwọn olùsèfẹ̀hónúhàn sún sẹ́yìn, sọ pé wọ́n nílò láti sètò ìfẹ̀hónúhàn pẹ̀lú súnkẹrẹ fàkẹrẹ tó ń kó jọ.
english: At 11:20, the police asked the protesters to move back on to the pavement, stating that they needed to balance the right to protest with the traffic building up.

yoruba: Ní nǹkan bí i aago mọ́kànlá kọjá ìṣẹ́jú mọ́kàndínlọ́gbọ̀n, ìfẹ̀hónù dé Whitehall, kọjá Trafalgar lójú ọ̀nà èbúté, kọjá Aldwych ó tún lọ sí òkè Kingsway lójúọ̀nà Holborn níbití ẹgbẹ́ Conservative ti ń se ètò ọlọ́dọọdún láwọn yàrá ilé ìtura ńlá Grand Connaught .
english: Around 11:29, the protest moved up Whitehall, past Trafalgar Square, along the Strand, passing by Aldwych and up Kingsway towards Holborn where the Conservative Party were holding their Spring Forum in the Grand Connaught Rooms hotel.

english: Each show would also provide recommendations for books that children should look for when they went to their library.

english: The storm, situated about 645 miles (1040 km) west of the Cape Verde islands, is likely to dissipate before threatening any land areas, forecasters say.

english: Fred currently has winds of 105 miles per hour (165 km/h) and is moving towards the northwest.

english: Fred is the strongest tropical cyclone ever recorded so far south and east in the Atlantic since the advent of satellite imagery, and only the third major hurricane on record east of 35°W.

yoruba: Ní ọjọ́ Kẹrìnlélógún, oṣù kẹsàn án ọdún 1759, ni Arthur Guinness fọwọ́ sí fífí St James' Gate Brewery ni Dublin, Ireland, sílẹ̀ fún yíya fún 9,000 ọdú.
english: On September 24, 1759, Arthur Guinness signed a 9,000 year lease for the St James' Gate Brewery in Dublin, Ireland.

yoruba: Iléeṣẹ́ Guinness ti dàràbà nínú okoòwò àgbáyé lẹ́yìn ọ̀tàlénígba ó dín mẹ́wàá ọdún tí ó sì ti mún bílíọ̀nù mẹ́wàá euro (bílíọ̀nù mẹ́rìnlá ó lé ẹ̀dẹ́gbẹ̀rin mílíọ̀nù dọ́là) lọ́dọdún.
english: 250 years later, Guinness has grown to a global business that turns over 10 billion euros (US$14.7 billion) every year.

yoruba: Jonny Reid tó jẹ́ alájọwà fún ikọ̀ A1GP New Zealand lónìí sàkọọ́lẹ̀ ìtàn nípa yíyára wakọ̀ ju Auckland Harbour Bridge ẹni ọdún méjìdínláàdọ́ta ti ilẹ̀ New Zealand níbàmún un pẹ̀lú òfin.
english: Jonny Reid, co-driver for the A1GP New Zealand team, today made history by driving the fastest over the 48-year-old Auckland Harbour Bridge, New Zealand, legally.

english: Mr Reid managed to drive the New Zealand's A1GP car, Black Beauty at speeds over 160km/h seven times over the bridge.

yoruba: Àwọn ọlọ́pàá ilẹ̀ New Zealand ní ìṣòro lílo ìbọn speed radar wọn láti rí bí Ọ̀gbẹ́ni Reid ṣe ń sáré tó, nítorí bí ẹwà ẹ̀nìyàn dudu ti ri, ẹ̀ẹ̀kan soso tí àwọn ọlọ́pàá yóò rí bí Ọgbẹ́ni Reid ṣe ń sáré sí ni ìgbà tí Ó sinmi díẹ̀, èyi ni ọgọ́ta kìlómíta láàrin wákàtí.
english: The New Zealand police had trouble using their speed radar guns to see how fast Mr Reid was going because of how low Black Beauty is, and the only time the police managed to clock Mr Reid was when he slowed down to 160km/h.

yoruba: Láàrin oṣù mẹ́ta tó kọjá, àwọn bí i ọgọ́rin tí wọ́n fi páńpẹ́ òfin gbé ni wọ́n ti dásílẹ̀ kúrò ní ọgbà Central Booking láláì fojú balé ẹjọ́.
english: In the last 3 months, over 80 arrestees were released from the Central Booking facility without being formally charged.

yoruba: Nínú osù kẹrin ọdún yí adájó Glynn pa àṣę ìkánilọ́wọ́kò onígbàkanná pé kí ilé iṣẹ́ náà tú àwọn tí wọ́n tí dè mọ́lẹ̀ fún wákàtí mẹ́rìnlélógún sílẹ̀ lẹ́yìn tí wọ́n gbà wọ́n sọ́dọ̀ tí wọn ò dę rí ìdájọ́ gbà láti ọ̀dọ̀ kọmíṣọ́nà kọ́ọ̀tú.
english: In April this year, a temporary restaining order was issued by Judge Glynn against the facility to enforce the release of those held more than 24 hours after their intake who did not receive a hearing by a court commissioner.

yoruba: Kọmíṣíọ́nà se àgbékalẹ̀ owó ìtanràn, tí ó bá bọ́ si, tí ó sì fì ẹ̀sùn tí agbófinró fi kàn múnlẹ̀. Ẹ̀sùn náà ni ó gbé wọnú ẹ̀rọ ayárabíàsá ìpínlẹ̀ tíwọ́n ti ń taná wádìí ẹ̀sùn náà.
english: The commissioner sets bail, if granted, and formalizes the charges filed by the arresting officer. The charges are then entered into the state's computer system where the case is tracked.

yoruba: Ìgbẹ́jọ́ náa sàmí ẹ̀tọ́ afurasí sí ṣíṣe ęjọ́ ní kíá kíá.
english: The hearing also marks the date for the suspect’s right to a speedy trial.

yoruba: Peter Costello, alákòso ìsúra orílè-èdè Australia tí o si jẹ ẹni tó sẹẹsẹẹ láti gbàjọba lówó adarí ìgbìmò ìjọba John Howard gẹ́gẹ́ bi adarí ẹgbẹ´ oẹlómìnira tí àtíléyìn hán sí ilẹ́ isẹ́ àdó olóró ní Australia.
english: Peter Costello, Australian treasurer and the man most likely to succeed Prime Minister John Howard as Liberal party leader has thrown his support behind a nuclear power industry in Australia.

english: Mr Costello said that when nuclear power generation becomes economically viable, Australia should pursue its use.

yoruba: “Tí ó bá di ti ọrọ̀ ajé, ó yẹ ká ní i. Ìyẹn ni pé, kò si ilòdì sí òfin ìpìlẹ̀ si ìdínkù agbára bi arákùnrin” Costello ṣe sọ.
english: "If it becomes commercial, we should have it. That is, there's no in-principle objection to nuclear energy" Mr Costello said.

english: According to Ansa, "police were concerned by a couple of top-level hits they feared might spark a full-blown war of succession.

english: Police said Lo Piccolo had the upper hand because he had been Provenzano's right-hand man in Palermo and his greater experience won him the respect of the older generation of bosses as they pursued Provenzano's policy of keeping as low as possible while strengthening their power network.

english: These bosses had been reined in by Provenzano when he put an end to the Riina-driven war against the state that claimed the lives of Mafia crusaders Giovanni Falcone and Paolo Borsellino in 1992."

english: Apple CEO Steve Jobs unveiled the device by walking onto the stage and taking the iPhone out of his jeans pocket.

yoruba: Brazil jẹ orílẹ̀-èdè ìlú ìjọ ẹlẹ́sin Róòmù tó pọ̀jù lọ làgbáyé, bẹ́ẹ̀ sì ní ìjọ ẹlẹ́sìn Róòmù tako ìgbéyàwó akọ pẹ̀lú akọ tàbí abo pẹ̀lú abo .
english: Brazil is the largest Roman Catholic country on Earth, and the Roman Catholic Church has consistently opposed the legalization of same-sex marriage in the country.

yoruba: Àpérò àpapọ̀ orílẹ̀èdè Brazil ti jíròrò lórí ìlànà òfin fún ọdún mẹ́wàá àti pé irú ìgbéyàwó ìlú yìí ní ó bá òfin mun ní Rio Grande do Sul.
english: The National Congress of Brazil has debated legalization for 10 years, and such civil marriages are currently only legal in Rio Grande do Sul.

yoruba: Olórí ìlú tẹ́lẹ̀ Mata Suplicy ló kọ òfin náà. Òfin tówàlọ́nà náà ti wà lọ́wọ́ Robato Jefasini, lẹ́yìn tí wọ́n ṣàtúnṣe sí.
english: The original bill was drafted by former mayor of São Paulo, Marta Suplicy. The proposed legislation, after being amended, is now in the hands of Roberto Jefferson.

yoruba: Àwọn afẹ̀hohnúhàn lérò láti gba ìfọwọ́bọ̀wé tó tó 1.2 miliọnu láti fi ṣọwọ́ sí àpérò orílẹ̀èdè ni oṣu kejìl.
english: Protesters hope to collect a petition of 1.2 million signatures to present to the National Congress in November.

yoruba: Lẹ́yìn tó hàn gbangba pé ọ̀pọ̀ àwọn ìdílé ń wá ìrànlọ́wọ́ òfin láti dojúkọ lílé jáde , ìpàdé kàn wáyé ní ogúnjọ́ oṣù kẹta ní ibùdó òfin ni agbègbè ìlà-Òòrùn ìyáwó omi òkun sínú ilé.
english: After it became apparent that many families were seeking legal help to fight the evictions, a meeting was held on March 20 at the East Bay Community Law Center for the victims of the housing scam.

yoruba: Nigbati awon olugbe bere sini pin iriri won, pupo ninu awon molebi ti o ni ipin nibe ri wipe Carolyn Wilson ti OHA ti ji ipin aabo idogo won, o si sa lo kuro ni ilu.
english: When the tenants started sharing what had occurred to them, most of the families involved suddenly realized that Carolyn Wilson of the OHA had stolen their security deposits, and skipped out of town.

yoruba: Àwọn ayalégbé ní Ọgbà Lockwood gbàgbọ wípé àwọn ìdílé ogójì míràn ma dojuko lilejade, níwọ̀n tí wọn gbọ wípé àwọn Ọlọpa OHA naa ń sẹ iwádìí àwọn ohùn ìní ilé gbogbo míràn tí o seese kí wọn ni irírí ètẹ ìtànje ilé.
english: Tenants at Lockwood Gardens believe that there may be another 40 families or more to face eviction, since they learned that OHA police are also investigating other public housing properties in Oakland that may be caught up in the housing scam.

yoruba: Àwọn ẹlẹ́gbẹ́ orin fagilé ìpàdé orin wọ́n ní pápá ìṣeré Maui War Memorial, eléyìí tó yẹ kí àwọn ènìyàn 9000 wá, wọ́n sí rá ws ẹ̀bl sí àwọn olólùfẹ́ wọn.
english: The band canceled the show at Maui's War Memorial Stadium, which was set to be attended by 9,000 people, and apologized to fans.

yoruba: Ile ise ti o n se amojuto eto, Hk Management Inc, ko so idi pataki ti won fi fagi le september 20, sugbon won kede ebi lori ailese amojuto bi o se ye ni ojo keji.
english: The band's management company, HK Management Inc., gave no initial reason when they canceled on September 20, but blamed logistical reasons by the next day.

yoruba: Wọ́n ti ti gbajúgbajà amòfin ilẹ Greek, Sakis Kechagioglou àti George Nikolakopoulos mọ́ àhámọ́ ọgbà ẹ̀wọ̀n Korydallus ti Athens, níbí wọ́n se ní wọ́n jẹ̀bi ẹ̀sùn ìwà jẹgúdújẹrá àti àjẹbánu.
english: The famous Greek lawyers, Sakis Kechagioglou and George Nikolakopoulos have been imprisoned in the Athens' jail of Korydallus, as they were found guilty of graft and corruption.

yoruba: Fún ìdí èyí,wọ́n ti se àgbéga ìbanilórúkọjẹ́ ńlá tó wáyé láàrin ìlú Greek látara ìlàlójú sáwọn ìwà tí kò bófin mun táwọn adájọ́, agbẹ́jọ́rò, alágbàwí, àtàwọn amòfin ti hù láwọn ọdún tó ti kọjá sẹ́yìn.
english: As a result of this, a big scandal within the Greek legal community has been raised through the exposure of illegal actions that judges, lawyers, solicitors and attorneys have done during the previous years.

yoruba: Ní ọ̀sẹ̀ díè sẹ́yìn, lẹyìn ìròyìn tí akọròyìn Makis Triantafylopoulos sẹ àtèjádẹ rẹ nínú gbajúgbajà àfihàn tẹlifísàn “Zoungla” ní Alpha TV, ọmọ ẹgbé ilẹ́ ìgbimọ̀ asòfin àti agbẹjórò, Petros Mantouvalos gba ìdáláre bi àwọn ọmọ ẹgbẹ́ míràn nínú ofiisi rẹ se lọ́wọ́ nínú ìwà ìbàjẹ́ àti ìlòdì sófin.
english: A few weeks ago, after the information published by the journalist Makis Triantafylopoulos in his popular Television show "Zoungla" in Alpha TV, the member of Parliament and lawyer, Petros Mantouvalos was abdicated as members of his office had been involved in illegal graft and corruption.

yoruba: Síwájú si, adájọ́ gíga Kalousis ti wà lẹ́wòn nítorí o jẹ̀bi èsùn íwá ájẹbánu àti íwá ìbàjẹ́.
english: Moreover, top judge Evangelos Kalousis is imprisoned as he found guilty of corruption and degenerate behaviour.

yoruba: Roberts kọ jalẹ lati sọ nipa akoko ti o gbagbọ ẹmi bẹrẹ si ni bẹ, ibeere ti o tobi nigba ti eniyan ba n wo ilana oyun sisẹ,wipe yo jẹ eyi ti ko ba ilana iṣe mu lati sọrọ nipa awọn oriṣiriṣi iṣẹlẹ.
english: Roberts flatly refused to say about when he believes life begins, an important question when considering the ethics of abortion, saying that it would be unethical to comment on the specifics of likely cases.

yoruba: O se atenumo oro re akoso wipe Roe v. Wade ni “ofin ilu ti o fidi mule”, ti o n se ise pataki si se deede pelu idajo ile ejo giga.
english: He did, however, reiterate his earlier statement that Roe v. Wade was the "settled law of the land", emphasizing the importance of consistent Supreme Court rulings.

yoruba: Ó tún fi yé ni pé òun gbàgbọ́ ninú ẹ̀tọ́ láti dáwà, èyí tí ìgbésẹ̀ Roe dálór.
english: He also confirmed that he believed in the implied right to privacy which the Roe decision depended upon.

yoruba: Maroochydore ti parí lórí òkè téńté àkàsọ̀, àmì ayò mẹ́fà ni ó fi la Noosa tí o wà ni ipò kejì.
english: Maroochydore had finished on top of the ladder, six points clear of Noosa in second.

yoruba: Àwọn igun méjèèjì ni yíó pàdé níkẹyìn àsekágbá ní ibi tí Noosa ti jáwé olúborí pẹ̀lú àmì mọ́kànlá.
english: The two sides would meet in the major semi final where Noosa ran out winners by 11 points.

yoruba: Ìlú Maroochydore sẹ́gun ìlú Caboolture nínú ìkópa kòmẹsẹ̀óyọ tó gbẹ̀yìn.
english: Maroochydore then defeated Caboolture in the Preliminary Final.

yoruba: Hesperonychus elizabethae je eya ebi Dromaeosauridae o si je omo egbon tabi aburo Velociraptor.
english: Hesperonychus elizabethae is a species of the family Dromaeosauridae and is a cousin of Velociraptor .

yoruba: Won gbagbo pe eye apaje yi to ni iye nigbogbo ara to si je alara gbigbona, ma n ri lori ese mejeje pelu eekana bii ti Felosirapito.
english: This fully feathered, warm blooded bird of prey was believed to have walked upright on two legs with claws like the Velociraptor.

yoruba: Èékánná rẹ̀ kan gùn ju ìkejì lọ, òhun ni ó fa orúkọ rẹ̀ Hesperonychus tí ó tún túmọ̀ sí “éèkánná ìlà oòrùn.”
english: Its second claw was larger, giving rise to the name Hesperonychus which means "western claw."

yoruba: Ní àfiikún yínyìn tó fọ́,̣ pẹ̀lú ojú Ọjọ́ tí ò rọgbọ ní ó dínà aájò ìdóòlà.
english: In addition to the crushing ice, extreme weather conditions have been hampering rescue efforts.

yoruba: Pittman dámọ̀ràn àtúnse ò ní wáyé bí kò di ọ̀sẹ̀ tó ń bọ̀.
english: Pittman suggested that conditions wouldn't improve until sometime next week.

yoruba: Gẹ́gẹ́ bí nkan tí Pitmani wí, oye àti kí yìnyìn na ṣe ki sí ló burú jù fún àwọn sílasì ní ọdún 15 tókọjá.
english: The amount and thickness of the pack ice, according to Pittman, is the worst it has been for sealers in the past 15 years.

yoruba: Ìròyìn tàn ká ní agbègbè Red Lake lónìí pé bí ìsìnkú Jeff Weise àti àwọn mẹta nínú mẹsan tó farakásá wáyé wípé ọwọ́ tẹ akẹ́kọ̀ọ́ míràn ní ìbáṣepọ̀ pẹ̀lú ìbọn yínyìn ní ilé ìwé ti ọjọ̀ kankọkànlélógún Oṣù Kẹta.
english: News spread in the Red Lake community today as funerals for Jeff Weise and three of the nine victims were held that another student was arrested in connection with the school shootings of March 21.

yoruba: Àwọn aláṣẹ sọ díẹ́ níorí iṣẹ́ tí wọ́n sì jẹ́rìsí mímú tí wọ́n mú wọn.
english: Authorities said little officially beyond confirming today's arrest.

yoruba: Àmọ́sá, èèyàn kan tó mọ̀ nípa ìwaàdí sọ fún iléeṣẹ́ Minneapolis Star-Tribune wípé Louis Jourdain tó jẹ́ ọmọọdún mẹ́rìndínlógún alága ẹ̀yà Red Lake, Floyd Jourdain ni.
english: However, a source with knowledge of the investigation told the Minneapolis Star-Tribune that it was Louis Jourdain, 16-year old son of Red Lake Tribal Chairman Floyd Jourdain.

yoruba: A kò mọ̀ ní àkókò yí àwọn àyípadà tí wọ́n ma ṣe àti nkan tó jẹ́kí wọ́n rí ọmọ okùnrin náà gbámú, ṣùgbọ́n ètò ilé ẹjọ́ fún ọ̀dọ́ ti bẹ̀rẹ̀.
english: It is not known at this time what charges will be laid or what led authorities to the boy but juvenile proceedings have begun in federal court.

yoruba: Lodin tún sọ pẹ àwọn òsisẹ́ pinu láti fagilé àtúndì ìbò láti le yọ àwọn ará Afghanistan lọ́wọ́ ìnáwó àti ewu ààbò tó lè jẹyọ nípasè ètò ìdìbò mìíràn.
english: Lodin also said officials decided to cancel the runoff in order to save Afghans the expense and security risk of another election.

yoruba: Àwọn aṣojú orílẹ̀ èdè sọ pé wọ́n ti ṣí ọkàn wọn sílẹ̀ fún òfin Ilẹ̀ Afghan láti lè pinu runoff bí èyí tí kò pọ́ndandan.
english: Diplomats said that they had found enough ambiguity in the Afghan constitution to determine the runoff as unnecessary.

yoruba: Èyí tako àbọ̀ àkọ́kọ́, tí ó sọ pé gbigbégilé àfikun ìbò ìba má ba ìlànà òfin lọ.
english: This contradicts earlier reports, which said that cancelling the runoff would have been against the constitution.

yoruba: Ọkọ̀ òfuruufún náà ti darí si Irkutsk pèlú àwọn ikọ ọmọ ogun abélé ni ìsàkósa re.
english: The aircraft had been headed to Irkutsk and was being operated by interior troops.

yoruba: A dá ìwádi lẹ̀ lati ṣe ìwád.
english: An inquiry was established to investigate.

yoruba: Il-76 ṣe pàtàkì púpọ̀ sí ikọ̀ ológun Russia àti Soviet láti 1970, bẹ́ẹ̀ náà sì ni ó ti rí ìjàǹbá ní Russia ní oṣù tó kọjá.
english: The Il-76 has been a major component of both the Russian and Soviet military since the 1970s, and had already seen a serious accident in Russia last month.

yoruba: Ní ọjọ́ keje osù kẹwàá, ẹ̀rọ ya ibi ìgbéra sọ́tọ, lálàì farapa. ilẹ̀ Russia bẹ̀rẹ̀ ní II-76s ní kéte tí ìjàm̀bá sẹlẹ̀.
english: On October 7 an engine separated on takeoff, without injuries. Russia briefly grounded Il-76s after that accident.

yoruba: Ẹgbẹ̀rin máìlì ọ̀nà ilé epo Trans-Alaska ni ó di títì pa, látàrí ẹgbẹgbẹ̀rún àgbá epo rọ̀bì to jò dànaù ni gúsù Fairbanks, ni Alaska.
english: 800 miles of the Trans-Alaska Pipeline System were closed down following a spill of thousands of barrels of crude oil south of Fairbanks, Alaska.

yoruba: Ìkùnà iná lẹ́yìn àdánwò dédé lóri ètò àṣẹ-iná, ṣokùnfa kí ihò ìrọ̀rùn ṣí sílẹ̀ epo rọ̀bí sì jò sẹ́gbẹ́ ìdí epo Forti Grili 9.
english: A power failure following a routine fire-command system test caused relief valves to open and crude oil overflowed near the Fort Greely pump station 9.

yoruba: Ṣíṣí ìdè ná sílẹ̀ jẹ́ kí ;ó ní ìtúsílẹ̀ fún ẹ̀rọ àti ìṣàn epo sí táǹkì 'tó lè di àgbá epo 55,000 mú (2.3 million gallons.
english: The valves opening allowed a pressure release for the system and oil flowed on a pad to a tank that can hold 55,000 barrels (2.3 million gallons).

yoruba: Ní ọ̀sán ọjọ́rú, táǹkì náà jò bóyá nítorí thermal expansion nínu táǹkì.
english: As of Wednesday afternoon, the tank vents were still leaking probably from thermal expansion inside the tank.

yoruba: ipele keji aye ikeru si miran ti o ni anfani lati gba 104,500 bareli ko i ti kun de enu.
english: Another secondary containment area below the tanks capable of holding 104,500 barrels was not yet filled to capacity.

yoruba: Ọ̀rọ̀ ọ̀hún lórí ètò amóhùnmáwòrán, ni ìgbà àkọ́kọ́ tí abẹnugan àgbà ilẹ̀ Iran kan gbà pé ìbáwí tí wọn fi léde ní ip.
english: The comments, live on television, were the first time that senior Iranian sources have admitted that the sanctions are having any effect.

yoruba: Wọn ti se àgbékalẹ̀ òfin fi de ìwọ̀n owó bẹ́ẹ̀ àjọ ìsọ̀kan ilẹ̀ yúròpù sì ti gbegi lé títa epo rọ̀bì sí ìta, ní èyí tí ètò ọrọ̀ ajé àwọn ara Iran fi ń gbà ìdá ọgọ́rin nínú owó̀ ilẹ̀ òkèrè wọn tó ń wọlé.
english: They include financial restrictions and a ban by the European Union on the export of crude oil, from which the Iranian economy receives 80% of its foreign income.

yoruba: Nínú àkọọ́lẹ̀ olóosòṣù rẹ tó jáde gbèyìǹ, OPEC ní ìkó epo jáde ti já wálẹ̀ fún bí i dẹ́kéèdèméjì bíi mílíọ̀nù mẹ́jì ó lé ẹgbẹ̀rin ẹgbẹ̀rún àgbá lóòjọ́.
english: In its most recent monthly report, OPEC said exports of crude had fallen to their lowest level for two decades at 2.8 million barrels per day.

yoruba: Alakoso ilu Ayatola Ali Kamneyi ti fi ifojule epo robi we “idẹkun” to bẹrẹ ki rogbodiyan musulumi ti Irani bẹrẹ ni 1979 ati ti o jẹ nkan ti o yẹ ki ilu gba ara rẹ la lọwọ e.
english: The country's supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has described the dependency on oil as "a trap" dating from before Iran's Islamic revolution in 1979 and from which the country should free itself.

yoruba: Bí kápísù bá dé inú ayé tí ó wọ inú afẹ́fẹ́, àti ní ǹkan bi í aago márùn ún òwúrọ̀ (àkókò ìlà òrùn) èyí ni wọ́n ń retí wípé yíó sàfihàn eré ráńpẹ́ fáwọn èèyàn tó wà ní àríwá California, Oregon, Nevada, àti Utah.
english: When the capsule gets to Earth and enters the atmosphere, at about 5am (eastern time), it is expected to put on quite a light show for folks in Northern California, Oregon, Nevada, and Utah.

yoruba: Kapsuli naa a jo bii irawo to n fo koja ofurufu.
english: The capsule will look much like a shooting star going across the sky.

yoruba: Kápísù náà yíò rìn bí i kìlómítà méjìlá ó lé mẹ́jọ tàbí máàlì mẹ́jọ láàrin ìṣẹ́jú kan, ó yára tó láti lọ sí San Francisco títí lọ Los Angeles láàrin ìṣẹ́jú kan.
english: The capsule will be traveling at about 12.8 km or 8 miles per second, fast enough to go from San Francisco to Los Angeles in one minute.

yoruba: Stardust yíò gbé àkọsílẹ̀ tí ó ga jùlọ kalẹ̀ pẹ̀lú bí ó se ba àkọ́sílẹ̀ ti oṣù karùn ún ọdún 1969 jẹ́ ní àkókò ìpadàbọ̀ Apollo X.
english: Stardust will set a new all-time record for being the fastest spacecraft to return to Earth, breaking the previous record set in May of 1969 during the return of the Apollo X command module.

yoruba: “O ma si lo sori apa iwo orun Kalifonia Ariwa o de ma tan ina si oku orun lati Kalifonia tit de aarin Orgoni lo si Nefada ati Idaho sinu Uta,” Tom Duxbury, oludari ise ErupeIrawo wi.
english: “It will move over the west coast of northern California and will light the sky from California through central Oregon and on through Nevada and Idaho and into Utah,” Tom Duxbury, Stardust's project manager said.

yoruba: Ìpinnú ọ̀gbẹ́ni Rudd láti buwọ́lú ojú ọjọ́ Kyoto yọ orílẹ̀ èdè Amẹríka sílẹ̀, tí yóò sì wá di orílẹ́ èdè kan ṣoṣo ní àgbáyé tí ó dàgbà tí kò sì wá́ àtúnṣe sí àjùmọ̀gbà.
english: Mr. Rudd's decision to sign the Kyoto climate accord isolates the United States, which will now be the only developed nation not to ratify the agreement.

yoruba: Ìjọba tí ó nííse pẹ̀lú ìsẹ̀dálẹ̀ ti ilẹ̀ Australia kọ̀ láti fi àṣẹ si Kyoto, sọ pé yíò se àkóbá fún ọrọ̀ ajé pẹ̀lú bí ó se kó gbogbo ara lé kíkó èédú lọ sókè òkun, nígbàtí orílẹ̀èdè bí i India àti China kò se ní ohunkóhun tó de àfojúsùn àgbéjáde èéfín wọn.
english: Australia's former conservative government refused to ratify Kyoto, saying it would damage the economy with its heavy reliance on coal exports, while countries like India and China were not bound by emissions targets.

yoruba: Ohun lo je oja to tobi ti won ru=ita ni itan gbogbo eBay.
english: It is the biggest acquisition in eBay's history.

yoruba: Iléeṣẹ́ nírètí àti wá ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ọ̀nà tí èrè yóó fi má a wọlé tí wọ́ yóó sì fi di ìlú mọ̀ọ́ká láwọn agbègbè tí ìtàkùn Skype ti gbalẹ̀, bí i China, apá ìlà oòrùn Europu àti Brazil.
english: The company hopes to diversify its profit sources and gain popularity in areas where Skype holds a strong position, such as China, Eastern Europe, and Brazil.

yoruba: Àwọn onímọ̀ sáyẹnsì ti funra sí́ Enceladus gẹ́gẹ́ bí iṣẹ́ rẹ̀, o ṣe e ṣe kí o jẹ orísun fuhn Saturn's icy E ring.
english: Scientists have suspected Enceladus as geologically active and a possible source of Saturn's icy E ring.

yoruba: Enceladus ni ó ṣe pàtàkì jùlọ nínú ètò solar, ó ń ṣe àfihàn ohun tí ó tayọ ìdá àádọ́rin nínú ìtàsán oòrun tí ó bà á.
english: Enceladus is the most reflective object in the solar system, reflecting about 90 percent of the sunlight that hits it.

yoruba: Olùgbéjáde géèmù Konami sọọ́ di mímọ̀ lónìí nínú ìwé ìròyìn ilẹ̀ Japan wípé àwọn kò ní se àgbéjáde géèmù Six Days ní Fallujah.
english: The game publisher Konami stated today in a Japanese newspaper that they will not be releasing the game Six Days in Fallujah.

yoruba: Eré náà dá lórí ìjà Fallujah ìkejì, ìjà kíkan láàrín àwọn ọmọ ogun Amerika àti Iraaki.
english: The game is based on the Second Battle of Fallujah, a vicious battle between American and Iraqi forces.

yoruba: ACMA tun wariri pe biotilejepe won fi fidio naa han lori intaneeti, Egbon Okunrin o tii lodi si awon ofin ipalenumo ero ori intaneeti tori won o ti da midia naa pada si ori saiti Egbon Okunrin.
english: The ACMA also found that despite the video being streamed on the Internet, Big Brother had not breached online content censorship laws as the media had not been stored on Big Brother's website.

yoruba: Òfin Ile Iṣẹ́ Gbohun Gbohun pèsè fún ìwọ̀nsí èròjà ayélujára, o gbúdọ̀ wá nínú sáfà kató lè pèé ní èròjà ayélujára.
english: The Broadcasting Services Act provides for the regulation of Internet content, however to be considered Internet content, it must physically reside on a server.

yoruba: Ile ise ijoba apapo ti orile ede Amerika ti o wa ni Nairobi, Kenya ti fi ikede lede pe awon “ajagun esin lati Somalia” n gbimopo lati ju ado oloro ni Kenya ati Ethiopia.
english: The United States embassy located in Nairobi, Kenya has issued a warning that "extremists from Somalia" are planning to launch suicide bomb attacks in Kenya and Ethiopia.

yoruba: Ilẹ̀ America sọ ọ́ di mímọ̀ pé àwọn gba ìròyìn látọ̀dọ̀ ẹnùkan tó farasin pé wọ́n yío lo àwọn ẹṣin ò kọ kú láti ju àdó abúgbàmù asekúpani sáwọn ibùdó gbòógì ní Ethiopia àti Kenya.
english: The U.S. says it has received information from an undisclosed source that specifically mentions the use of suicide bombers to will blow up "prominent landmarks" in Ethiopia and Kenya.

yoruba: Sááju Ètò Ojoojúmọ́ àtì Àbábọ̀ Colbert, Heck àti Johnson ti ma ń wòye àgbéjáde tí yíó fara jọ ìròyìn—àti ìjábọ̀ ìròyìn—nígbàtí wọ́n jẹ́ akẹ́ẹ̀kọ́ ní UW ní 1988.

yoruba: Láti ìgbàyẹn, àwọn Brasili ti kópa ní eré 53 fún ẹgbẹ́ ní gbogbo ìdíje wọ́n dẹ̀ ti gbá goolu 24.
english: Since then, the Brazilian has featured in 53 matches for the club in all competitions and has scored 24 goals.

yoruba: Dr.Lee náà fi ẹ̀dùn ọkàn hàn lórí ìrìyìn pé àwọn ọmọ tó wà ní ilẹ̀ Turkey ti kó kòkòrò A(H5N1) èyí tó jẹ́ ààrùn kòkòrò avian influenza láláì se àìsàn.
english: Dr. Lee also expressed his concern about reports that children in Turkey have now become infected with the A(H5N1) avian influenza virus without becoming ill.

yoruba: Àwọn ìwádí mín sèwòye wípé àrùn náà gbọ́dọ̀ lọlẹ̀ kó tó di àjàkálẹ̀ àrùn tó kárí ayé.
english: Some studies suggest that the disease must become less lethal before it can cause a global epidemic, he noted.

yoruba: Ìkọlóminú wà wípé àwọn aláìsàn lè máa kó àìsàn ran àwọn ènìyàn míràn nípa lilọ́ se àwọn iṣẹ́ òòjọ́ wọn tí kòkòrò náà bá sì wà nílẹ̀.
english: There is concern that patients may continue to infect more people by going through their daily routines if the flu symptoms remain mild.

yoruba: Leslie Aun, tí ó jẹ́ agbẹnusọ àjọ Komen, sọ pé ẹgbẹ́ náà ti ní òfin tuntun tí kò fi ààyè gba fífún àwọn àjọ tí ó wà labẹ́ ìwádìí òfin ní owó-ìfiṣẹ̀rànwọ́-fún tàbí ẹ̀bù.
english: Leslie Aun, a spokesperson for the Komen Foundation, said the organization adopted a new rule that does not allow grants or funding to be awarded to organizations that are under legal investigation.

yoruba: Ìlànà Komen yọ Ètò Òbí kúrò látàrí ìwádìí tọ́ ń lọ lọ́wọ́ bí Ètò Òbí ṣe náwó àti bí wọ́n ṣe jábọ̀ owó nípasẹ̀ Àṣójú Cliff Stearns.
english: Komen's policy disqualified Planned Parenthood due to a pending investigation on how Planned Parenthood spends and reports its money that is being conducted by Representative Cliff Stearns.

yoruba: Steni n wádi bóya wọn lo owó orí fún oyún ṣíṣẹ́ ní Ìlànà Òbí ní ipò rẹ̀ gẹ́gẹ́ bi alága Igbimo Ofasaiti àti Ìwádi, tó wà lábẹ̀ àsíya Igbimo Agbára àti Iṣòwò.
english: Stearns is investigating whether taxes are used to fund abortions through Planned Parenthood in his role as chairman of the Oversight and Investigations Subcommittee, which is under the umbrella of the House Energy and Commerce Committee.

yoruba: Gómínà Massachusetts ní ìgbà kan rí, Mitt Romney gbé igbá orókè nínú ètò ìdìbò ẹgbẹ Republican ti ilẹ̀ Florida láti díje fún ipò ààrẹ ní ọjọ́ ìṣẹ́gun pẹ̀lú ìwọn ìdá mẹ́rìndínláàdọ́ta ìbo.
english: Former Massachusetts governor Mitt Romney won the Florida Republican Party presidential primary election on Tuesday with over 46 percent of the vote.

yoruba: Ẹni tí ó ti fìgbà kan jẹ́ abẹnugan ilé nílẹ̀ U.S, Newt Gingrich ló gbé ipò kejì pẹ̀lú ìdá méjìlélọ́gbọ̀n.
english: Former U.S. Speaker of the House Newt Gingrich came in second with 32 percent.

yoruba: Gẹ́gẹ́ bíi ilú onípòkíní-gba-gbogbo-rẹ̀, ìlú Florida rán èyàn aadọ́ta lọsí Romni, èyí tìí síwájú ní eré ìbò Ẹgbẹ́ Òmìnir.
english: As a winner-takes-all state, Florida awarded all fifty of its delegates to Romney, pushing him ahead as the front-runner for the Republican Party nomination.

yoruba: Àwọn agbátẹrù ìfẹ̀hónúhàn ní àwọn èèyàn tó ju ọgọ́rùn ún ẹgbẹ̀rún kan ni o jáde ní àwọn ìlú German bí i Berlin, Cologne, Hamburg, àti Hanover.
english: Organisers of the protest said about 100,000 people turned up in German cities such as Berlin, Cologne, Hamburg, and Hanover.

yoruba: Ní Berlin, iléeṣẹ́ ọlọ́pàá ti ṣí àwọn olùsèfẹ̀inúhàn ẹgbẹ̀rún mẹ́fà ó lé ẹ̀ẹ́dẹ́gbẹ̀ta.
english: In Berlin, police estimated 6,500 protestors.

yoruba: Ìfẹ̀húnú hàn tún wáyé ní ìlú Pàrísì, Sofia ní Bọ̀lùgáríà, Fílínọ́s ní Lìtùánà, Fàlẹ́tà ní Málítà, Tàlínì ní Ẹsitóníà, àti Ẹdínbọ́gù àti Gílásígò ní Ìlú Scotland.
english: Protests also took place in Paris, Sofia in Bulgaria, Vilnius in Lithuania, Valetta in Malta, Tallinn in Estonia, and Edinburgh and Glasgow in Scotland.

yoruba: Nìlú Londoni o tó bìí èyàn 200 tó fìfè éhónú hàn níwájú dìé nínú àwọn ilé isé nla tó n di ètò ìlò mu.
english: In London, about 200 people protested outside some major copyright holders' offices.

yoruba: Ni osu to koja, itako nla pupo wa ni Polandi nigbati orileede naa fowo si ACTA, to muu ki ijoba Polandi pinu lati ma fowo si adehun naa, nisiyin.
english: Last month, there were major protests in Poland when that country signed ACTA, which has led to the Polish government deciding not to ratify the agreement, for now.

yoruba: Latvia àti Slovakia ti mún ìdádúró bá ìlànà àti darapọ̀ mọ́ ACTA.
english: Latvia and Slovakia have both delayed the process of joining ACTA.

yoruba: Ìtúsílẹ̀ Ęranko àti Ęgbẹ́ Ọlọ́ba tó Lòdí sí fífi àwọn ẹranko jìyà (RSPCA) wọ́n ti ń pè fún fífí kámẹ́rà ìṣóni tó n jẹ́ CCTV sí gbogbo ilé pęran pęran ní Ìlú Australian.
english: Animal Liberation and the Royal Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Animals (RSPCA) are again calling for the mandatory installation of CCTV cameras in all Australian abattoirs.

yoruba: RSPCA New South Wales olùbẹ̀wò David O'Shanessy sọ fún ABC pé àkíyèsí àti ìbẹ̀wò ibi ti wọ́n ti n pa ẹran niláti jẹ́ ibi tí gbogbo ènìyàn ms ní Australia.
english: RSPCA New South Wales chief inspector David O'Shannessy told the ABC that surveillance and inspections of abattoirs should be commonplace in Australia.

yoruba: “CCTV ma fi àmì tó lágbára ṣọwọ́ sí àwọn ènìyàn tó ṣe iṣẹ́ pẹ̀lú àwọn ẹranko nítorí àláfíà wọn ló jù.”
english: "The CCTV would certainly send a strong signal to those people working with animals that their welfare is of the highest priority."

yoruba: Máàpù ìwáádìí ẹ̀kọ nípa ilẹ̀ ohun ìwárìrì-ilẹ̀ ti orìlé-èdè, Amerika sàfihàn pẹ́ ́àwọǹ ìwárìrì-ilẹ kò sẹlẹ̀ ni Iceland ni ọ̀sẹ̀ tó sáájú.
english: The United States Geological Survey international earthquake map showed no earthquakes in Iceland in the week prior.

yoruba: ọfíìsì Icelandic Meteorological náà kò ní àkọsílẹ̀ ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ ni agbègbè Helka láti bí wakàtí méjìdín ní àádọ́ta sẹ́yì.
english: The Icelandic Meteorological Office also reported no earthquake activity in the Hekla area in the past 48 hours.

yoruba: Ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ ìwárìrì ilẹ̀ tó ní ipa mú àbájáde dé bá ibi àyípadà àkọ́kọ́ tó wáyé ní ọjọ́ Kẹ̀wá Oṣù Kẹta ní àríwá ìlà-Òòrùn igun ní bi àpapò àwọn òkè.
english: The significant earthquake activity resulting in the phase change had took place on March 10 on the northeast side of the volcano's summit caldera.

yoruba: Wọ́n ròyìn orun tí kò jẹ mọ́ jẹmọ́ ìṢesí folkano ní ẹsẹ̀ oké náà.
english: Dark clouds unrelated to any volcanic activity were reported at the base of the mountain.

yoruba: Kùrukùru ń fa ìrújú lórí bóyá ìbúgbàmù ti wáyé.
english: The clouds presented the potential for confusion as to whether an actual eruption had taken place.

yoruba: Ọkọ̀ ojú omi náà gbé ọgọ́fà sí ọgọ́jọ ìwọ̀n mita epo nígbà tí ó dẹnu kọlẹ̀ tí ìjì àti ìgbì tì í sínú ibi ọkọ̀ omi .
english: The Luno had 120–160 cubic metres of fuel aboard when it broke down and high winds and waves pushed it into the breakwater.

yoruba: Oko ofurufu gba awom mejila to je osise inu baalu na la ati pe enikan lo fi imu sese.
english: Helicopters rescued the twelve crewmembers and the only injury was a broken nose.

yoruba: Ọkọ̀ ojú omi 100 metre tí padà láti lọ gbé àwọn ajílẹ̀ àwọn òṣìṣk sì bẹ̀rù pé ọkọ̀ náà lè dànù lọ́nà.
english: The 100-metre ship was en route to pick up its usual fertiliser cargo and initially officials feared the vessel could spill a load.

yoruba: Àbá àtúnse tigbawájú ilé méjéèjì kọjá lọ́dún 2011.
english: The proposed amendment already passed both houses in 2011.

yoruba: Àyípadà wáyé níjòòkó ilé ìgbìmọ̀ asòfin nígbà tí Ilé Igbìmọ̀ Asojúsòfin pa ìdájọ́ àkọ́kọ́ rẹ́, tí wọ́n sì tí ilé ìgbímọ̀ asòfin àpapọ̀ sì buwọ́lu àbá tó fara pẹ́ẹ lọ́jọ́ ajé.
english: A change was made this legislative session when the second sentence was deleted first by the House of Representatives and then was passed in a similar form by the Senate Monday.

yoruba: Ìkùnà ọ̀rọ̀ kejì, tí ó gbèrò láti fòpin sí same sex civil unions, lè ṣí ìlẹ́kùn fún civil union ní ọjọ ìwájú.
english: The failure of the second sentence, which proposes to ban same-sex civil unions, could possibly open the door for civil unions in the future.

yoruba: Ní sísèntèlé ìlànà náà, àwọn ìgbìmò asòfin ti a máà yan máà tún sẹ àgbéyèwò HJR-3, ní odún 2015 tàbí 2016 kó ma sàíj´ ìlànà.
english: Following the process, HJR-3 will be reviewed again by the next elected legislature in either 2015 or 2016 to remain in process.

yoruba: Yàtọ̀ ise takun takun ni ṣíṣe olùdarí, ara àwọn ohun tí Fọ́tíyà gbéṣe ni ìdájíjẹsílẹ̀ ní ọdún 1973 láti takò nkan tí ó jọmọ́ ìpanilẹ́numọ́ ti òṣèl.
english: Vautier's achievements outside of directing include a hunger strike in 1973 against what he viewed as political censorship.

yoruba: Àyípadà bá òfin ilẹ Faransé. À ti ọdún márùndínlógún ló ti bẹ̀rẹ̀ ìjàgbara nígbà tí ó darápọ̀ mọ́ àwọn ajagun ilẹ̀ faransé lákòókò ogun àgbáyé kejì.
english: French law was changed. His activism went back to age 15 when he joined the French Resistance during World War II.

yoruba: O fi orúkọ rẹ sílẹ̀ sínú ìwé 1998.
english: He documented himself in a 1998 book.

yoruba: Lọ́dún !960, ó padà sí ilẹ̀ olómìnira tuntun Algeria láti kọ́ ìdarí fíìmù.
english: In the 1960s he headed back to newly-independent Algeria to teach film directing.

yoruba: Japanese judoka Hitoshi Saito, olúborí Olympic gold medals méjì, kú ni ọmọ ọdún mẹrìnléláàdọ́ta.
english: Japanese judoka Hitoshi Saito, winner of two Olympic gold medals, has died at the age of 54.

yoruba: Jẹjẹrẹ òróòro ni wọn kéde pé ó fa ikú náà.
english: The cause of death was announced as intrahepatic bile duct cancer.

yoruba: O ṣe aláìsi ní Osaka ní Ọjọ́ Ìségun.
english: He died in Osaka on Tuesday.

yoruba: Bí ó ti jẹ́ ọ̀gágun ní ìgbà kan rí fún ìdíje Olympic àti ti olúborí àgbáyé, bákan náà ni Saito jẹ́ the alága ìgbìmọ̀ gbogbogbò fún ìdíje Judo ti ilẹ̀ Japan títí dí ọjọ́ ikú rẹ̀.
english: As well as a former Olympic and World champion, Saito was the All Japan Judo Federation training committee chairman at the time of his death.

yoruba: Ó kéré jù ọgọ́rún àwọn ènìyàn ló wá sí àpèjẹ náà, láti ṣàjọyọ àṣeyẹ àkọ́kọ́ tókọtaya tí ọja ìgbeyàwó wọ́n jẹ ọdún tó kọjá.
english: At least 100 people had attended the party, in order to celebrate the first anniversary of a couple whose wedding was held last year.

yoruba: Ètò ayẹyẹ nígbàkanrí ni wọ́n sàgbékalẹ̀ lọ́jọ́ míràn gẹ́gẹ́ bí àwọn aláṣẹ se wí.
english: A formal anniversary event was scheduled for a later date, officials said.

yoruba: Tọkọ tí ìyàwó ti Ṣe ìyàwó ní Tegsaasi ọdún kan sẹ́yìn , wọ́n wá láti bá àwọn ọ̀rẹ́ àti mọ̀lẹ́bí won yọ.
english: The couple had married in Texas one year ago and came to Buffalo to celebrate with friends and relatives.

yoruba: Ọkọ rẹ̀ ẹni ọgbọ̀n ọdún tí wọ́n bí ní Buffalo ní ó jẹ́ ọ̀kan lára àwọn mẹ́rin tí wọ́n sekúpa nínú ìyìnbọn pani, sùgbọ́n ìyàwó rẹ̀ kò fara pa.
english: The 30-year-old husband, who was born in Buffalo, was one of the four killed in the shooting, but his wife was not hurt.

yoruba: Karno jẹ́ gbajúgbajà olùkọ́ èdè òyìnbó, ṣùgbọ́n ó tún jẹ́ aláríyànjiyàn olùkọ́ tó ṣiṣẹ́ lábẹ́ Modern Education and king’s Glory to sọpé àwọn ní ẹgbẹ̀rún mẹ́sàn-án akẹ́kọ̀ọ́ tó ń lékè níbi iṣẹ́ wọn.
english: Karno is a well-known but controversial English tutor who taught under Modern Education and King's Glory who claimed to have 9,000 students at the peak of his career.

yoruba: Ninu ikosile re o lo awon oro to le ni imo awon obi won ni o de ma n lo oro buruku ni kilaasi.
english: In his notes he used words which some parents considered coarse, and he reportedly used profanity in class.

yoruba: Ẹ̀kọ́ ìgbàlódé fẹ̀sùn kàán fún ṣíṣàtẹ̀jáde ìpolówó tó pọ̀ nínu ọkọ̀ akérò láì gba àṣẹ tó sì parọ́ nípa síso pé òhun ní olùkọ́ Èdè gẹ̀ẹ́sì.
english: Modern Education accused him of printing large advertisements on buses without authorisation and lying by saying that he was the chief English tutor.

yoruba: Wọn ti kàn lẹ́sùn jíja olè iṣé agbárí , ṣùgbọ́n wọn o gbé lọ sí ile ẹjọ́.
english: He has also been accused previously of copyright infringement, but was not charged.

yoruba: Akẹkọ nigba kan ri sọ wipe ‘oun a ma sọ isọkusọ ninu yara ikawe, a ma kọni ni ọgbọn ibasepọ ninu iwe, o si dabi ọrẹ si gbogbo awon akẹkọ.’
english: A former student said that he 'used slang in class, taught dating skills in notes, and was just like the students' friend.'

yoruba: Nígbà ọgbọ̀n ọdún sẹ́yìn,pẹ̀lú jíjẹ́ ìpínlẹ̀ tó fọwọ́sowọ́pọ̀, China ti ní ètò ọrọ̀ ajé tó ga jù.
english: During the last three decades, despite officially remaining a communist state, China has developed a market economy.

yoruba: Abe idari Deng Xiaoping ni wọn ti kọ́kọ́ sẹ àtúnsẹ sí ọrọ̀ ajẹ́.
english: The first economic reforms were made under the leadership of Deng Xiaoping.

yoruba: Lati igba naa, oko owo ilu China ti gbera so ni aadorun igba.
english: Since then, China's economic size has grown by 90 times.

yoruba: Fún Ìgbà àkọ́kọ́ ní ọdún tó kọjá ní Ṣina fí ọkọ̀ púpọ̀ ránṣẹ́ sí òkè òkun jú Jamani lọ, o tún kọjá Ilu Amerika ni ipò ilu tó ní ọjà tó tóbi jù nínú òwò náà.
english: For the first time, last year China exported more cars than Germany and surpassed the United States as the biggest market for this industry.

yoruba: GDP ti orile ede China le tobi ju ti orile ede United States lo laarin ogun odun.
english: China's GDP could be larger than the United States within two decades.

yoruba: Tropical Storm Danielle, orúkọ ìjì kẹrin ti ọdún 2010 àkókò ìjì líle Atlantic, ti kórajọ ní ìla oòrùn Atlantic Ocean.
english: Tropical Storm Danielle, fourth named storm of the 2010 Atlantic hurricane season, has formed in the eastern Atlantic Ocean.

yoruba: Ìjì líle tó wà ní bí i ẹgbẹ̀rún mẹ́ta máìlì láti Miami, Florida ti ní atẹ́gùn tótó ogójì mph (64 kph).
english: The storm, located approximately 3,000 miles from Miami, Florida, has maximum sustained winds of 40 mph (64 kph).

yoruba: Àwọn onímọ̀ ẹ̀rọ ní Ibùdó Ìjì líle Orílẹ̀ èdè sọ àsọtẹ́lẹ̀ pé Danielle ma ní agbára sí ìjì líle tó bá di Wednesday.
english: Scientists at the National Hurricane Center predict that Danielle will strengthen to a hurricane by Wednesday.

yoruba: Gẹ́gẹ́ bí ìjì náà ṣe jìnà si à ti dé ilẹ̀, o túbọ̀ ṣòro láti mọ ipa rẹ̀ lórí ilẹ̀ Amẹríkà tàbí Caribbean.
english: As the storm is far from landfall, it remains difficult to assess potential impact to the United States or Caribbean.

yoruba: Wọ́n bi ní ìpínlẹ̀ Croatian, Zagreb, Bobek jẹ́ ìlúmọ̀ọ́lá nígbàtí ò ń gbá bọ́ọ̀lù fún Partizan Belgrade.
english: Born in the Croatian capital, Zagreb, Bobek gained fame while playing for Partizan Belgrade.

yoruba: Ó dara pọ̀ mọ́wọn ní ọdún 1945, ó sì dúró di ọdún 1958.
english: He joined them in 1945 and stayed until 1958.

yoruba: Ní àkókò tó lò pẹ̀lú ikọ̀ náà ó jẹ góòlù mẹ́tàlénírinwó ní ìkópa ọ̀tàlénírinwó ó lé mẹjọ.
english: During his time with the team, he scored 403 goals in 468 appearances.

yoruba: Kó sẹ́lòmín tó ṣeré jù tàbí jẹ góòlù jù fún ikọ̀ agbábọ̣̀ọ̀lù ju Bobek.
english: No one else has ever made more appearances or scored more goals for the club than Bobek.

yoruba: Ní 1995 oun ni wọn dìbò fun gégé bi òsèré tí o dárajù nií ìtàn-àkọsílè Partizan.
english: In 1995 he was voted the best player in Partizan's history.

yoruba: Ayẹyẹ náà bẹ̀rẹ̀ pẹ̀lú àwọn eré pàtàkì látọwọ́ gbajúgbajù ẹgbẹ́ àgbáyé Cirque du Soleil.
english: The celebrations started with a special show by the world-renowned group Cirque du Soleil.

yoruba: Àwọn olórin stanbul State Symphony, àwọn akọrin Janissary àtàwọn olórin Fatih Erkoç tó fi mọ́ Müslüm Gürses ló tẹ̀le.
english: It was followed by the Istanbul State Symphony Orchestra, a Janissary band, and the singers Fatih Erkoç and Müslüm Gürses.

yoruba: Nígbà tí Whirling Dervishes dé sórí ìtàgé.
english: Then Whirling Dervishes took to the stage.

yoruba: Arabirin onidan ara Turkeyi - Sezen Aksu seré pẹ̀lú olórin tẹ́nọ̀ tó wá láti Italy Alessandro Safina ati olorin ara Greek singer Haris Alexiou.
english: Turkish diva Sezen Aksu performed with the Italian tenor Alessandro Safina and Greek singer Haris Alexiou.

yoruba: Níparí, àwọn ẹgbẹ́ oníjó ti Tọ́kì - Iná Anatoliya, ṣe eré “Troyi”.
english: To finish, Turkish dance group Fire of Anatolia performed the show "Troy".

yoruba: Peter Lenz,ọmọ ọdún mẹ́tàlá a fi kẹ̀kẹ́ sáré ìje jáde láyé lẹ́yìn àkọlù tó wáyé ni ojú ọ̀nà márosẹ̀ Indianapolis.
english: Peter Lenz, a 13-year-old motorcycle racer, has died after being involved in a crash at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway.

yoruba: Ní àkókò tí ó ń gbáradì, Lenz subu lórí alùpùpù rẹ̀, akẹgbẹ́ rẹ̀, Xavier Zayat sì rọ́ọ̀lú.
english: While on his warm-up lap, Lenz fell off his bike, and was then struck by fellow racer Xavier Zayat.

yoruba: Àwọn onímọ̀ ìlera tó wà ní ojú iṣẹ́ kọjú sí lẹ́sẹ̀kësẹ̀, wọ́n dẹ̀ gbée lọ sí ilé ìwòsàn agbègbè ní ibí tí o ti gbẹ́mímì.
english: He was immediately attended to by the on-track medical staff and transported to a local hospital where he later died.

yoruba: Zayat kò farapa ní ibi ìjànmbá ọkọ̀.
english: Zayat was unhurt in the accident.

yoruba: Nípa ọ̀rọ̀ towódé tò gba gbogbo àgbáyé, Sapatero sì n wípé “ọ̀nà ìṣe owó wà lára ajé, pàápàá èya ara pàtàkì.
english: Regarding the global financial situation, Zapatero continued by saying that "the financial system is a part of the economy, a crucial part.

yoruba: A ni isoro eto isuna ni nkan bi odun kan seyin, eyi ti o feju keke ni bi osu meji seyin, eyiti mo lero ni bayi wipe ojo isuna ti bere si ni gbera so pada bayi.”
english: We have a year-long financial crisis, which has had its most acute moment in the past two months, and I think now the financial markets are beginning to recover."

yoruba: Ní ọ̀sẹ̀ tó kọjá, ìròyìn Naked News kéde pé òun yó málèkún bá èdè àgbéyé fún ìjábọ̀ ìròyìn pẹ̀lú ìgbóhùnsáfẹ́fẹ́ mẹ́ta tuntun.
english: Last week, Naked News announced that it would dramatically increase its international language mandate to news reporting, with three new broadcasts.

yoruba: Wọ́n ti ń jábọ̀ ní èdè gẹ̀ẹ́sì àti Japan, àjọ àgbáyé ti ń sàfilọ́lẹ̀ ìlàna ètò lédè ilẹ̀ Spain, Italy, àti Korea fún ẹ̀rọ amóhùnmáwòrán, ìtàkù ayélujára àtàwọn ẹ̀rọ ìbáraẹnisọ̀rọ̀.
english: Already reporting in English and Japanese, the global organization is launching Spanish, Italian, and Korean-language programs, for television, the web, and mobile devices.

yoruba: “Orí kómi yọ, ohunkohun kò sẹlẹ̀ sí mi, sùgbọ́n mo rí ìran ikú, bí àwọn ènìyàn se ń gbìyànjú àti fọ́ ojú fèrèsé fi jáde.
english: "Luckily nothing happened to me, but I saw a macabre scene, as people tried to break windows in order to get out.

yoruba: Àwọn ènìyàn bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí ni ju àga lu fèrèsé, ṣùgbọ́n àwọn fèrèsé náà kò lè fọ.
english: People were hitting the panes with chairs, but the windows were unbreakable.

yoruba: Ọ̀kan nínú àwọn gíláàsì fèrèsé padà fọ́, wọ́n sì bẹ̀rẹ̀ sí ń gba ojù fèrèsé náà jáde,” Ọ̀kan nínú àwọn tó yè Franciszek Kowal ló sọ bẹ́ẹ̀.
english: One of the panes finally broke, and they started to get out by the window," said survivor Franciszek Kowal.

yoruba: Àwọn ìràwọ̀ ma n taná nítorí agbára tón jáde nígbàtí aidrojini atọ́mu ba papò (tàbí sopọ̀) láti di ẹ́límẹ́ntì tó wúwo.
english: Stars give off light and heat because of the energy made when hydrogen atoms are merged (or fused) together to form heavier elements.

yoruba: Àwọn onímọ̀ ìjìnlẹ̀ sáyẹ̀nsì ti ń siṣẹ́ láti se àgbékalẹ̀ ohun tó le má a mún agbára wá ní ọ̀nà kan náà.
english: Scientists are working to create a reactor that can make energy in the same way.

yoruba: Amo, Ọ̀rọ yí i ni rọgbọ lati Ṣe, ó ma gbà ọdún púpọ̀ kató rí àpere kíkò riakto ajokanpọ̀.
english: This, however, is a very hard problem to solve and will take many years before we see useful fusion reactors built.

yoruba: Abẹ́rẹ́ onírin fò lórí omi látàrí ọwọ́ lílé ojú omi.
english: The steel needle floats on top of the water because of surface tension.

yoruba: Surface tension ṣẹlẹ̀ nítorí water molecules ní orí òkè omi fa ara wọn ju bí air molecules lọ eléyìí tó wà ní òkè wọn.
english: Surface tension happens because the water molecules at the surface of the water are strongly attracted to each other more than they are to the air molecules above them.

yoruba: Àwọn patikulu náà màa ǹ sẹ àwọn àyè àifojúrí orí ojú omi èyí tí o fàyè gbà àwọn nkan bi abẹ́rẹ́ láti léèfó téńté sórí omi.
english: The water molecules make an invisible skin on the water’s surface that allows things like the needle to float on top of the water.

yoruba: Abẹ náà lórí síkààtì yìnyín ìgbàlódẹ́ ní igun olújúmẹ́jì pẹ̀lú ihò róbótó láàrin wọn. Àwọn igi méjèèjì fàyègbà láti di yìnyín mu dada, àní nígbàtí ó bá di títẹ̀.
english: The blade on a modern ice skate has a double edge with a concave hollow between them. The two edges allow for a better grasp of the ice, even when tilted.

yoruba: Nítorí wípé wọ́n yọgun díẹ̀ sí abẹ́ abẹfẹ́lẹ́, bí abẹfẹ́lẹ́ bá yí sí igun kan tàbí òmíràn, igun èyí tó fatakan yìnyín náà yó yọgun.
english: Because the bottom of the blade is slightly curved, as the blade tilts to one side or the other, the edge which is in contact with the ice also curves.

yoruba: Èyí fa kí àwọn tó ń fi ǹkan bí táyà rìn yí, tí ǹkan bí táyà náà bá yí sí apá ọ̀tún, ó ma yí sí apá ọ̀tún, tí ó bá yí sí òsì, ǹkan bí táyà náà ma yí sí òsì.
english: This causes the skater to turn. If the skates tilt to the right, the skater turns right, if the skates tilt to the left, the skater turns left.

yoruba: Láti padà sí ipò agbára titélè, wọn gbọdọ̀ mú ìfikún agbára èyí tí wọn gba láti ara ìmólẹ̀ kúrò.
english: To return to their previous energy level, they must get rid of the extra energy they got from the light.

yoruba: Bi won se ma n seyi nipe won a ma yo ina kekere ti an pe ni “foton.”
english: They do this by emitting a tiny particle of light called a "photon".

yoruba: Àwọn onímọ̀ sáyẹ́nsì ma n pè ní “stimulated emission of radiation” torí àwọn átọ́mù ma ń fura sí iná tó ràn èyí ṣokùn fa kí fotoni ina maa jáde,iná dẹ̀ jẹ́ irúfẹ́ redieṣọ́ni.
english: Scientists call this process "stimulated emission of radiation" because the atoms are stimulated by the bright light, causing the emission of a photon of light, and light is a type of radiation.

yoruba: Àwòrán tókàn ṣàfihàn atoms tó ń jáde láti photons. Lóòtọ́, ní ojú ayé photons kéré gan jú àwọn tí à ń rí nínu àwòrán.
english: The next picture shows the atoms emitting photons. Of course, in reality photons are a lot smaller than those in the picture.

yoruba: Àwọn ìwọn iná tún kéré ju àwọn n´ kan tó kórajọ di àwọn ìwọn nkan tó gba àyè tó kéré jùlọ.
english: Photons are even smaller than the stuff that makes up atoms!

yoruba: Lẹ́yìn ọgarun wákàtí ìṣẹ́ ṣíṣe iná gílóbù ná jó kò sí ṣiṣẹ́ mọ́.
english: After hundreds of hours of operation the filament in the bulb eventually burns out and the light bulb no longer works.

yoruba: Gílóòbù tí nílò à ti pààrọ̀. O sé pàtàkì láti kíyè sára bí a bá ń pààrọ̀ gílòbù.
english: The light bulb then needs replacing. It is necessary to be careful in replacing the light bulb.

yoruba: Àkọ́kọ́, ibí ti a ti ń tan iná ní láti wà ní pípa tàbí kí a yọ gbogbo wáyà iná náà dàn.
english: First, the switch for the light fixture needs to be turned off or the cable disconnected.

yoruba: Ìdí ni wípé iná tó ń gba ara ihò níbi tí apákan irin tí bọ́ọ̀bù wà le gbé ọ bí o bá fọwọ́ kan inú ihò àbí ti ibùjókòó irin bọ́ọ̀bù nígbàtí apákan rẹ̀ sì wà nínú ihò.
english: This is because electricity flowing into the socket where the metallic part of bulb sits can give you a severe electric shock if you touch the inside of the socket or the metal base of the bulb while it is still partly in the socket.

yoruba: Ẹ̀yà ara pàtàkì fún ìgbẹ́jẹ̀ kiri ni ọkàn, èyí tó má ń pín ẹ̀jẹ̀ káàkiri.
english: The major organ of the circulatory system is the heart, which pumps the blood.

yoruba: Túùbù tí a mọ̀ sí arteries a máa mú ẹ̀jẹ̀ kúrò nínú ọkàn, nígbàtí túùbù tí a mọ̀ sí isan, a máa mú kí ẹ̀jẹ̀ wọ inú ọkàn. Túùbù tí ó kéré jùlọ ni a mọ̀ sí capillaries.
english: Blood goes away from the heart in tubes called arteries and comes back to the heart in tubes called veins. The smallest tubes are called capillaries.

yoruba: Eyín ẹranko tí a mọ̀ sí oníwo mẹta kòbá má lè da ewé lásán gé ṣùgbọ́n yóò ba ẹ̀ka àti gbòngbò jẹ.
english: A triceratops' teeth would have been able to crush not only leaves but even very tough branches and roots.

yoruba: Àwọn onímò sayensi kan lérò wípé àwọn ẹronko dáínósọ̀ jẹ àwọn ẹwẹ́ cycads, àwọn ẹwẹ́ tó wọ́pọ̀ nínú ìgbà Cretaceous.
english: Some scientists think Triceratops ate cycads, which are a type of plant that was common in the Cretaceous.

yoruba: Àwọn ewé yí jọ igi ọ̀pẹ́ kékeré pẹ̀lú ewé tó gùn jáde tó mú.
english: These plants look like a small palm tree with a crown of sharp, spiky leaves.

yoruba: Ẹranko Triceratop oníwo mẹ́ta ti lè lo ęnú rẹ̀ tó le láti ya àwọn ewé kúrò kótó ję ẹ̀yìn igi náà.
english: A Triceratops could have used its strong beak to strip off the leaves before eating the trunk.

yoruba: Àwọn onímọ̀ sáyẹ̀nsì ti sọ̀ ọ di mímọ̀ pé àwọn ewéko yìí ní oró púpọ̀, torína kò dájú pé ẹran dínósọ̀ kankan má a lè jẹ́ bí b ẹyẹ odídẹrẹ́ ( tó jẹ́ ìran dínósọ̀) ó lè jẹ ewé tàbí èso olóró.
english: Other scientists argue that these plants are very poisonous so it is unlikely that any dinosaur ate them, even though today the sloth and other animals like the parrot (a descendant of the dinosaurs) can eat poisonous leaves or fruit.

yoruba: Báwo ni òfin giga ṣe lè fà mí wálẹ̀? tí o bá dúró lórí ohun ti ó rí pẹrẹsẹ, o kò ní níwọ̀n púpọ̀ bí ìgbà tí o dúró lórí aye.
english: How would Io's gravity pull on me? If you stood on the surface of Io, you would weigh less than you do on Earth.

yoruba: Ẹni tí ó wọn 200 pounds (90kg) ní Ayé ma wọn 36 pounds (16kg). Nítorínà agbára yẹn ma fà ọ́.
english: A person who weighs 200 pounds (90kg) on Earth would weigh about 36 pounds (16kg) on Io. So the gravity, of course, pulls less on you.

yoruba: Oorun ko ni erun ti a le duro le lori bi Aye se ni. Gbogbo ara orun ni a se pelu gaasi, ina ati pilasima.
english: The Sun doesn't have a crust like the Earth that you can stand on. The whole Sun is made out of gases, fire, and plasma.

yoruba: Afẹ́fẹ́ ma ń tíńrín sí i, bí o bá se n jìnnà sí ààrin òòrùn.
english: The gas becomes thinner as you go farther from the center of the Sun.

yoruba: Ibi apá kan níta tí a lè̀rí nígbà tàà bá wo oòrùn ní à ń pè̀ ní àwòrán-àyíká, èyí tó túnmò sí “iná bọlù”.
english: The outer-part we see when we look at the Sun is called the photosphere, which means "ball of light".

yoruba: Níbí ẹgbẹ̀rún mẹ́ta ọdún tó yá, ní ọdún 1610, awòràwọ̀ ọmọ ilẹ̀ Italy, Galileo Galilei lo ohun amóhuntóbi láti wo bóyá Venus nípele, níbi òṣùpá se ní.
english: About three thousand years later, in 1610, Italian astronomer Galileo Galilei used a telescope to observe that Venus has phases, just as the moon does.

yoruba: Ìpelenípele ma ń wáyé nítorí pé igun kan ti Venus (tàbí ti òṣùpá) tó kọjú sí òòrùn ma ń rí ìmọ́lẹ̀. Ìpele Venus sagbátẹrù iṣẹ́ ìwádìí Copernicus tó sọ wípé ayé ń yípo òòrùn.
english: Phases happen because only the side of Venus (or of the Moon) facing the Sun is lighted. The phases of Venus supported the theory of Copernicus that the planets go around the Sun.

yoruba: Nigbanaa, lẹyin ọdun diẹ ni ọdun 1639, ara Gẹẹsi awoirawọ kan to n jẹ Jerimaya Horiki ri irin ajo Fenus.
english: Then, a few years later in 1639, an English astronomer named Jeremiah Horrocks observed a transit of Venus.

yoruba: Orílẹ̀èdè England ti ní ìrírí àlàáfíà ọlọ́jọ́ pípẹ́ lẹ́yìn ìṣẹ́gun ilẹ̀ Danelaw.
english: England had experienced a long period of peace after the reconquest of the Danelaw.

yoruba: Àmọ́ ní 991 Ẹ́tẹ́rẹ́ẹ̀dì bá ẹgbẹ́ ogun Fáikini pàdé tí ó tóbi jù gbogbo ẹgbẹ́ ogun lọ láti ìgbà tí Gútírùmi tí ó wáyé ní ọgọ́run ọdún tí ó ṣájú.
english: However in 991 Ethelred was faced with a Viking fleet larger than any since Guthrum's a century earlier.

yoruba: Olaf Trygvasson ṣáàjú ọks náà, ará Norway tó ní ọjọ́ ọ̀la láti gba orílẹ̀ èdè rẹ̀ lọ́wọ́ Danish.
english: This fleet was led by Olaf Trygvasson, a Norwegian with ambitions to reclaim his country from Danish domination.

yoruba: Lẹ́yìn ìfàṣẹ́yìn àwọn ológun, Etherlred fohùnsọ̀kan pẹ̀lú Olaf, ẹni tí ó padà sí Norway láti gbìyànjú fi gba ìjọba rẹ̀ padà pẹlù àṣeyọrí tí kò dán mọ́rán.
english: After initial military setbacks, Ethelred was able to agree to terms with Olaf, who returned to Norway to try to gain his kingdom with mixed success.

yoruba: Hangooli ni alifabeeti kan soso ti a moomo seda ti a si n lo lojumo. Won seda alifabeeti naa ni 1444 nigbati Oba Sejongi wa lori oye (1418 - 1450.
english: Hangeul is the only purposely invented alphabet in popular daily use. The alphabet was invented in 1444 during the reign of King Sejong (1418 – 1450).

yoruba: Ọba Sejong ni ọba kẹrin ti ìran Joseon ó sì jẹ́ ìkan lára àwọn tí wọ́n bọ̀wọ̀ fún jùlọ.
english: King Sejong was the fourth king of the Joseon Dynasty and is one of the most highly regarded.

yoruba: Láti ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ pẹ̀pẹ̀, ohun tí ó sọ alifábẹ́ẹ̀tì Hangeul ni Hunmin Jeongeum, ni èyí tí ó túnmọ̀ sí “ìpohùn àwọn ẹ̀nìyàn tí ó tọ̀nà jùlọ”.
english: He originally named the Hangeul alphabet Hunmin Jeongeum, which means "the correct sounds for the instruction of the people".

yoruba: Ọ̀pọ̀ ìmọ̀ ìjìnlẹ̀ nípa bí Sankrit se bẹ̀rẹ̀ ló wà. Ọkàn lára wọn ni ti asíkiri Aryan láti ìwọ̀ oòrùn lọ sí ilẹ̀ India tó mún èdè wọn wá pẹ̀lu wọn.
english: There are many theories to how Sanskrit came into existence. One of them is about an Aryan migration from the west into India who brought their language with them.

yoruba: Sanskrit jẹ́ èdè tó ti pẹ́ a sì lè fi wé èdè latin tí wọ́n ma sọ ní Europe.
english: Sanskrit is an ancient language and is comparable to the Latin language spoken in Europe.

yoruba: Iwe akọkọ ti a mọ ni agbaye ni wọn kọ pẹlu ede Sanskrit. Lẹyin akojọpọ Upanishads, Sanskrit sadede parẹ nitori eto igbelewon.
english: The earliest known book in the world was written in Sanskrit. After the compilation of Upanishads, Sanskrit just faded due to hierarchy.

yoruba: Ede lile ati aladun ni Sanskriti, o ti wulo gege bi orisun opo ede Indiya igbalode, gege bii ede Latini se je orisun awon ede Yuropi bii Faranse ati Speen.
english: Sanskrit is a very complex and rich language, which has served to be the source for many modern Indian languages, just like Latin is the source for European languages like French and Spanish.

yoruba: Bí ogun ilẹ̀ Faransé tí ṣe parí, Germany bẹ̀rẹ̀ si ní gbaradì láti gbógun ti erékùsù ilẹ̀ Britain.
english: With the battle for France over, Germany began to get ready to invade the island of Britain.

yoruba: Jẹmaní pẹ ìnajíjẹ́ àwọn ìkolù náà ní “Isẹ́ Sealion”. Ọmọ ogun IÌú Gẹ̀ẹ́si ti pàdánù ọ̀pọ̀ àwọn ohun ìjà àti ìpẹ̀sẹ̀ ikan ogun rẹ nígbàtí o kójádẹo kúrò ní Dunkirk, fún ìdí èyí, ikọ̀ ọmọ ogun náà ti di alàílágbára.
english: Germany code-named the attack “Operation Sealion”. Most of the British Army's heavy weapons and supplies had been lost when it evacuated from Dunkirk, so the army was fairly weak.

yoruba: Ṣùgbọ́n ikọ̀ ológun orí omi Royal ṣì ní agbára ju ikọ̀ ológun orí omi ti Germany lọ (“Kriegsmarine”) Wọ́n sì le borí ìgbógun kí ìgbógun tí ẹkùn Gẹ̀ẹ́sì ìbá gbé wá.
english: But the Royal Navy was still much stronger than the German Navy (“Kriegsmarine”) and could have destroyed any invasion fleet sent across the English Channel.

yoruba: Amọ́ṣá, ìwọ̀nba àwọn ọkọ̀ ojú omi ọlọ́ba ló wà lẹ́gbẹ̀ẹ́ ọ̀nà àkọlù níbí ẹ̀rù se ń ba àwọn ọ̀gágun pé àkọlù ojú òfúrufú ilẹ̀ Germany yíó bo àwọn mọ́lẹ.
english: However, very few Royal Navy ships were based near the likely invasion routes as the admirals were afraid they would be sunk by German air attack.

yoruba: Ẹ jẹ́ kí á bẹ̀rẹ̀ pẹ̀lú àlàyé ìlànà ilẹ̀ Italy. orílẹ̀èdè Italy ló jẹ́ “àbúrò ọkùnrin” fún ilẹ̀ Germany àti Japan.
english: Let's start with an explanation about Italy's plans. Italy was mainly the "little brother" of Germany and Japan.

yoruba: Lóòọ́tọ́ wọ́n sẹ̀sẹ̀ kọ́ àọn ọkọ̀ ojú omi tuntun mẹ́rin sáájú kí ogun tó bẹ̀rẹ̀, ó ní ikọ̀ ológun tí kò lágbára àti àwọn ikọ̀ ológun ojú omi tí kò ra jaja.
english: It had a weaker army and a weaker navy, although they had just built four new ships right before the beginning of the war.

yoruba: Gbogbo aleba Itili je awon ilu Afirika. Lati ka awon ilu naa mo, won maa nilati ni ibi ti awon ajagun ti le gba lo ja, ki eon lr gba oju Osa Meditereniani lati lo kogun ja Afirika.
english: Italy's main goals were African countries. To capture those countries, they would need to have a troop launching pad, so as troops could sail across the Mediterranean Sea and invade Africa.

yoruba: Torí náà, wọ́n ni láti pa ibùdó àt ọkọ̀ ojú omi tí ilẹ̀ Gẹ̀ẹ́sì tó wà ní́ Egypt run . Yàtọ̀ sí àwọn ìgbésẹ̀ wọ̀nyí, kò yẹ́ kí ìjagun ojú omi ilẹ̀ Italy seohunkóhun.
english: For that, they had to get rid of British bases and ships in Egypt. Besides those actions, Italy's battleships were not supposed to do anything else.

yoruba: Nisin fun Japani, orileede irekusu ni Japani je, gege bii Briteeni.
english: Now for Japan. Japan was an island country, just like Britain.

yoruba: ọkọ̀ abẹ́ omi ni àwọn ọkọ̀ ojú omí tí wọn ṣe láti máa rìn ní abẹ́ omi, kí wọ́n sì tún wà ní abẹ́ omí fún ìgbà púpọ̀.
english: Submarines are ships designed to travel underwater, and remain there for an extended amount of time.

yoruba: Wọ́n lo àwọn ọkọ̀ ìjagun ojú omi nínú ogun àgbáyé àkọ́kọ́ àti ogun àgbáyé kejì. Nígbà náà, wọ́n má ń lọ́ra gan won kò sì le yìnbọn níbi tí ó jìnnà.
english: Submarines were used in World War I and World War II. Back then they were very slow and had a very limited shooting range.

yoruba: Níbẹ̀rẹ̀ ogun wọn sábà rín àjò lọ sí òkè òkun ṣùgbọ́n bí wọn ṣe n ṣẹ̀dá rédà tí ó pé si àwọn ọkọ̀ abẹ́ omi níláti jìn sínú omí kí wọ́n ma baà rí wọn.
english: In the beginning of the war they mostly travelled on top of the sea, but as radar began developing and becoming more accurate the submarines were forced to go under water to avoid being seen.

yoruba: Àwọn ọkọ̀ ìjagun ojú omi ilẹ̀ Germany ni wọ́n ń pè ní U-Boats. Ilẹ̀ Germany dára púpọ̀ nínú à ń tukọ̀ àwọn ọkọ̀ ìjagun ojú omi.
english: German submarines were called U-Boats. The Germans were very good at navigating and operating their submarines.

yoruba: Nítorí àṣeyọrí wọ́n pẹ̀lú àwọn ọkọ̀ inú omi kékèké, lẹ́yìn tí a kò gbọ́kànlé àwọn ọmọ ogun Germany láti ní púpọ̀ wọn.
english: Because of their success with submarines, after the war Germans aren't trusted to have many of them.

yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ni! Ọba Tutankhamun, ti wọ́n fi ìgbà kan pè ní “King Tut” tàbí “ọba àwọn ọkùnrin”, jẹ́ ọ̀kan lára ọba Egypt àtijọ́ tí ó gbajúmọ̀ ní ayé òdi òní.
english: Yes! King Tutankhamun, sometimes referred to as "King Tut" or "The Boy King", is one of the most well known ancient Egyptian kings in modern times.

yoruba: Bi ìyanilénu, wọn kò ka kún èyàn Pàtàkì ní àyẹ àtijọ́ wọn o dẹ kọ orúkọ rẹ sílè ni púpọ̀ nínú àwọn àkọsílè ọba àtijọ́.
english: Interestingly, he was not considered to be very important in ancient times and was not recorded on most ancient king lists.

yoruba: Àmọ́ṣá, síse àwárí ibojì rẹ̀ ní 1922 sọ ọ́ di olóòkìkí. Niigbàtí wọ́n ja ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ibojì látẹ̀yìnwá lólè, ibojì yìí nìkan ní wọ́n fi sílẹ̀ láì fọwọ́kàn.
english: However, the discovery of his tomb in 1922 made him a celebrity. While many tombs of the past were robbed, this tomb was left virtually undisturbed.

yoruba: Wọ́n ti dá ọ̀pọ̀ àwọn ohun èlò tí wọ́n sin pẹ̀lú Tutankhamun sí, lára è ní ẹgbẹ̀lẹ́gbẹ̀ iṣẹ́ ọnà tí wọ́n se pẹ̀lú irin àti òkúta tó ṣọ̀wọ́n.
english: Most of the items buried with Tutankhamun have been well preserved, including thousands of artefacts made from precious metals and rare stones.

yoruba: Sísẹ àwárí kèké sọ kèké ogun Assiria di fífúyé, oníjàfáfá, àti dáradára láti sárẹ́ kọja jagunjagun àti kèké ogun míràn.
english: The invention of spoke wheels made Assyrian chariots lighter, faster, and better prepared to outrun soldiers and other chariots.

yoruba: ọ̀kọ̀ láti ọrun oró wọn lè wọlé sí ìhámọ́ra ọ̀tá wọn. bíi 1000 B.C, ni àwọn Assyria gbé cavalry àkọ́kọ́ jáde.
english: Arrows from their deadly crossbows could penetrate the armor of rival soldiers. About 1000 B.C., the Assyrians introduced the first cavalry.

yoruba: Calvary ni a ń pe àwọn ikọ̀́ ọmọ ogun to máa n jà lórí ẹṣin. Ìjòkó ẹ̀yìn ẹṣin kòì tí ì sí ní ìgbà náà, fún ìdí èyí, ẹ̀yìn ẹṣin lásan ni awọn Assyrian máa ń jòkó sí láti jà.
english: A cavalry is an army that fights on horseback. The saddle had not yet been invented, so the Assyrian cavalry fought on the bare backs of their horses.

yoruba: A mọ ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ olóṣèlú, onímọ̀ sayẹnsì, àti òṣèré ilẹ̀ Greek. Ó ṣe é ṣe kí ẹni tí a mọ̀ jùlọ nínú àṣà yìí jẹ́ Homer, ògbọǹtarìgì afọ́jú akéwì, ẹni tí ó ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ ewì méjì kan tí o yááyì tí ó sì gbajúgbajà púpọ̀ nínú lítíréṣọ̀ ilẹ̀ Greek: ewì Iliad àti Odyssey.
english: We know many Greek politicians, scientists, and artists. Possibly the most known person of this culture is Homer, the legendary blind poet, who composed two masterpieces of Greek literature: the poems Iliad and Odyssey.

yoruba: Sopocies àti Aristophanes ṣì jẹ́ gbajúgbajà olùkọ̀wé eré àti pé àwọn eré wọn jẹ́ ìkan lára iṣẹ́ tó tóbi jù lọ ní lítírésọ̀.
english: Sophocles and Aristophanes are still popular playwrights and their plays are considered to be among the greatest works of world literature.

yoruba: Ìlú mọ̀ká ará Gíríkì mìíràn ni Onímọ̀-Ìṣirò Pythagoras, tí ọ̀pọ̀ ènìyàn mọ̀ fún tíọ́rì rẹ̀ tí o dálórí ìbáṣepọ̀ ẹ̀gbẹ̀gbẹ́ àwọn igun ọ̀tún.
english: Another famous Greek is a mathematician Pythagoras, mostly known for his famous theorem about relations of the sides of right triangles.

yoruba: Ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ìsirò iye àwọn èèyàn tó ń sọ èdè Hindi ló wà. Wọ́n sí i wípé ó wà láàrin èdè méjì sí mẹ́rin tí wọ́n ń sọ jùlọ lágbàláaye.
english: There are varying estimates for how many people speak Hindi. It is estimated to be between the second and fourth most commonly spoken language in the world.

yoruba: Oyè àwọn tó gbọ́ èdè ìbílẹ̀ mà n yàtọ̀ ní torí bóyá àwọn a ma n ka àwọn ẹ̀ya èdè to súnmọ́ ara wọ.
english: The number of native speakers varies depending on whether or not very closely related dialects are counted.

yoruba: Ìṣirò àwọn tó ma sọ̀rọ̀ jẹ́ láti mílíọ́nù 340 sí mílíọ́nù 500, àti jù bẹ́ẹ̀ lọ bí mílíọ́nù 800 àwọn ènìyàn lè mọ èdè náà sọ.
english: Estimates range from 340 million to 500 million speakers, and as many as 800 million people can understand the language.

yoruba: Èdè ìperí Hindi àti Urdu jọ ara wọn, ṣùgbọn ìyàtọ̀ wà nínú kíkọ wọn sílẹ̀;Nínú ìbánisọ̀rọ̀ ojoojúmọ́, àwọn olùsọ̀ èdè méjèèjì lè gbọ́ ara wọn yé yéké.
english: Hindi and Urdu are similar in vocabulary but different in script; in everyday conversations, speakers of both languages can usually understand each other.

yoruba: Ní 15th Century, apá Àríwá Estonia ní ipa àṣà ìlú Germany.
english: Around the 15th century, northern Estonia was under great cultural influence of Germany.

yoruba: Awọn ará ilẹ̀ German mú Ọlọ́rún wá súnmọ́ àwọn ẹnìyàn ìbilẹ̀, torí náà wọ́n ṣe àgbékalẹ̀ èdè Estonia.
english: Some German monks wanted to bring God closer to the native people, so they invented the Estonian literal language.

yoruba: Nitori alfabeeti Jamani ati okan ni won se fi “Õ/õ” kun.
english: It was based on the German alphabet and one character "Õ/õ" was added.

yoruba: Níbí ọjọ́ se ń gorí ọjọ́, ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ọ̀rọ̀ tí wọ́n yá láti Germany dirapọ̀ di ọ̀kan. Èyí ni ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ ìlàlọ́yẹ̀.
english: As time passed, many words that were borrowed from German coalesced. This was the beginning of enlightenment.

yoruba: Ní ìlànà àṣà, ajogún ìtẹ yíò dara pọ̀ mọ àwọn ológun lẹ́yìn tó bá keẹ́kọ̀ọ́ tán.
english: Traditionally, the heir to the throne would go straight into the military after finishing school.

yoruba: Àmọ́ṣá, Charles lọ silé ẹ̀kọ́ gíga fásitì ní Trinity College, Cambridge, níbití ó ti kọ́ nípa ẹ̀dá àti àwùjọ àti ìtàn ẹ̀dà, lẹ́yìn èyí ló tún kọ nípa ìtàn, ó pari pẹ̀lu 2:2 (ipò kejì kékeré).
english: However, Charles went to university at Trinity College, Cambridge where he studied Anthropology and Archaeology, and later History, earning a 2:2 (a lower second class degree).

yoruba: Charles ní Ẹbí Ọba ìlú Òyìnbó àkọ́kọ́ tí yóò gba ìdálọ́lá ìwé èrí dègírì.
english: Charles was the first member of the British Royal Family to be awarded a degree.

yoruba: Awon alawofunfun ni orile ede Turkey (iwo oorun Thrace tabi Rumelia ni ekun Balkan peninsula) kun 3% awon eeyan orile ede naa.
english: European Turkey (eastern Thrace or Rumelia in the Balkan peninsula) includes 3% of the country.

yoruba: Agbègbè ilẹ̀ Turkey gùn ju kìlómítà ẹgbẹ̀jọ (ẹgbẹ̀rún kan mi) àti ìwọ̀n fífẹ̀ ẹgbẹ̀jọ kìlómítà (ẹ̀ẹ́dẹ̀gbẹ̀ta mi), pẹ̀lú igun mẹ́rin.
english: The territory of Turkey is more than 1,600 kilometres (1,000 mi) long and 800 km (500 mi) wide, with a roughly rectangular shape.

yoruba: Agbègbè Turkey pẹ̀lú adágún gba bí i ààyè ọ̀rìn lé lẹ́ẹ̀dẹ́gbẹ̀rin ó lé mẹ́ta ẹgbẹ̀rún onígun mẹ́rin kìlómítà (300,948 sq mi),nínú èyí tí ọ̀tà lé lẹ́ẹ̀dẹ́gbẹ̀rin ó dín márùn ún ẹgbẹ̀rún ó lé ọ̀rìn lé lẹ́gbẹ̀ta ó lé mẹ́jọ onígun mẹ́rin kìlómítà (291,773 sq mi) ló wà ní ẹkùn ìwọ̀ oòrùn gúúsù Asia àti ẹgbẹ̀rún mẹ́tàlélógún, ọ̀tà lé lẹ́ẹ̀dẹ́gbẹ̀rin ó lé mẹ́rin onígun mẹ́rin kìlómítà (9,174 sq mi) in Europe.
english: Turkey's area, including lakes, occupies 783,562 square kilometres (300,948 sq mi), of which 755,688 square kilometres (291,773 sq mi) are in south west Asia and 23,764 square kilometres (9,174 sq mi) in Europe.

yoruba: Agbẹ̀gbẹ̀ Turkey jẹ́ orílé-èdè ẹ̀kẹtàdínlógójí ní tìtòbi, o jẹ iyẹ ìwọ̀n olú ílu Faransé àti ílu ọba lápapò.
english: Turkey's area makes it the world's 37th-largest country, and is about the size of Metropolitan France and the United Kingdom combined.

yoruba: Orile ede Turkey je eyiti okun nla meta yi ka: okun Aegean ni apa iwo oorun, Okun Dudu ni ariwa ati Okun Medirettanean ni apa ila oorun.
english: Turkey is encircled by seas on three sides: the Aegean Sea to the west, the Black Sea to the north and the Mediterranean Sea to the south.

yoruba: Luxembourg ní ìtàn tó gùn ṣùgbọ́n déètì òmìnira rẹ láti 1839..
english: Luxembourg has a long history but its independence dates from 1839.

yoruba: Ibi tí a mọ̀ ní Belgium jẹ́ ara apá Luxembourg látẹ̀yìnwá, sùgbọ́n ó di Belgian lẹ́yìn ogun ìgbàjọba Belgian ní ọdún 183.
english: Present-day parts of Belgium were part of Luxembourg in the past but became Belgian after the 1830s Belgian Revolution.

yoruba: Lusemboogi ti gbiyanju lati je orileede ti kii da si oro sugbon ni Ogun Agbaye Ikini Ati Ikeji, Jamani fi agbara gba won.
english: Luxembourg has always tried to remain a neutral country but it was occupied in both World War I and World War II by Germany.

yoruba: Ni 1957 Lusembogi di omoegbe ipile igbimo ti a n pe ni Apapo Yuropi.
english: In 1957 Luxembourg became a founding member of the organization which is today known as the European Union.

yoruba: Drukgyal Dzong jẹ́ odi tí ó ti bàjẹ́ àti iléègbé àwọn mọ́ǹkì Buddhist ní òkè agbègbè Para (ní abúlé Phondey.
english: The Drukgyal Dzong is a ruined fortress and Buddhist monastery in the upper part of the Paro District (in Phondey Village).

yoruba: Wọ́n sọ ọ́ di mímọ̀ pé lọ́dun 1649, Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyel sèdásílẹ̀ odi láti fi sèrántí ìṣẹ́gun rẹ̀ tako ikọ̀ ológun Tibetan-Mongol.
english: It is said that in 1649, Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyel created the fortress to commemorate his victory against the Tibetan-Mongol forces.

yoruba: Ní ọdún 1951, ìjàm̀bá iná sàkọlù sáwọn ohun atọ́kasí ìtàn tó sẹ́kù ní Drukgyal Dzong, bí i àwòrán Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal.
english: In 1951, a fire caused for only some of the relics of the Drukgyal Dzong to remain, such as the image of Zhabdrung Ngawang Namgyal.

yoruba: Lẹ́yìn ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ iná, wọ́n dá ààbò bo àgọ́ náà, bẹ́ẹ̀ni wọ́n pa á mọ́, èyí tó mún un jẹ́ ọ̀kan gbòógì ohun afanimọ́ra ní ìlú Bhutan.
english: After the fire, the fortress was preserved and protected, remaining to be one of Bhutan's most sensational attractions.

yoruba: Ní sẹ́ńtúrì kejìdínlógún, wọ́n rí Cambodia níbi tí wọ́n fún un sí láàrin àwọn agbègbè méjì, Thailand àti Vietnam.
english: During the 18th century Cambodia found itself squeezed between two powerful neighbors, Thailand and Vietnam.

yoruba: Àwọn ara Thai wolé sí Cambodia lára ní ọ̀pọ̀ ìgbà ni sẹńtúrì kejìdínlógún àti ni ọdún 1772, wọ́n siba Phnom Phen jẹ́.
english: The Thais invaded Cambodia several times in the 18th century and in 1772 they destroyed Phnom Phen.

yoruba: Ní àwọn ọdun aipẹ julọ century 18 náà àwọn eniyan ti Vietnam gbógun ti Cambodia.
english: In the last years of the 18th century the Vietnamese also invaded Cambodia.

yoruba: Ìdá méjìdílógún àwọn èèyàn ilẹ̀ Venezuela ni kò ní iṣẹ́, ọ̀pọ̀ àwọn tó sì níṣẹ́ ló ń siṣẹ́ ní ẹka ọrọ̀ ajé tí kìí se fọ́mà.
english: Eighteen percent of Venezuelans are unemployed, and most of those who are employed work in the informal economy.

yoruba: Ìdá mẹ́ta àwọn ènìyàn ilẹ̀ Venezuela tó ń siṣẹ́ ló wà lẹ́ka tó ń pèsè iṣẹ́, ìdá mẹ́ta wà ní ilé- iṣẹ́ nígbàtí ìkọrùn ún wà lẹ́ka tó ń rí sí ohun ọ̀gbìn.
english: Two thirds of Venezuelans who work do so in the service sector, nearly a quarter work in industry and a fifth work in agriculture.

yoruba: Ilé iṣẹ́ to ṣe pàtàkì ní ilé iṣẹ́ epo Venezuelans jẹ́, bí ó tilẹ̀ jẹ́ pé ìdá kan nínú àwọn ọmọ orílẹ̀ èdè ló ń ṣiṣẹ níbẹ̀.
english: An important industry for Venezuelans is oil, where the country is a net exporter, even though only one percent work in the oil industry.

yoruba: Nínú ìwé ìròyìn olómìnira àpapọ̀, ọgbà ewéko to wà ní Singapore ní àwọn akọ́ṣẹ́mọṣẹ ti ṣe ìrànlọ́wọ́ láti sọ erékùsù di ìlú tígi pọ̀ sí jù.
english: Early in the nation's independence, Singapore Botanic Gardens' expertise helped to transform the island into a tropical Garden City.

yoruba: Ní ọdún 1981,Vanda Miss Joaquim, wọ́n mún èso orchid gẹ́gẹ́ bí i òdòdó àpapọ̀.
english: In 1981, Vanda Miss Joaquim, an orchid hybrid, was chosen as the nation's national flower.

yoruba: Ní nǹkan bí i osù kẹwàá ọdọọdún ni àwọn ẹranko tó ń jẹ ewé tíye wọ́n tó mílíọ̀nù kan ó lé ẹ̀ẹ́dẹ́gbẹ̀ta ma ń rìnrìn àjò lọ sí ẹkùn gúúsù, fo odò Mara láti àwọn òkè àríwá fún òjò.
english: Every year around October nearly 1.5 million herbivores travel towards the southern plains, crossing the Mara River, from the northern hills for the rains.

yoruba: Nìgbàtí wọ́n padà sí àríwá nípasẹ̀ ìwọ oòrùn, lẹ́ẹ̀kan si nígbàtí wọ́n gba odò Mara kọjá, lẹ́yìn àkókò òjò ní oṣù kẹrin.
english: And then back to the north through the west, once again crossing the Mara river, after the rains in around April.

yoruba: Agbon ti Serengeti se akopo Serengeti National Park, Ngoromgoro Conservative Area ati Maswa Game Reserve ni orile’de Tanzania ati Maasai Mara NAtional Reserve ti Kenya.
english: The Serengeti region contains the Serengeti National Park, the Ngorongoro Conservation Area and Maswa Game Reserve in Tanzania and the Maasai Mara National Reserve in Kenya.

yoruba: Kíkọ́ láti ṣẹ̀da ìgbóhùnsáfẹ́fẹ́ alájọsọ nílò́ àwọn ohun ìbára-ẹni-sọ̀rọ̀ àti àwọn nǹkan abínibí, àti pẹ̀lú láti ni di ògbóntarìgì nihnú ìdánilẹ́kọ̀ọ́ onílànà ìbara-ẹni sọ̀rọ̀ (sísọ̀tàn-pẹ̀lú-pátákò, ṣíṣe àtúntò àwọn ohùn àti ffọ́nrán amóhùnmáwòrán, sísọ ìtàn, abbl.)
english: Learning to create interactive media requires conventional and traditional skills, as well as tools mastered in interactive classes (storyboarding, audio and video editing, story telling, etc.)

yoruba: Àgbékalẹ̀ Ibara-ẹni-ṣepọ̀ nílò pé kí a tún èrò wa yẹ̀wò nípa ìgbéjáde ayélujára, kí a sì kọ́ láti ronú ní ọ̀nà ọ̀tọ.
english: Interactive design requires that you re-assess your assumptions about media production and learn to think in a non-linear ways.

yoruba: Àgbékalẹ̀ ìbáraẹnisọ̀rọ̀ pè fú kí àwọn ẹka àkànṣe iṣẹ́ lọ́pọ̀ mọ́ra wọn sùgbọ́n ó tún mọ́pọlọ lọ́wọ́ bí wọ́n bá wà lẹ́yọkọ̀ọ̀kan.
english: Interactive design requires that components of a project connect to each other, but also make sense as a separate entity.

yoruba: Nkan tí ò dáa nipa lensì soomì nipé agbára rẹ láti dájúle nkan àti oye lensì tó nilò láti rí ìdójúké oyé púpọ̀ o ti pọ̀jù tí lensì primu.
english: The disadvantage of zoom lenses is that the focal complexity and number of lens elements required to achieve a range of focal lengths is much greater than for prime lenses.

yoruba: Kii se isoro pupo mo nitori pe awon to n se lensi ti n se aseyori ni iselopo lensi.
english: This is becoming less of an issue as lens manufacturers achieve higher standards in lens production.

yoruba: Èyí jẹ́ kí lẹ́nsì tó lè ṣàmùgbòrò láti gbé àwọn ojúlówó àwòrán jáde k;i a fi wé lẹ́nsì tó jé pé kò lè ṣe àmúgbòrò àwòrán.
english: This has allowed zoom lenses to produce pictures of a quality comparable to that achieved by lenses with fixed focal length.

yoruba: Àléébù mìíràn tí lẹ́ǹsì àwosúnsún nipé bi ihò bà ṣe tóbi sí (bí o ṣe yára sí ) bẹẹni lẹ́ǹsì lọ́pọ̀ ìgbà yíò sì kéré sí .
english: Another disadvantage of zoom lenses is that the maximum aperture (the speed) of the lens is usually lower.

yoruba: Èyí mún kí àwọn àwo amúnìkangbòòrò nira láti lò níbi tí òòye kò ti pọ̀ láìsí ìtànsán.
english: This makes inexpensive zoom lenses hard to use in low-light conditions without a flash.

yoruba: Òkan lára àwọn ìṣòro tó wọ́pọ̀ nígbà tí ènìyàn ń gbìyànjú láti yí sinima padà sí èrò DVD ni ìṣújù .
english: One of the most common problems when trying to convert a movie to DVD format is the overscan.

yoruba: Ọ̀pọ̀ ẹ̀rọ amóhùnmáwòrán ni wọ́n ṣẹ̀dá láti tẹ́ àwọn ará ìlú lọ́rùn.
english: Most televisions are made in a way to please the general public.

yoruba: Fun idi eyi, gbogbo nkan ti o ba n ri lori amohunmaworan ni won ge ala okoe, isale ati egbegbe re kuro.
english: For that reason, everything you see on the TV had the borders cut, top, bottom and sides.

yoruba: Èyí ni láti ri wípé àwòrán náà bo gbogbo ìbòjú. Òhun ni wọ́n ń pè ní ìṣíjú.
english: This is made to ensure that the image covers the whole screen. That is called overscan.

yoruba: Ó ṣeni láàánú pé tí o bá ṣe DVD, eteti rẹ náà ṣe é ṣe kí ó gé kúrò pẹ̀lú, tí fídíò náà bá ní àdàkọ sií èdè mìíràn tí o sì súnmọ́ abẹ́, wọn ò ní hàn dáadáa.
english: Unfortunately, when you make a DVD, it's borders will most likely be cut too, and if the video had subtitles too close to the bottom, they won't be fully shown.

yoruba: Ilé ńlá oníṣe àtijọ́ ti sàgbélárugẹ ìwòye ojú inú, sàkójọ̀ àwòrán akọni, ayẹyẹ, àti àwọn olóyè Arthur.
english: The traditional medieval castle has long inspired the imagination, conjuring up images of jousts, banquets and Arthurian chivalry.

yoruba: Kódá dídúró láàrin ìparun ẹgbẹ̀rún ọdún, ó rọrùn láti fi ìró àti òórùn ogun ọjọ́ pípẹ̀ sọ́kàn, débí gbígbọ́ gbùgbùgbù ẹsẹ ẹranko lórí òkúta àti gbígbọ́ òórùn ẹ̀rù tó jáde látinú ọ̀gbun.
english: Even standing amidst thousand year-old ruins it is easy to bring to mind the sounds and smells of battles long gone, to almost hear the clatter of hooves on the cobbles and to smell the fear rising from the dungeon pits.

yoruba: Ṣùgbọ́n ṣé ìwoṣàkun wa dá lórí ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ ojú ayé bi? Kí ló dé tí wọ́n ń kọ ilé ìṣọ́ gan an? Báwo ni wọ́n ṣe yà wọ́n, tí wọ́ sì kọ́ wọn?
english: But is our imagination based on reality? Why were castles built in the first place? How were they designed and built?

yoruba: Bí àsìkò yìí ṣe rí nìyẹn, Kirby Muxloe Castle jọ ilé tí wọ́n tò dáadáa ju ilé ìṣọ́ l.
english: Typical for the period, Kirby Muxloe Castle is more of a fortified house than a true castle.

yoruba: Ferese nla re ti won kun ati ogiri re to tirin on ni le duro pe ti won ba diidi doju ija ko.
english: Its large glazed windows and thin walls would not have been able to resist a determined attack for long.

yoruba: Ní àwọn ọdún 1480, nígbàtí kíkọ́ rẹ bẹ̀rẹ̀ láti ọwọ́ Lord Hastings, orílè-èdè náà ní àlàáfíà díè àti pe ìwúlò wà fún ìdábòbò lòdì sí àwọn ikọ́ ogun alárìnká kékèké.
english: In the 1480s, when its construction was begun by Lord Hastings, the country was relatively peaceful and defense was only required against small bands of roving marauders.

yoruba: Ìsedédé agbára ni ètò tí ilẹ̀ Europu ń lò láti fi se ìsàkóso ìjẹgàba àpapọ̀ àwọn orílẹ̀ Europu lápapọ.
english: The balance of power was a system in which European nations sought to maintain the national sovereignty of all European states.

yoruba: Èróngbà ni pé gbogbo orílẹ̀ èdè Europe níláti wà láti lè dènà orílẹ̀ èdè kan láti má ní agbára, àti pé ìjọba ma ń yí ọ̀rẹ́ wọn kí wọ́n bá lè ní ìwọ̀ntúnwọ̀nsí.
english: The concept was that all European nations had to seek to prevent one nation from becoming powerful, and thus national governments often changed their alliances in order to maintain the balance.

yoruba: Ogun Igbapò ilẹ̀ Spain ni ó jẹ́ alákọ̀ọ́kọ́ tí yóò dá lórí dídọ́gba agbára.
english: The War of Spanish Succession marked the first war whose central issue was the balance of power.

yoruba: Eleyi jẹ apẹẹrẹ ti o ṣe pataki, bi awọn alagbara alawọ funfun ko ṣe ni ma lugọ sẹyin ogun ẹsin mọ. Bẹẹni, Ogun Ọlọgbọn Ọdun naa ni yio jẹ eyi ti o gbẹyin ti a o pẹ ni ogun ẹsin.
english: This marked an important change, as European powers would no longer have the pretext of being religious wars. Thus, the Thirty Years' War would be the last war to be labeled a religious war.

yoruba: Tẹ́ḿpìlì Artemis ní Éfésù di pípa run ní Oṣù Keje, ọdún 356 BCE ìjàmbá iná tí Herostratus dá silesílè.
english: The temple of Artemis at Ephesus was destroyed on July 21, 356 BCE in an act of arson committed by Herostratus.

yoruba: Bi itan naa se wi, nkan to fi se iwuri nip e ko je gbajumo ni onakona. Awon ara Efesu, won ti binu, won kede pe ki won ma ko oruko Erostratusi lai lai.
