english: According to the story, his motivation was fame at any cost. The Ephesians, outraged, announced that Herostratus' name never be recorded.

yoruba: Asọ̀tàn Greek padà wá kọ orúkọ náà, èyí tí a ma lọ́nìí. Wọ́n wó ilé àdúrà náà ní alé náà tí wọ́n bí Alexander the great.
english: The Greek historian Strabo later noted the name, which is how we know today. The temple was destroyed on the same night that Alexander the Great was born.

yoruba: Alexander, gẹ́gẹ bi ọba pinu láti sanwó fún àtúnkọ́ tẹ́ḿpìlì náà sùgbọ́n a kò gba ìpinu rẹ láàyè. Lẹ́yìna, ikú Alexander, tẹ́ḿpìlì náà di àtúnkọ ní 323 BCE.
english: Alexander, as king, offered to pay to rebuild the temple, but his offer was denied. Later, after Alexander died, the temple was rebuilt in 323 BCE.

yoruba: Rí i dájú pé ọwọ́ rẹ ní ìsinmi bó se yẹ nígbàtí o bá ń kọlu gbogbo àkíyèsí tí ó tó - pẹ̀lú pé ki o má dààmú ọmọ ìka ọwọ́ rẹ jùlọ.
english: Make sure your hand is as relaxed as possible while still hitting all the notes correctly - also try not to make much extraneous motion with your fingers.

yoruba: Bó ba n ṣe báyí kò ní rẹ̀ ẹ́ jù. Rántí pé o kò níláti fi agbára tẹ ojú rẹ̀ kí ohun rẹ̀ ga síi bí ti piano.
english: This way, you will tire yourself out as little as possible. Remember there's no need to hit the keys with a lot of force for extra volume like on the piano.

yoruba: Láti mú ohun akodioni tóbi si wàá lò àwọn belò pẹ̀lú agbára tàbí ere sí i.
english: On the accordion, to get extra volume, you use the bellows with more pressure or speed.

yoruba: Ìgbàgbọ́ Mysticism ní síse àwárí ajọsepọ̀ pẹ̀lú ìdánimọ̀ tàbí ìmọ̀lára ìdánilójú gbòógì, ìdẹ́ẹ̀dá, òtítọ́ ẹ̀mí tàbi Ọlọ́run.
english: Mysticism is the pursuit of communion with, identity with, or conscious awareness of an ultimate reality, divinity, spiritual truth, or God.

yoruba: Àwọn onígbàgbọ́ máa ń wa ìríri tààrà́, ìmọ̀́lára tàbí fi ojú inú wo ohun tótọ́ ti ọ̀run tàbí àwọn alálẹ̀ òrìsà.
english: The believer seeks a direct experience, intuition, or insight into divine reality/the deity or dieties.

yoruba: Àwọn agbátẹ̀lé ma n tẹ́lẹ̀ àwọn ìlànà àti ìgbésí ayé kan tó ma n jẹ́kí àwọn ìrírí yẹn rẹ̀ si.
english: Followers pursue certain ways of living, or practices that are intended to nurture those experiences.

yoruba: Misticisimi da yato laarin awon esin miin ati ona ijosin, toripe o tenumo iriri tara eni ni ipo imo ohun to dayato, paa paa julo awon to niwa suuru, ironu jinle, alayo abi idun.
english: Mysticism can be distinguished from other forms of religious belief and worship by its emphasis on the direct personal experience of a unique state of consciousness, particularly those of a peaceful, insightful, blissful, or even ecstatic character.

yoruba: Sikhism je esin awon ara iha India. O bere lati agbegbe Punjab ni senturi 15th laarin awon eya ti o pin ninu asa awon Hindu.
english: Sikhism is a religion from the Indian sub-continent. It originated in the Punjab region during the 15th century from a sectarian split within the Hindu tradition.

yoruba: Àwọn Síìkì gbàgbó pé ẹ̀sìn wọn ò sí lára Induisimi ṣùgbọ́n wọ́n gbà pé Síìkì wá láti àṣà àti ìṣe Indù.
english: Sikhs consider their faith to be a separate religion from Hinduism though they acknowledge its Hindu roots and traditions.

yoruba: Àwọn Sikhs ń pe ẹ̀sìn wọn ní Gurmat, èyí tí wọ́n pè ní Punjabi “nílànì àwọn guru”. Àwọn guru ní ẹ̀sìn àkọ́kọ́ gbogbo ẹ̀sìn Indian àmọ́ Sikhism ti di pàtàkì èyí tí ó jẹ́ pàtàkì ìgbàgbọ́ Sikh.
english: Sikhs call their religion Gurmat, which is Punjabi for "way of the guru". The guru is a fundamental aspect of all Indian religions but in Sikhism has taken on an importance that forms the core of Sikh beliefs.

yoruba: Wọ́n sèdásílẹ̀ ẹ̀sìn náà ní sẹ́ńtu (1469–1539).
english: The religion was founded in the 15th century by Guru Nanak (1469–1539). There followed in succession a further nine gurus.

yoruba: Àmọ́ ní Okudu 1956, a dán àwọn ìlérí Kruṣẹ́fì wò nígbà rogbodiyan ní Polandi m, tí àwọn oṣiṣẹ́ ń takò àìsí oúnjẹ́, ìdínkù owó iṣẹ́, tó dí rogbodiyan gbogbogbò láti takò Komunisimi.
english: However, in June 1956, Krushchev's promises were put to the test when riots in Poland, where workers were protesting against food shortages and wage cuts, turned into a general protest against Communism.

yoruba: O kàn jẹ́ pé ni òpin, Krushchev fi àgbá ráṣẹ́ fún àwọn tí wọ́n ń béèrè ọjà, ó fi ààyè sílẹ̀ fún àwọn ohun th iètò ọrọ̀ ajé fẹ́ ó sì gbà láti fi gbajúgbajà náà Wladyslaw Gomulka jẹ Ẹmẹ̀wà tuntu.
english: Although in the end, Krushchev sent in tanks to restore order, he did give way to some economic demands and agreed to appoint the popular Wladyslaw Gomulka as the new prime minister.

yoruba: Ní àárin gbùngùn Indus, Ọ̀làjú jẹ́ àkókò Bronze Age, ọlàjú ní àríwá-ìwọ̀oòrùn India fi ara pẹ́ ọ̀pọ̀ ti òde òní tí ó wà ní Pakistan àti àwọn agbègbè ní Aríwá-ìwọ̀oòrùn Afghanista.
english: The Indus Valley Civilization was a Bronze Age civilisation in the northwest Indian subcontinent encompassing most of modern-day Pakistan and some regions in northwest India and northeast Afghanistan.

yoruba: Olàjú náà gbilẹ̀ nínú Iyàwòomiòkun sínú ilé tí odò Indus níbití orúkọ rẹ tí gbẹjẹyọ .
english: The civilisation flourished in the basins of the Indus River wherefore it derives its name.

yoruba: Biotilejepe awon omowe kan daba pe nitori pe awon ara besini Odo Sarasfati to ti wa gbe laju, o ye ki won pe ni Olaju Indus-Sarasfati, awon kan maa n pe ni Olaju Harapani, akoko ninu awon ibi ti won wu jade ni 1920s.
english: Although some scholars speculate that since the civilisation also existed in the basins of the now dried up Sarasvati River, it should be aptly called the Indus-Sarasvati Civilization, while some call it the Harappan Civilization after Harappa, the first of its sites to be excavated in the 1920s.

yoruba: Ìjẹ́ ológun ilẹ̀ Roman sèrànwọ́ fún ìdàgbàsóke ètò ìsègùn òyìnbó.
english: The militaristic nature of the Roman empire aided in the development of medical advances.

yoruba: Ọba Augustus gbàwọn onímọ̀ ìsègùn òyìnbó, bakanáà lótún sàgbékalẹ̀ ikọ̀ onísègùn ilẹ̀ Róòmùn àkọ́kọ́ láti lò lẹ́yìn ogun.
english: Doctors began to be recruited by Emperor Augustus and even formed the first Roman Medical Corps for use in the aftermath of battles.

yoruba: Àwọn oníṣẹ́abẹ nímọ̀ lórí ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ òògun oorun nínú èyí tí mọfìnì tí wọ́n se látara àyọkúrò kóró pọ́pì àti scopolamine latara àwọn kóró àgbo.
english: Surgeons had knowledge of various sedatives including morphine from extracts of poppy seeds and scopolamine from herbane seeds.

yoruba: Wọ́n di akọ́ṣẹ́mọṣẹ́ ògbóntarìgì nínú kí a gé ẹsẹ̀/apá láti dóòlà ẹ̀mí àwọn aláìsàn, bẹ́ẹ̀ náà ni wọn a tún máa ṣe àtọwọ́dá ọwọ/ẹsẹ̀ láti lè dá ẹ̀jẹ̀ dúró.
english: They became proficient at amputation to save patients from gangrene as well as tourniquets and arterial clamps to stem blood flow.

yoruba: Ni opo senturi, ijoba Roomu dari si ere nla ninu owo iwosan oun lo de papa di pupo ninu imo ti a ni loni.
english: Over multiple centuries, the Roman empire led to great gains in the field of medicine and formed much of the knowledge we know today.

yoruba: Títẹ ìwẹ́ ní ọ̀nà tọ dánsáká ni ìwẹ́ títẹ̀ oni gbẹ̀dẹ́kẹ pèlú sísẹ títè kan ṣoṣo lèèkàn náà, kò fàyàgbà àwọn ọ̀nà ìwẹ títẹ̀ tọ ṣòro bi aládáàpadàsẹ́yìn, áti wípé gbogbo títẹ̀ ni o sẹẹsẹ tààrà.
english: Pureland origami is origami with the restriction that only one fold may be done at a time, more complex folds like reverse folds are not allowed, and all folds have straightforward locations.

yoruba: John Smith ló dasílẹ̀ ní 1970 láti ran fódà tí kò nú ìrírí lọ́wọ́ tàbí àwọn tí kò mọ ọkọ̀ wà dáadáa.
english: It was developed by John Smith in the 1970s to help inexperienced folders or those with limited motor skills.

yoruba: Àwọn ọmọdé ma ń ní ìmọ̀lára orísun àti ìhùwàsí ti nípa ẹléyàmẹ̀yà yìí máa ń ní ìpalára fún ìhùwàsí.
english: Children develop an awareness of race and racial stereotypes quite young and these racial stereotypes affect behavior.

yoruba: Fun apẹẹrẹ, awọn ọmọ ti wọn damọ si ẹya to kere ti o si gbagbọ pe awọn kole se dada ni ile iwe,nitori naa ni wọn ki fi se dada ni ile iwe nitori igbagbọ wọn to so mọ eya wọn.
english: For instance, children who identify with a racial minority that is stereotyped as not doing well in school tend to not do well in school once they learn about the stereotype associated with their race.

yoruba: MySpace ni òpó weebu tó lókìkí kẹta jùlọ ni orílè-èdè Améríkà o si ni èrìnlélàádọ́ta millionu prófáílì lọ́wọ́lọ́wọ́.
english: MySpace is the third most popular website used in the United States and has 54 million profiles currently.

yoruba: Àwọn ojú òpó weebu wònyí ti ní àkíyèsí púpọ̀, ní pàtàkì ní agbọn ètò èkó.
english: These websites have gotten a lot of attention, especially in the education setting.

yoruba: Anfani wa lori awon saiti wonyi bii o seese lati da oju iwe weebu kilasi sile to le ni blogi, fidio ati awon aworan ati awon eroja miin.
english: There are positive aspects to these websites, which include, being able to easily setup a class page which can include blogs, videos, photos, and other features.

yoruba: O lè ráyèsí ojú ìwé yìí nípa pípèsè àdírẹ́sì wkẹ̀bù kan, èyí tó jẹ́ kó rọrùn láti rántí tó dẹ̀ rọrùn láti tẹ̀ fún àwọn akẹ́kọ̀ọ́ tó lé ní ìdàmú nínu lílo keyboard tàbí pẹ̀lú sípẹ̀lì.
english: This page can easily be accessed by providing just one web address, which makes it easy to remember and easy to type in for students who may have trouble using the keyboard or with spelling.

yoruba: Àkànse lè ba láti mún un kó rọrùn láti ka àti pẹ̀lú àwọ̀ díẹ̀ tàbí iye tí wọ́n bá fẹ́.
english: It can be customized to make it easy to read and also with as much or little color as desired.

yoruba: Attention Deficit Disorder, ni ààrùn ọpọlọ tí o máa ń fa kí ènìyàn máa ní àwọn àpẹẹrẹ bí wérewère, wọ́nranwọ̀nran, ránhunràun, àti wàìwàì tàbí gànràngànrà.”
english: Attention Deficit Disorder "is a neurological syndrome whose classic defining triad of symptoms including impulsivity, distractibility, and hyperactivity or excess energy".

yoruba: Kìn ṣe àìkè kọ́ ẹ̀kọ́, bí kò ṣe ṣìṣe ségeṣège ní ẹ̀kọ́ kíkọ́, ó ma ń nípa lórí 3-5 percent gbogbo àwọn ọmọdé, kódà mílíọ́nù méjì àwọn ọmọ kékèké ìlú Amékíkà.
english: It is not a learning disability, it is a learning disorder; it "affects 3 to 5 percent of all children, perhaps as many as 2 million American children".

yoruba: Awon omode to ni ADD, o ma n soro fun won lati fokan si nkan bii ise ile iawe sugboon wo le fokan si awon nkan to ba wu won bii ere sise abi wiwo ere katooni ti won feran julo abi kiko gbolohun lai lo awon ami gbolohun.
english: Children with ADD have a hard time focusing on things like school work, but they can concentrate on things they enjoy doing like playing games or watching their favorite cartoons or writing sentences without punctuation.

yoruba: Àwọn ọmọ wọ̀nyí le dojúkọ ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ìsòro nítorí wọn a máà “wu àwọn ìwà tó léwu, kópa nínú jàgídíjàkan, àti ìsọ̀dì sòfin” láti lè se ìwúrí fún ọpọlọ wọn, níwọn ìgbà tí ọpọlọ wọn kò lè sisẹ́ ìwúrí lónà déédéé.
english: These children tend to get into a lot of trouble, because they "engage in risky behaviors, get into fights, and challenge authority" in order to stimulate their brain, since their brain can not be stimulated by normal methods.

yoruba: ADD ma n ní ipá lóri ibasepo pẹ̀lú àwọn ëgbẹ́ min tori pe awon omode o le mo idi ti won fi n se bi won se ma n se tabi idi ti won fi n pe oro bi won se ma n pe tabi pe idagbasoke won yato.
english: ADD affects relationships with other peers because other children can not understand why they act the way that they do or why they spell they way they do or that their maturity level is different.

yoruba: Níbí àyípadà se bá agbára àti ní ìmọ̀ òhun ìkẹ́ẹ̀kọ́ lọ́nà tó ju àlàkalẹ̀ lọ tí wọ́n fi ń kọ́ nipa ìmọ̀ lọ.
english: As the ability to obtain knowledge and to learn changed in such a way as mentioned above the base rate at which knowledge was obtained changed.

yoruba: Bí a ṣe lè gba ìwífún yàtọ̀. Mi kò ṣe mọ́. Àfetígbọ́ wà nínu olúkúlùkù, ṣùgbọ́n agbára láti rántí jẹ́ ǹkan tó yẹ kí a mú ní ọ̀kúnkúndùn.
english: The approach to obtaining information was different. No longer did pressure reside within individual recall, but the ability to recall text became more of a focus.

yoruba: Ní àkótán, ọ̀làjú náà se àyípadà pàtàkì sí ilànà ẹ̀kọ́ àti ìtànkálẹ̀ ìmọ̀.
english: In essence, the Renaissance made a significant change in the approach to learning and the dissemination of knowledge.

yoruba: Yàtọ̀ sí àwọn primeeti òmíràn àwọn ominiidi o ki fọwọ́ rìn tàbí gbẹ́rù tàbí gun igi.
english: Unlike other primates, hominids no longer use their hands in locomotion or bearing weight or swinging through the trees.

yoruba: Ọwọ́ àti ẹsẹ̀ ìnànkí náà dọ́gba ní íwọ́n tìtòbi áti gígùn, o sàfihàn pe ọwọ́ náà ni wón fi n gbéra bí wọ́n se rìn lórí ọwọ́́.
english: The chimpanzee's hand and foot are similar in size and length, reflecting the hand's use for bearing weight in knuckle walking.

yoruba: Ọwọ́ ènìyàn kéré sí ẹsẹ̀ rẹ̀, pẹlù eegun ẹsẹ̀ tí ó tọ́.
english: The human hand is shorter than the foot, with straighter phalanges.

yoruba: Àwọn egungun ọwó ohun àlùmọ́ọ́nì fún mílíọ̀nù ọdún méjì safihàn àyípadà pàtàkì ọwó láti lílò ipò sí ìfowóyí .
english: Fossil hand bones two million to three million years old reveal this shift in specialization of the hand from locomotion to manipulation.

yoruba: Àwọn èyàn wà tó gbàgbọ́ pé àlákálà lọ́pọ̀jù ma n ma n jẹ́ kó rę èyàn.
english: Some people believe that experiencing many artificially induced lucid dreams often enough can be very exhausting.

yoruba: Ìdí gbòógì fú ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ yìí ni àbájáde àwọn ààlá tó hàn kedere fún nínawọ́jà gígùn àkókò láàrin àwọn ìpínlẹ̀ REM tó sẹ́kù.
english: The main reason for this phenomenon is the result of the lucid dreams expanding the length of time between REM states.

yoruba: Tí REMs bá dínkù ní alẹ́ kan, ipò yii tí oti ń rí orun sùn, àti dídánara rẹ kò ni dán mọ́ ọn ran mọ́ láti di ìṣòr.
english: With fewer REMs per night, this state in which you experience actual sleep and your body recovers becomes infrequent enough to become a problem.

yoruba: Eyi je ki o re ara bi eni wipe eniyan nilo lati ma ji iseju ogun si ogbon lera-lera lati wo ero mohunmaworan.
english: This is just as exhausting as if you were to wake up every twenty or thirty minutes and watch TV.

yoruba: Àbájádẹ yìí dálé lórí bí ọpọlọ rẹ se n sakitiyan láti la àlá kẹdẹrẹ ní alẹlẹ́.
english: The effect is dependent on how often your brain attempts to lucidly dream per night.

yoruba: Nnkan kò lọ dáadáa fún àwọn ará ilẹ̀ Italy ní ẹkùn àríwá ilẹ̀ adúláwọ̀ láti ìbẹ̀ẹ̀rẹ̀. Láàárin ọ̀ṣẹ̀ kan tí ilẹ̀ Italy síde ogun ni ọjọ kẹwa ọṣù kẹfà ọdún 1940, British kọkànlá Hussars ti fipá gba Fort Capuzzo ni Libya.
english: Things did not go well for the Italians in North Africa almost from the start. Within a week of Italy's declaration of war on June 10, 1940, the British 11th Hussars had seized Fort Capuzzo in Libya.

yoruba: Ní ìbániníbùba ní ìlà-òrùn Bardia, àwọn ara IÌú Gẹ̀ẹ́si mu olórí Amojú-Ẹ̀rọ Ìpín-Kẹ̀wá Italian, General Lastucci.
english: In an ambush east of Bardia, the British captured the Italian Tenth Army's Engineer-in-Chief, General Lastucci.

yoruba: Ní oṣù kẹfà ọjọ́ 28, Mashal Ìalo Balbo, Governor General ti ìlú Libya àti ajogún Mussolini, ní inà tí kò léwu pa nígbàtí ó ń basílẹ̀ ní Tobruk.
english: On June 28, Marshal Italo Balbo, the Governor-General of Libya and apparent heir to Mussolini, was killed by friendly fire while landing in Tobruk.

yoruba: Wọ́n lè ṣeré eré ìdárayá ìjà ẹlẹ́ni mẹ́ta ìgbàlódé ní ìpele méta, láti akẹ́kọ̀ọ́ tó wà ní ilé ẹ̀kọ́ àgbà sí àkọ́ṣkmọṣẹ́ àti ìdíje Olympics.
english: The modern sport of fencing is played at many levels, from students learning at a university to professional and Olympic competition.

yoruba: Meji meji ni won ma n gba ere idaraya na, fensa kan n du elomiin.
english: The sport is primarily played in a duel format, one fencer dueling another.

yoruba: Eré Gọ́ọ̀fù jẹ́ eré táwọn eléré ńlò kílọ̀bù fi gbá bọ́ọ̀lù sínú ihò.
english: Golf is a game in which players use clubs to hit balls into holes.

yoruba: Wọn fi ihò méjìdínlọ́gún ṣeré ní abala tó wọ́pọ̀, pẹ̀lú àwọn òṣèrè tó má bẹ́rẹ́ pẹ̀lú ihò àkọ́kọ́ ní abala yẹn tó sì parí lórí oní méjìdínlógún.
english: Eighteen holes are played during a regular round, with players usually starting on the first hole on the course and finishing on the eighteenth.

yoruba: Òṣèrè tó wẹ omi díẹ̀, tàbí mú orí ẹgbẹ́ rl wù, láti parí ìdíje náà.
english: The player who takes the fewest strokes, or swings of the club, to complete the course wins.

yoruba: Orí koróko ni wọ́n tí ń se eré náà, àti pé wọ́n má ń gé koríko tó wà ní ẹ̀bá ihò kúrú èyí tí wọ́n ń pè ní àwọ̀ ewéko.
english: The game is played on grass, and the grass around the hole is mown shorter and called the green.

yoruba: Bóyá ìrìn àjò afẹ́ tó wọ́ pọ̀jù lọ ní ní èyi tí àwọn èyàn ma ń kà mọ́ ìrìn àjò: ìrìn àjò ìdárayá.
english: Perhaps the most common type of tourism is what most people associate with traveling: Recreation tourism.

yoruba: Èyí ni bí àwọn èèyàn bá lọ sí ibìkan tó yàtọ̀ sáwọn ibi tó yàtọ̀ sí ìgbé aué òòjọ́ wọn láti lọ gbádùn , se fàájì ara wọn.
english: This is when people go to a place that is very different from their regular day-to-day life to relax and have fun.

yoruba: Ẹtí òkun, ọgbà ńlá àti àwọn ìpàgọ́ jẹ́ ibí tí àwọn arìnrìn-àjò-afẹ́ máa ń rin jùlọ.
english: Beaches, theme parks and camp grounds are often the most common places frequented by recreational tourists.

yoruba: Tí èróngbà ìbẹ̀wò sí ibi kan ní láti mọ̀ nípa ìtàn rẹ̀ ati àṣà rẹ̀ á jẹ́ pé ìrún àbẹ̀wò yìí ní a lè pè ní àbẹ̀wò aláṣà.
english: If the objective of one’s visit to a particular place is to get to know its history and culture then this type of tourism is known as cultural tourism.

yoruba: Awọn olubẹwo arinrinajo le ṣe abẹwo si oriṣiriṣi ibi ti o ba ṣe pataki ni orilẹ ede kan tabi ki wọn yan lati dojukọ ibikan pato.
english: Tourists may visit different landmarks of a particular country or they may simply opt to focus on just one area.

yoruba: Awon amunisin, nigbati won ri isele ti o sele, ti pe fun iranlowo.
english: The Colonists, seeing this activity, had also called for reinforcements.

yoruba: Àwọn ọmọ ogun tí o ń fi agbára kún lílọ síwájú pẹ̀lú ìlànà ìpín ìkíní àti ìkẹta New Hampshirs pẹ̀lú igba ọmọ ogun lábé olùsọ John Stark àti James Reed (tí àwọn méjèèjì sì padà di ọgagun nígbà tó yá.
english: Troops reinforcing the forward positions included the 1st and 3rd New Hampshire regiments of 200 men, under Colonels John Stark and James Reed (both later became generals).

yoruba: Lórí ọ̀rọ̀ àwọn amúnisìn, tó wà ní ààlà tí ó kángun ní àríwá ni àwọn ọkùnrin Stark fi ìkàlẹ̀ s.
english: Stark's men took positions along the fence on the north end of the Colonist's position.

yoruba: Nigbati sisan omi ya egbe kan lule ni ojusan odo Mystic lona ariwa Peninsula,ni kiakia ni won mo gegele pelu okuta ni ariwa eyiti o fori so adagun kekere kan.
english: When low tide opened a gap along the Mystic River along the northeast of the peninsula, they quickly extended the fence with a short stone wall to the north ending at the water's edge on a small beach.

yoruba: Gridley tàbí Stark gbé gègé bí i ìwọn ẹsẹ̀ ọgọ́rùn ún (ọgbọ̀n mítà) níwájú ósì pàṣẹ pé wọn kò gbọdọ̀ tanná títí tí òfin déédé yóó fi gùn ún.
english: Gridley or Stark placed a stake about 100 feet (30 m) in front of the fence and ordered that no one fire until the regulars passed it.

yoruba: Ètò ìlú Amerika gbẹ́kẹ̀ lé kíkọjú ìjàkadì láti ibi ọ̀tọ̀tọ̀ mẹ́ta.
english: The American plan relied on launching coordinated attacks from three different directions.

yoruba: Ọ̀gágun John Cadwalder ma ṣàfilọ́lẹ̀ ìkọlù ìyàtọ̀ tó lòdì sí British garrison ní Bordentown, láti lè dínà fún ìrànlọ́wọ́.
english: General John Cadwalder would launch a diversionary attack against the British garrison at Bordentown, in order to block off any reinforcements.

yoruba: Ọ̀gágun James Ewing yóò kó ọmọ ológun ẹ̀ẹ́dẹ́gbẹ̀rin fo oi tó wà ní Trenton Ferry, gba afárá lórí Odọ̀ Assunpinki, gbégi rénà ikọ̀ ọlọ̀tẹ̀ kí wọ́n má lè sá.
english: General James Ewing would take 700 militia across the river at Trenton Ferry, seize the bridge over the Assunpink Creek and prevent any enemy troops from escaping.

yoruba: Àwọn ikọ̀ ajagun tí okùnrin ẹgbẹ̀rún méjì ó lé irinwó máa la odò máìlì mẹ́san ní apá àríwá Trenton kọjá, wọ́n á sì pín sí ọ̀wọ̣́ méjì; ọ̀kan lábę Greene àti ọ̀kan láẹ́ Sullivan, láti lè se àkọlù kílẹ̀ tó mọ́.
english: The main assault force of 2,400 men would cross the river nine miles north of Trenton, and then split into two groups, one under Greene and one under Sullivan, in order to launch a pre-dawn attack.

yoruba: Pẹ̀lú àyípadà eré sísá láti ìdámẹ́ta sí ìdá méjì máìlì, yíyára dí ohun tí kò fi bẹ́ẹ̀ wúlò lọ́pọ̀ tí ìfaradà sì di dandan.
english: With the change from the quarter to the half mile run, speed becomes of much less importance and endurance becomes an absolute necessity.

yoruba: Bẹ́ẹ̀ni, ògbóǹragìrì onídajì máìlì, ọkùnrin tó lè na ìṣẹ́jú méjì, ó gbọ́dọ̀ ní iye ìrìn tó tó lẹ́sẹ̀, àmọ́ ìfàyàrán gbọ́dọ̀ wà ní fún gbogbo ewu.
english: Of course a first-class half-miler, a man who can beat two minutes, must be possessed of a fair amount of speed, but endurance must be cultivated at all hazards.

yoruba: Àwọn orílẹ̀èdè kan máa ń sáré ní àsìko òtútù, tí wọn a sì paápọ̀ mọ́ ìgbáradì fún erésísa láti jẹ́ kí ara wọn wuyì lókè, ẹ̀yí ni ìgbáradì ti ó dára jùlọ fún ìdíje eré sísá.
english: Some cross country running during the winter, combined with gymnasium work for the upper part of the body, is the best preparation for the running season.

yoruba: Síse àwọn àsà asarelóore lásán kò le ní sagbẹ́tẹrù eréje tó dára jù, àmọ́ ó lè nípa lórí ìjípépé àwọn ọ̀dọ́mọdé eléré-ìje.
english: Proper nutritional practices alone cannot generate elite performances, but they can significantly affect young athletes' overall wellness.

yoruba: Wíwà ní ìlera àti okun tí ó pé, mímú omi bí oh ti tọ́ àti níní òye àwọn ohun tí ẹniyàn lè máa jẹ tí yóò fún ni ní ìlera kíkún lè ran àwọn tí wọn máa n ṣe eré ìdárayá lọ́wọ́ láti gbádun ìdárayá, kí wọ́n sì ṣe dáadáa nínú rẹ̀.
english: Maintaining a healthy energy balance, practicing effective hydration habits, and understanding the various aspects of supplementation practices can help athletes improve their performance and increase their enjoyment of the sport.

yoruba: Ìsáré ní ààrín méjí jé eré ìdárayá tí kò wọ́n jú, síbẹ́ àwọn èrò tí kò jẹ òótọ́ tó jẹma àwọn irinṣẹ́ díẹ̀ tí o nílò láti kópa.
english: Middle distance running is a relatively inexpensive sport; however, there are many misconceptions regarding the few pieces of equipment required to participate.

yoruba: A lè ra ohun èlò nígbàkúgbà tí lílò bá kàn án, àmọ́ ọ̀pọ̀ ní kò ní ní ipa púpò tàbí kankan lóri ìsé.
english: Products can be purchased as needed, but most will have little or no real impact on performance.

yoruba: Awon elere idaraya le ro pe won feran oja kan pelu pe ko fun wa ni anfani gidi kan kan.
english: Athletes may feel that they prefer a product even when it provides no real benefits.

yoruba: Átọ́mù jẹ ǹkan tó ṣe pàtàkì láti kọ lé.
english: The atom can be considered to be one of the fundamental building blocks of all matter.

yoruba: Ó jẹ́ ǹkan tó ṣẹlẹgẹ́ tó ní, gẹ́gẹ́ bí àwòṣe Bohr, ti nucleus àárín orbited by electrons, tó jọ planet orbiting the sun wo Figure 1.1.
english: Its a very complex entity which consists, according to a simplified Bohr model, of a central nucleus orbited by electrons, somewhat similar to planets orbiting the sun - see Figure 1.1.

yoruba: Èròjà méjì ní ó wà ninú ìwọ́jọsí - àwọn náà ni neutrons àti protons.
english: The nucleus consists of two particles - neutrons and protons.

yoruba: Protons ní agbára iná nígbàtí neutron kò ní agbára rárá. Awon electrons ni aagbara ina olodi.
english: Protons have a positive electric charge while neutrons have no charge. The electrons have a negative electric charge.

yoruba: Láti sàyẹ̀wò afarakáásá, o gbọ́dọ̀ kọ́kọ́ se àyèwò ibi ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ fún ààbò ara rẹ.
english: To Check the victim, you must first survey the scene to ensure your safety.

yoruba: O ni láti ṣe àwòfin ipò tí ẹni tó fi ara pa wà bí o ṣe ń sún mọ́ ọ.
english: You need to notice the victim's position as you approach him or her and any automatic red flags.

yoruba: Ti o ba farapa nigbati o n ṣe iranlọwọ, awọn nkan le bajẹ sii.
english: If you get hurt trying to help, you may only serve to make matters worse.

yoruba: Iṣẹ́ ìwádìí náà fìdí rẹ̀ múlẹ̀ pé ìbànújẹ́, ẹ̀rù àti àjálù má a ń ṣokùnfà ìrora àti àìlera fún àwọn tó má a ń ní ìrora ní ìsàlẹ̀ ẹ̀hìn.
english: The study found that depression, fear and catastrophising mediated the relationship between pain and disability in lower back pain sufferers.

yoruba: Awọn ipa ti ajalu nikan, kiise ibanujẹ tabi ẹru ni o jẹ awọn isẹlẹ gbẹdẹkẹ ọlọsọọsẹ ti akoko PA.
english: Only the effects of catastrophizing, not depression and fear was conditional of regular weekly structured PA sessions.

yoruba: Àwọn tó n ṣe ìṣe dédé wọn nílò ìgbèlẹ́yìn sí nípasẹ̀ ojú burúkú tí a fi n rí ẹ̀dùn sí, láti tọ́ka sí ìyàtọ̀ láàrín ẹ̀dùn ti o jẹ́ àìsàn àti ẹ̀dùn tí o wáyé torípé a gbọn ara.
english: Those participating in regular activity required more support in terms of negative perception of pain distinguishing the differences of chronic pain and discomfort feel from normal physical movement.

yoruba: Ìran tàbí okun láti ríran dá lórí ẹ̀yà ìríran .
english: Vision, or the ability to see depends on visual system sensory organs or eyes.

yoruba: Orisirisi ona ni a n gba da oju, eyi ti o pe orisirisi lori bi awon ohun ti kokoro aifojuri se lagbara to.
english: There are many different constructions of eyes, ranging in complexity depending on the requirements of the organism.

yoruba: Àwọn oríṣiríṣi ìkọ́lé náà ni onírúru agbára, wọn si ní ìmòlára sí onírurú ìgbì áti wípé wọn ní yíyàtọ̀ ìwọ̀n sí mímu, pèlu pé wọn nílò onírúurú ìlànà láti mu ìtumò jádẹ lára ìgbéwọlẹ́ áti oríṣiríṣi nọ́mbà láti sisẹ́ dáradára.
english: The different constructions have different capabilities, are sensitive to different wave-lengths and have differing degrees of acuity, also they require different processing to make sense of the input and different numbers to work optimally.

yoruba: Iye olùgbé ni ákójọ àwọn ohun ẹlẹ́yà kan láàrin agbègbè .
english: A population is the collection of organisms of a particular species within a given geographic area.

yoruba: Nígbàtí àwọn èèyàn tó wà níbùgbé bá ní ìdánimọ̀ to ní í se pẹ̀lú ìjọra jíìnì, wọ́n má ń pèwọ́n ní oníjọra.
english: When all individuals in a population are identical with regard to a particular phenotypic trait they are known as monomorphic.

yoruba: Ti eyan kan ba fi opolopo orisi iwa kan han a je pe won wa polimofiki.
english: When the individuals show several variants of a particular trait they are polymorphic.

yoruba: Àwọn ikọ̀ ológun àwọn kòkòrò ma ń tó lọ́wọ̀ọ̀wọ́ sinmi nípele nípele pẹ̀lú.
english: Army ant colonies march and nest in different phases as well.

yoruba: Ní àyè àtijọ́, àwọn ọmọ ogun ìjàlọ rìn ní alé wọ́n sí ma dúró láti sùn ní ojú mọmọ.
english: In the nomadic phase, army ants march at night and stop to camp during the day.

yoruba: Ìletò náà bẹ̀rẹ̀ ìgbéayé má a kó kiri látibìkan sí ibòmíràn ńgbàti àdínkù bá oúnjẹ tó wà nílẹ̀, ìletò náà se ìtẹ́ ẹyẹ fúngbà kanná, èyí tí wọ́n má ń pààrọ̀ lójoójúmọ́.
english: The colony begins a nomadic phase when available food has decreased. During this phase, the colony makes temporary nests that are changed everyday.

yoruba: Òkànkan àwọn wíwu ìwà ipá àti ìfẹ̀hónúhàn àwọn alárànká yìí yìó wáyé fún bi ọjọ́ métàdínlógún.
english: Each of these nomadic rampages or marches lasts for approximately 17 days.

yoruba: Kínni sẹ́ẹ̀lì? ọ̀rọ̀ náà wá látinú ọ̀rọ̀ Latin tí wọ́n pè ní “cella”, ìtumọ̀ “iyàrá kékerá” tí wọ́n sì mún un jáde látinu àyẹ̀wò àwọn kòkòrò kékeré àti ètò ìdẹ́nu.
english: What is a cell? The word cell comes from the Latin word "cella", meaning "small room", and it was first coined by a microscopist observing the structure of cork.

yoruba: Sẹ́ẹ̀lì jẹ́ ǹkan pàtàkì ní ara gbogbo ǹkan tó ń mí, àti pé gbogbo organism lóní sẹ́ẹ̀lì kan tàbí jù bẹ́ẹ̀ lọ ní ara wọn.
english: The cell is the basic unit of all living things, and all organisms are composed of one or more cells.

yoruba: Sẹ́ẹ̀lì jẹ́ nǹkan pàtàkì láti mọ̀ nípa ẹ̀kọ́ ayé, kódà wọn a máa pè wọn ni “amọ̀ tí a fi ń mọ ilẹ ayé wa”.
english: Cells are so basic and critical to the study of life, in fact, that they are often referred to as "the building blocks of life".

yoruba: Ohun tí ó ń mójútó bí àwọn iṣan ara se n siṣẹ́ nígbogbo ìgbà láti ri wípé ohun gbogbo ń lọ déédé nípa fifi ìró ránṣẹ́ sí gbogbo ẹ̀yà ara làti ri wípé ẹ̀jẹ̀ ń lọ káakkiri bí ó ti yẹ láìní ìdíwọ́ kankan.
english: The Nervous System maintains homeostasis by sending nerve impulses through out the body to keep the flow of blood going as well as undisturbed.

yoruba: Awon irora isan wonyi le lo kaakiri ara ti o ma n daabo bo ara lati owo ihale kankan to le waye.
english: These nerve impulses can be sent so quickly throughout the body which helps keep the body safe from any potential threat.

yoruba: Èfúùfù líle kọlu àwọn agbègbè kékeré yàtọ̀ sí àwọn ìjì líle mìíràn, sùgbọ́n wọ́n lè ba gbogbo nǹkan tó wà lójú ọ̀nà wọn jẹ́.
english: Tornadoes strike a small area compared to other violent storms, but they can destroy everything in their path.

yoruba: Ẹ̀fúùfù ńlá máa ń wú igi ti gbòǹgbò ti gbòǹgbò, a máá sí páànù ilé, a sì máa gbé ọkọ̀ lọ lójú òfurufú. Ìdá méjì ìjì a máa lò tayọ wákàtí mẹ́ta gbáko.
english: Tornadoes uproot trees, rip boards from buildings, and fling cars up into the sky. The most violent two percent of tornadoes last more than three hours.

yoruba: Ìjì líle ti gbì tó okòódínníẹ̀dẹ́gbẹ̀ta kìlómítà láàrin wákàtí (133 m/s; 300 mph).
english: These monster storms have winds up to 480 km/h (133 m/s; 300 mph).

yoruba: Awon eyan ti n se lo lensi won de ti n fi fe iran fun egbegberun odun.
english: Humans have been making and using lenses for magnification for thousands and thousands of years.

yoruba: Sibesibe, ero awoye akoko ni a se ni ilu awon alawo funfun ni opin 16th century.
english: However, the first true telescopes were made in Europe in the late 16th century.

yoruba: Awon ero iwoye yi n lo apapo lensi meji ti o n mu ki ohun ti o jina dabi ohun ti o sun mo ni, ti o si tobi.
english: These telescopes used a combination of two lenses to make distant objects appear both nearer and larger.

yoruba: Ìmọ taraẹni àti ìjẹkújẹ ma wà nínu wa ó sí jẹ́ nínu ìfọwasowọ́pọ̀ ní nígbàtí ọ̀pọ̀ ènìyàn bá jẹ́ àǹfàní ni a ma ṣe rí èrè ní ọjọ́ tí kò pẹ́
english: Greed and selfishness will always be with us and it is the nature of cooperation that when the majority benefit there will always be more to gain in the short term by acting selfishly

yoruba: A n retí pé ọ̀pọ̀ èyàn ló ma ríi pé iṣẹ́ àparapọ̀ ṣe pẹ̀lú àwọn èyàn ló sàn jù fún ìgbà gígùn.
english: Hopefully, most people will realise that their long term best option is to work together with others.

yoruba: Ọ̀pọ̀ èyàn ló n lálàá ọjọ́ tí ọmọ èyàn á rin ìrìn àjò lọ sínú ìràwọ̀ mín tí wọn á káàkiri agbaye mín, àwọn èyàn mín ń ro nkan tí ó wà ní agbaye mín àwọn kan gbàgbọ́ pé àwọn àjòjì mín wà, tàbí pé alàyè wà ní àwọn agbọn mín.
english: Many people dream of the day when humans can travel to another star and explore other worlds, some people wonder what's out there some belive that aliens or other life may live on another plant.

yoruba: Sùgbón, tí èyí ba´ sèlè rárá èyí ti kò dájú láti sẹlè ní ọjọ́ iwájú. Àwọn ìràwọ̀ náà fọn kàkiri tó bẹ gẹ wípé àwọn àìmoyẹ ìbùsọ̀ láárin àwọn ìràwọ̀ wònyí tí sẹ “aládùúgbò”.
english: But, if this ever does happen probably won't happen for a very long time. The stars are so spread out that there are trillions of miles between stars that are "neighbors".

yoruba: Bóyá ní ọjọ́ kan, àwọn ọmọ ọmọ ọmọ rẹ yí ò ma dúró lórí ilẹ̀ àjèjì máa wòye nípa àwọn baba àtijọ wọn?
english: Maybe one day, your great grandchildren will be standing atop an alien world wondering about their ancient ancestors?

yoruba: Àpapọ̀ seeli púpọ̀ la fí dá ẹlẹ́ran ara. Wọ́n ma n jẹun wọn dẹ̀ ma n fọ́ọsí wẹ́wẹ́ nínú wọn. Púpọ̀ nínú ẹlẹ́ran ara ló lè rìn.
english: Animals are made of many cells. They eat things and digest them inside. Most animals can move.

yoruba: Awon eranko nikan ni o ni opolo (bo tile je wipe kiise gbogbo eranko ni o ni opolo; fun apere eja jelly, ko ni opolo).
english: Only animals have brains (though not even all animals do; jellyfish, for example, do not have brains).

yoruba: Eranko wà káàkiri Gbogbo àgbáyé. Wọn a ma gbẹ́lẹ̀, wọ́n a má lúwẹ̀ẹ́ ọ̀sà, wọ́n a ma fò lójú ọ̀run.
english: Animals are found all over the earth. They dig in the ground, swim in the oceans, and fly in the sky.

yoruba: Seeli je eya ara (awon) alaye to kere ju to ni itosona ati ise.
english: A cell is the smallest structural and functional unit of a living (things) organism.

yoruba: Seeli wa lati oro Latiini cella to tumo si iyara kekere.
english: Cell comes from the Latin word cella which means small room.

yoruba: Tí ẹ bá wo àwọn nǹkan ẹlẹ́mìí nínú ẹ̀rọ-awòjìji-amóhun-bín-tín-fẹ̀, a máa rí pé láti ara nnkan onígun tàbí nǹkan roboto ni a ti ṣẹ̀dá wọ.
english: If you look at living things under a microscope, you will see that they are made of small squares or balls.

yoruba: Robaati Hooki, Olùmọ̀ bayọ́lọ́jì láti ìlú Gẹ̀ẹ́sì, ri skwia kekeke ninu kooki pẹ̀lú ẹ̀rọ mikroskopi.
english: Robert Hooke, a biologist from England, saw small squares in cork with a microscope.

yoruba: Wọn jọ iyàrá. Òun ní ẹni àkọ́kọ́ tí ó kọ́ sèwòye òòkú sẹ́ẹ̀lì.
english: They looked like rooms. He was the first person to observe dead cells

yoruba: Àwọn elimenti ati compandi lè pàrọ̀ ara láti dídáwà kan sí òmíràn láì yípadà.
english: Elements and compounds can move from one state to another and not change.

yoruba: Nitrogen gẹ́gẹ́ bí gáàsì ṣì ní ohun èlò bí i nitrogen olómi. ipò omi ti dìpọ̀ ṣùgbọ́n molecule náà kò yàtọ.
english: Nitrogen as a gas still has the same properties as liquid nitrogen. The liquid state is denser but the molecules are still the same.

yoruba: Omi jẹ́ àpẹrẹ mìíràn. omi compound water ni wọ́n fi hydrogen atom méjì àti oxgen atom kan se.
english: Water is another example. The compound water is made up of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.

yoruba: O ní àgbẹkalẹ̀ mólíkuúlà kan náà yálà o jẹ aféfé gaasi, olómi, tàbí ní lìlẹ.
english: It has the same molecular structure whether it is a gas, liquid, or solid.

yoruba: Ṣùgbọ́n ipò tó wà lè yípadà, ipò kẹ́míkà rè lè wà bó ṣe wà.
english: Although its physical state may change, its chemical state remains the same.

yoruba: Àkókò loun tí ó yíwa káàkiri, tí ó sì má ń nípa lórí ohun gbogbo tí a bá ṣe, à mọ́ ó ṣòro láti mọ̀.
english: Time is something that is all around us, and affects everything we do, yet is difficult to understand.

yoruba: Àwọn ẹlẹ́sìn, oní fìlọ́sọ́fà àtàwọn ọnímọ̀ sáyẹ̀nsìti ń se ìwáàdí àkókò fún ẹgbẹ̀lẹ́gbẹ̀ ọdún.
english: Time has been studied by religious, philosophical, and scientific scholars for thousands of years.

yoruba: A ní ìrírí àkókò gẹ́gẹ́ bí ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ tó ń ṣẹ̀ lẹ́sẹsẹ láti ọjọ́ iwájú wá sí àkókò yìí lọ sẹ́yìn.
english: We experience time as a series of events passing from the future through the present to the past.

yoruba: Àkókò tún jẹ́ bi a se ń ṣe àfiwẹ́ iye àkókò (gígùn) àwọn ìsẹ̀lẹ̀.
english: Time is also how we compare the duration (length) of events.

yoruba: O le ka bi igba se n lo fun ara re ko maa wo bi isele ayipo se tun sele. Isele ayipo je nkan to ma n saba sele leemewa si.
english: You can mark the passing of time yourself by observing the repetition of a cyclical event. A cyclical event is something that happens again and again regularly.

yoruba: Lóde òní wọ́n máa ń lo Kòǹpútà láti sàtúnrọ àwòrán àti fọ́ńrán.
english: Computers today are used to manipulate pictures and videos.

yoruba: A le lo ẹ̀rọ ayárabíàṣá láti ya àwọn Awòrán ìgbàlódé tó jẹ ojú ni gbèsè, àwọn irúfẹ́ àwòrán wọ̀nyí ni wọ́n ń lo ní orí amóhùnmáwòrán àti ní inú àgbéléwò báyìí.
english: Sophisticated animations can be constructed on computers, and this kind of animation is increasingly being used in television and films.

yoruba: A má n sába gba orin sílẹ̀ pẹ̀lú àwọn ẹ̀rọ ayára bí àṣá gíga ti a fi n lò láti ṣètò àti po àwọn ìró ohùn pọ̀.
english: Music is often recorded using sophisticated computers to process and mix sounds together.

yoruba: Fún ọjọ́ pípẹ́ lákòókò sẹ́ńtúrì kọkàndínlógún àti ogún, wọ́n gbàgbọ́ wípé àwọn tó kọ́kọ́ gbé ní New Zealand làwọn èèyàn Maori, tí wọ́n má ń dọdẹ ẹyẹ ńlá tí wọ́n pè ní moas.
english: For a long time during the nineteenth and twentieth centuries, it was believed the first inhabitants of New Zealand were the Maori people, who hunted giant birds called moas.

yoruba: Àlàyé náà wá fìdí àbá náà múlẹ̀ pé àwọn ènìyàn Maori kúrò láti Polynesia pẹ̀lú ọkọ̀ tó dára wọ́n sì lọ sí New Zealand láti Maori tí wọ́n sì dá ẹgbẹ́ ǹkan ọ̀gbìn sílẹ̀.
english: The theory then established the idea that the Maori people migrated from Polynesia in a Great Fleet and took New Zealand from the Moriori, establishing an agricultural society.

yoruba: Àmọ́ṣá, ẹ̀rí tuntun sàfihàn pé àwọn Moriori jẹ́ ọ̀kan lára àwọn Maori tí wọ́n kó kúrò ní New Zealand lọ sí erékùsù Chatham, se àgbéga àṣà ti wọn tó yàtọ̀ találàfíà.
english: However, new evidence suggests that the Moriori were a group of mainland Maori who migrated from New Zealand to the Chatham Islands, developing their own distinctive, peaceful culture.

yoruba: Èyà míràn tún wà ní ẹrékùsù Chatham àwon ara´ Maori tí won sèwá láti New Zealand.
english: There was also another tribe on the Chatham islands these were Maori who migrated away from New Zealand.

yoruba: Wọn pẹ ara wọn ní àwọn ónilé ẹrẹ́kúsú Polynesia àwọn àkùdẹ́ díè bàwọn ní ìgbẹ̀yìn, àwọn ónilé ẹrẹ́kúsú Polynesia di píparẹ́.
english: They called themselves the Moriori there were a few skirmishes and in the end, the Moriori were wiped out

yoruba: Àwọn ẹnìyàn tí wọ́n wà nínu rl fún ọdún pípẹ́ gbóríyìn fún agbára àti ìfẹ́ wa nígbà tí wọ́n ṣàyẹ̀wó àìṣedédé wa àti ìjákulẹ̀ w.
english: Individuals who had been involved for several decades helped us appreciate our strengths and passions while candidly assessing difficulties and even failures.

yoruba: Nígbà tá à ń gbọ́ bí oníkálukú se ń sọ ìtàn oníkóówá, mọ̀lẹ́bí, àti ẹgbẹ́jẹgbẹ́, a rí ìwòye tó péye sí ìṣẹ̀lẹ̀ tó ti sẹ̀ sẹ́yín àti àwọn èèyàn tó ti se rere tàbí ìbàjẹ́ sí àṣà ẹgbẹ́.
english: While listening to individuals share their individual, family, and organizational stories, we gained valuable insight into the past and some of the personalities who influenced for good or ill the culture of the organization.

yoruba: Bí èyàn bá mọ ìtàn àbáláyé ẹni kò túmọ̀ si wípe o mọ àṣà náà, ṣùgbọ́n ó ṣáà ma n jẹ́ kí ibi tí èyàn wà bọ́ sí ètò ilé iṣé náà yé wọn.
english: While understanding one’s history does not assume understanding of culture, it does at least help people gain a sense of where they fall within the history of the organization.

yoruba: Nigba ti aba n se ayewo aseyori, ti a si n se akiesi awon ijakule, onikaluku ati gbogbo awon to kopa se akiyesi ti o jinle lori isepataki si, awon ohun ti o se koko ati opakutele ti ajo naa.
english: While assessing the successes and becoming aware of failures, individuals and the whole of the participating persons discover more deeply the values, mission, and driving forces of the organization.

yoruba: Fún àpẹẹrẹ yìí, sise ìrántí àwọn ìhùwàsí onísòwò àti àwọn àbájáde àsẹyorí rẹ máà n ran àwọn ènìyàn lọ́wọ́ làti tẹ́wọ́gba àwọn ìyàtọ titun àti ìdarí fún ìjọ agbẹ̀gbẹ̀.
english: In this instance, recalling previous instances of entrepreneurial behavior and resulting successes helped people to be open to new changes and new direction for the local church.

yoruba: Àwọn ìtàn àseyọrí mún àdínkù bá ìbẹ̀rù àyípadà, níbí ó se ń sàfilọ́lẹ̀ ìfọkànsìn rere fún àyípadapà ọjọ́ iwájú.
english: Such success stories lessened the fears of change, while creating positive inclinations toward change in the future.

yoruba: Ona ijiroro lorisirisi ti isowopo je ara ona ti an gba wa ojutu si awon isoro ti o so orisi imo ati iyapa papo.
english: Convergent thinking patterns are problem solving techniques that unite different ideas or fields to find a solution.

yoruba: Afojusun ero okan yi ni ijafafa, ogbon inu ati isefefe o tun je ida otito mo, lilo awon eeto aatele ti o wa nile, kiko otito jo.
english: The focus of this mindset is speed, logic and accuracy, also identification of facts, reapplying existing techniques, gathering information.

yoruba: Ohun tí ó se pàtàkì jù nínú èrò yìí ni : ìdáhùn ẹyọ̀kan ṣoṣo ni ó tọ̀na. Ìsáhùn méjì péré ni yíò wá sọ́kàn rẹ, èyí náà ni, èyí tó tọ̀nà tàbí èyí tí kò tọ̀nà.
english: The most important factor of this mindset is: there is only one correct answer. You only think of two answers, namely right or wrong.

yoruba: Iru ironu yii ni nkan se pelu imo ijinle abi gbedeke ilana to wa.
english: This type of thinking is associated with certain science or standard procedures.

yoruba: Àwọn ènìyàn tó ní irú ìrònú yìí ní ọpọlọ, wọ́n sì lè gbé ǹkan sórí, wọ́n lè yànjú ìṣòro wọ́n sì lè ṣiṣẹ́ lórí ìdánwò síẹ́nsì.
english: People with this type of thinking have logical thinking, are able to memorize patterns, solve problems and work on scientific tests.

yoruba: Àwọn ọmọ ènìyàn ni wọ́n ni ẹ̀bùn kí wọ́n máa wòye ohun tó ń lọ ni ọkan ẹlòmìíràn.
english: Humans are by far the most talented species in reading the minds of others.

yoruba: Eyi tumọ si wipe a ṣe aṣeyọri lori sisọ asọtẹlẹ lori ohun ti awọn eniyan n ro, pinnu, gbagbọ, mọ tabi gba lero.
english: That means we are able to successfully predict what other humans perceive, intend, believe, know or desire.

yoruba: Nínú gbogbo àwọn okun wọ̀nyí, mímọ èròngbà àwọn ẹlòmíràn ṣe kókó. O maa ń fàyè gbà wá láti yanjú ìṣòro tó séeṣée kó jẹyọ láti ara ìbàkégbépọ ara wa.
english: Among these abilities, understanding the intention of others is crucial. It allows us to resolve possible ambiguities of physical actions.

yoruba: Fun apeere, ti o ba ri enikan to n fo gilaasi oko, iwo o rope o fe ji oko oloko ni.
english: For example, if you were to see someone breaking a car window, you would probably assume he was trying to steal a stranger’s car.

yoruba: Ọkùnrin náà yíò nílò láti gba ìdájọ́ ní àràọ̀tọ̀ tó bá jẹ́ wípé ó pàdánù kọ́kọ́rọ́ ọkọ̀ rẹ̀ tó sì jẹ́ wípé ọkọ̀ rẹ̀ ló ń gbìyànjú àti já wọ̀.
english: He would need to be judged differently if he had lost his car keys and it was his own car that he was trying to break into.

yoruba: MRI dálórí kàyéfì fisiksi ta n pè ní nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR), tí Felisi Boṣi (tó n ṣiṣẹ́ ní Ile Iwe Giga Stanfodi) àti Edwodi Pọseli (láti Ile Iwe Giga Hafadi) wárí ní 1930s.
english: MRI is based on a physics phenomenon called nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR), which was discovered in the 1930s by Felix Bloch (working at Stanford University) and Edward Purcell (from Harvard University).

yoruba: Nínú àbájáde yìí, àwọn pápá agbérin àti ìgbì rádíò tó ń fa átọ̀mù tó fún àwọn rádìò kéèkèé ní àfihàn.
english: In this resonance, magnetic field and radio waves cause atoms to give off tiny radio signals.

yoruba: Ní ọdún 1970, dókítà oníṣègùn òyìnbó àti ìwádìí ìmò sáyẹ́nsì, Raymond Damadian, ṣàwárí lílo ohun òǹfà ìgbohún-pẹ̀lú -àwòrán jáde gẹ́gẹ́ bí ẹ̀rọ fún àyẹ̀wò àwọn ìwádìí àìsàn.
english: In the year 1970, Raymond Damadian, a medical doctor and research scientist, discovered the basis for using magnetic resonance imaging as a tool for medical diagnosis.

yoruba: Lẹ́yìn ọdún mẹ́rin wọ́n fàṣe sí ẹ̀tọ́ náà, ó jẹ́ ẹ̀tọ́ àkọ́kọ́ tí wọ́n ma fàṣę sí nínú ẹ̀kọ MRI.
english: Four years later a patent was granted, which was the world's first patent issued in the field of MRI.

yoruba: Ni 1977, Dokita Damadiani pari kiko skana MRI “ti gbogbo ara” to pe ni “Ailesegun”.
english: In 1977, Dr. Damadian completed the construction of the first “whole-body” MRI scanner, which he called the ”Indomitable”.

yoruba: Ijiroro ti ko waye ni akoko kanna ma n fi aye sile fun arojinle ati ifura si.
english: Asynchronous communication encourages time for reflection and reaction to others.

yoruba: Ó fún àwọn akẹ́ẹ̀kọ́ ní agbára àt siṣẹ́ pẹ̀lú ipa wọn àti bí wọ́n se lè kápá àlàyé ìròyìn wọn.
english: It allows students the ability to work at their own pace and control the pace of instructional information.

yoruba: Ní afikún, àwọn ìfòfindè àkóókò pẹ̀lú àfojúsùn àti múndẹ̀rùn akókò isẹ́ wáyé. (Bremer, 1998)
english: In addition, there are fewer time restrictions with the possibility of flexible working hours. (Bremer, 1998)

yoruba: Lilo ẹrọ ayelujara ati itakun-agbaye fun awọn akẹkọ maa jẹki wọn ni anfani si alaye ni gbogbo igba.
english: The use of the Internet and the World Wide Web allows learners to have access to information at all times.

yoruba: Àwọn akẹ́ẹ̀kọ́ lè fi ìbéèrè wọn ṣọwọ́ sáwọn olùdarí nígbàkúgbà kí wọ́n sì retí èsì kíákiá, ju kí wọ́n má a dúró de àkókò ípàdé ojú ko ojú tó ń bọ̀.
english: Students can also submit questions to instructors at any time of day and expect reasonably quick responses, rather than waiting until the next face-to-face meeting.

yoruba: Ìgbẹ́sẹ̀ igbeyin ìgbàlódẹ́ lọ́wọ́lọ́wọ́ sí ẹ̀kọ́ kíkọ́ sètò òmìnira kúrò lọ́wọ́ àwọn Ìgbẹ́sẹ̀ áìlatúnsẹ. Kò sí ọ̀nà kan sọsọ tò dára jùlo fún ẹ̀kọ́ kíkọ.
english: The postmodern approach to learning offers the freedom from absolutes. There is no one good way to learn.

yoruba: Kódà, kò sí ohunkóhun tí ó dára láti kọ́. ìkọ́ni máa ń wáyé nínú ìrírí láàrín akẹ́kọ̀ọ́ àti ìmọ̀ tí a gbé kalẹ̀.
english: In fact, there is not one good thing to learn.Learning takes place in the experience between the learner and the knowledge presented.

yoruba: Ìrírí wa lọ́wọ́lọ́wọ́ pélù sísẹsé fúnraeni àti fífi àlàyé síta, àwọn àfihàn amóhùnmáwòrán fún ìkẹ́ẹ́kọ́ sàpèjúwẹ ìtọ́kasí yìí.
english: Our current experience with all the do-it-yourself and information presenting, learning-based television shows illustrates this point.

yoruba: Opolopo ninu wa ni a ba ara wa ti a n wo ere ori amohunmaworan to sàfihàn iriri tabi isesi ti a o le kopa ninu tabi ya imo re lo lai lai.
english: So many of us find ourselves watching a television show that informs us of a process or experience in which we will never participate or apply that knowledge.

yoruba: A o ni ti moto pale lai lai, ko orisun omi si eyinkule wa, rin lo Peru lati lo wo erunrun atijo, abi tun ile aladugbo wa ko.
english: We will never overhaul a car, build a fountain in our backyard, travel to Peru to examine ancient ruins, or remodel our neighbour's house.

yoruba: Ọpẹlopé àsopọ̀ okùn fibre lábẹ́ òkun tsi Yuropi ati boolu gbohungbohun satelaiti, Greenland ní àsopọ̀ dada pẹ̀lú 93% àwọn èrò tó ráyè lo íńtánẹ̀ẹ̀tì.
english: Thanks to undersea fiber optic cable links to Europe and broadband satellite, Greenland is well connected with 93% of the population having internet access.

yoruba: Agbàlejò tàbí ilé ìtura rẹ (tí o bá ń wà ní ilé ìgbàlejò tàbí ilé ẹnìkan) lè ní wifi tàbí íntánẹ́ẹ̀tì tí wọ́n sopọ̀ mọ́ ẹ̀rọ kọ́mpútà, àti pé gbogbo ilé ìgbàlejò lóní ìyàrá íntánẹ́ẹ̀tì tàbí ibi tóní wifi fún gbogbogbò.
english: Your hotel or hosts (if staying in a guesthouse or private home) will likely have wifi or an internet connected PC, and all settlements have an internet cafe or some location with public wifi.

yoruba: Bi a se daruko loke, biotilejepe ni ilu Amerika won gba ilo “Eskimo”, o si je eebu leti awon ti o ki n se ara Amerika - awon ara Atiki paa pa julo ni Kanada.
english: As mentioned above, though the word "Eskimo" remains acceptable in the United States, it is considered pejorative by many non-U.S. Arctic peoples, especially in Canada.

yoruba: O lè ma gbọ́ pé áwọn ará Grinlandikí se n ló oro náà, o yẹ kí àwọn àjèjì má a sọ́ra fún un.
english: While you may hear the word used by Greenlandic Natives, its use should be avoided by foreigners.

yoruba: Àwọn ará ìlú Greenland ma ń pera wọ́n ní Inuit ní Canada àti Kalaalleq (ọ̀pọ̀ Kalaallit Greenlander ní Greenland.
english: The native inhabitants of Greenland call themselves Inuit in Canada and Kalaalleq (plural Kalaallit), a Greenlander, in Greenland.

yoruba: ọ̀ràn, àti èrò ibi sí àlejò, jẹ ohun tí wọn kò mọ̀ ni Greenland. Kódà ní ìlú náàm kò sí “agbègbè tó rí jákujàk.”
english: Crime, and ill-will toward foreigners in general, is virtually unknown in Greenland. Even in the towns, there are no "rough areas."

yoruba: Ojú ọjọ́ to tutù sẹésẹ́ ko jé ẹwu kan sọsọ tí aláìmúrasílè ma dojú kọ.
english: Cold weather is perhaps the only real danger the unprepared will face.

yoruba: To ba bẹ Grinlandi wo ni igba otutu (bi o ṣe n sumọ apa ariwa, o n tutu si i) o ṣe dandan fun ẹ lati mu aṣọ otutu to peye wa.
english: If you visit Greenland during cold seasons (considering that the further north you go, the colder it will be), it is essential to bring warm enough clothing.

yoruba: Awon ojo to gun ju ni igba ooru le muu ki o soro lati ri orun to peye sun ati awon ailera bee.
english: The very long days in the summer can lead to problems getting sufficient sleep and associated health issues.

yoruba: Ní àsìkò ooru, fojúsílẹ̀ pẹ̀lú fún àwọn ẹ̀fọn alárìnká. Bó tilẹ̀ jẹ́ pé wọ́n kì ń tan àrùn ká, wọ́n a má a rìnyànláyà.
english: During the summer, also watch out for the Nordic mosquitoes. Although they do not transmit any diseases, they can be irritating.

yoruba: Níwọn ìgbà tí aje San Francisco sopọ mọ bó ṣe jẹ́ ibi ti gbogbo arìnrìnajo àgbáyẹ nífẹsí, ajẹ́ rẹ jẹ́ oníruurú.
english: While San Francisco's economy is linked to it being a world-class tourist attraction, its economy is diversified.

yoruba: Àwọn ẹka tó ń gbanisíṣẹ́ jùlọ ní àwọn akọ́ṣẹ́-mọṣẹ́, ìjọba, ìnáwó, ìsòwò àti ìrìn àjò afẹ́.
english: The largest employment sectors are professional services, government, finance, trade, and tourism.

yoruba: Ìfihàn àwòrán rẹ lóorèkòorẹ́ nínú orin, ẹrẹ́, lítírẹ́sọ̀ àti àsa olókìkí ti ran Ìlu náà áti sọ ámí rẹ di mímọ̀ ní gbogbo àgbáyẹ́.
english: Its frequent portrayal in music, films, literature and popular culture has helped make the city and its landmarks known throughout the world.

yoruba: San Francisco ti ka ilé ńlá fún àwọn tó rìnrìn àjò wá sí ìlú yẹn pẹ̀lú ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ àwọn ilé ìtura, ilé óúnjé, àti ibi tí wọ́n ti lè ṣe ìpàdé.
english: San Francisco has developed a large tourist infrastructure with numerous hotels, restaurants, and top-notch convention facilities.

yoruba: San Francisco jẹ́ ọ̀kan lára àwọn ibi tó dára jù ní orílẹ̀èdè fún oúnjẹ ilẹ Asia; Korean, Thai, Indian, àti Japan.
english: San Francisco is also one of the best places in the nation for other Asian cuisine: Korean, Thai, Indian, and Japanese.

yoruba: Ìrìn àjò lọ sí Walt Disney World, dúró gẹ́gẹ́ ìrìnàjò pàtàkì fún àwọn ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ẹbí ní ilẹ̀ Amẹ́ríkà.
english: Traveling to Walt Disney World represents a major pilgrimage for many American families.

yoruba: Lọ́pọ̀ igba, àbẹ̀wò náà màa ń pẹ fún rìnrìn-àjò òfuurufú sí pápákọ̀ òfuurufú àgbáyé ti Orlando, wíwọ ọkọ̀ akérò sí ilé ìtura Disney, lílọ bi ọ́sẹ́ kan láìkúrò lórí ́àwọǹ ohun-ìní Disney, àti pípadà sílẹ́.
english: The "typical" visit involves flying into Orlando International Airport, busing to an on-site Disney hotel, spending about a week without leaving Disney property, and returning home.

yoruba: Orírìíríṣìí ìdí ni ó wà tí o lè fà á, ṣùgbọ́n èyí jẹ́ ohun tí ọ̀pọ̀ ní lọ́kàn tí wọ́n ba ń sọ̀rọ̀ nípa “lílọ sí Disney World”.
english: There are infinite variations possible, but this remains what most people mean when they talk of "going to Disney World".

yoruba: Awọn tikẹẹti ti wọn ta lori oju iwe ayelujara ti wọn ti n se basa bii eBay abi Craiglist, ma n jẹ aloku tikẹẹti ọlọjọ-pupọ fokiri-fokiri.
english: Many tickets sold online through auction websites such as eBay or Craigslist are partially used multi-day park-hopper tickets.

yoruba: Biotilejepe ise yi maa n saba waye, Awon Disney lodi si; tíkẹ̀tì ò sí se é gbé fúni.
english: While this is a very common activity, it is prohibited by Disney: the tickets are non-transferable.

yoruba: Ipàgọ́ lábẹ́ etí Canyon ńlá nílò ìwé àṣę ilú ẹ̀yìn.
english: Any camping below the rim in Grand Canyon requires a backcountry permit.

yoruba: Gbígbani láàyè ní gbèǹdéke láti dáàbòbò ọ̀gbun ńlá àti láti jẹ́ kó wà ní ọjọ́ àkọ́kọ́ inú oṣù, oṣù mẹ́rin sí ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ oṣù.
english: Permits are limited to protect the canyon, and become available on the 1st day of the month, four months prior to the start month.

yoruba: Torinaa iwease fun odiilu to bere ni Osu Ebibi ma w anile lati Sere .
english: Thus, a backcountry permit for any start date in May becomes available on 1 Jan.

yoruba: Àyè fún àwọn agbègbè olókìkí, bi ilè àgọ Brighy Angel ní ìkángun sí oko ẹran ọ̀sìn Phantom, lọ́pọ̀ ìgbà màa ń jẹ́ gbígbà tan nípa ìpè làti yọ̀nda tí o di gbígbà ní Ọjọ́ àkọ́kọ́ wọn si fàyẹ̀ gba mímú àyè sílẹ̀ síwáájú.
english: Space for the most popular areas, such as the Bright Angel Campground adjacent to Phantom Ranch, generally fill up by the requests received on first date they are opened to reservations.

yoruba: Wọn tí fi díè nínún áwọn ìwé àse pamó fún àwọn tó ba rìn wá sẹ bẹ̀rẹ̀r; o wà nílè fún ẹnikéni tó bá kọ́kọ́ wá.
english: There are a limited number permits reserved for walk-in requests available on a first come, first served basis.

yoruba: Wíwọ orílẹ̀èdè South Africa pẹ̀lú ọkọ̀ jẹ́ ọ̀nà ìyanu láti rí àwọn ẹwà ẹkùn náà àti láti dé àwọn ibi táwan arìnrìnajó kò lè dé.
english: Entering Southern Africa by car is an amazing way to see all the region's beauty as well as to get to places off the normal tourist routes.

yoruba: A le se eyi ni oko ayokele pelu eto sugbon 4x4 ni won saba gba ni amoran pelu pe a ko le de awon ibo miin lai lo 4x4 ti ese re ga soke daada.
english: This can be done in a normal car with careful planning but a 4x4 is highly advised and many locations are only accessible with a high wheel base 4x4.

yoruba: Fi bí o ṣe ń ṣètò sọ́kàn pé biotilejepe gúúsù Ilẹ̀ Afirika rọgbọ kìí ṣe gbogbo ìlú tó yíiká ló rí bẹ́ẹ̀.
english: Keep in mind while planning that although Southern Africa is stable not all neighboring countries are.

yoruba: Owó àti ohun èlò iwé físà ma n yàtọ̀ láti ìlú kan sí òmíràn ìlú tí o ti wa naa ma nípa nínú ẹ̀.
english: Visa requirements and costs vary from nation to nation and are affected by the country you come from.

yoruba: Orílẹ̀èdè kọ̀ọ̀kan ní àwọn òfin tó dá ń tọ́ tó ń bèèrè fáwọn ohun èlò pàjáwìrì tó yẹ kí ó wà nínú ọkọ̀.
english: Each country also has unique laws requiring what emergency items need to be in the car.

yoruba: Victoria Falls je ilu ni agbegbe iwo oorun Zimbabwe eyi ti o la enu ibode lati Livingstone, Zambia koja eyi ti o sunmo Botswana.
english: Victoria Falls is a town in the western portion of Zimbabwe, across the border from Livingstone, Zambia, and near Botswana.

yoruba: Ìlú náà tẹ̀dó ní kété sí àwọn ìdàwálẹ̀ omi, àti wípẹ́ wọn jẹ ibi ìfamọ́ra pàtàkì, sùgbón ibi ìdúrósí olókìkí fún àwọn arìnrìnàjó yìí pẹ̀sẹ̀ ànfàní lọ́pọ̀lọpọ̀ fùn àwọn arìnrìnàjó ìgbafẹ́ àti ẹ̀rò ìwòran dúró pípẹ́.
english: The town lies immediately next to the falls, and they are the major attraction, but this popular tourist destination offers both adventure seekers and sightseers plenty of opportunities for a longer stay.

yoruba: Ní àsìkò òjò, (oṣù kọkànlá sí oṣù kẹta), ìwọn omi yíó pọ̀ sí tí ìjáwásílẹ̀ yíò sí dàbí àrà.
english: In the rainy season (November to March), the water volume will be higher and the Falls will be more dramatic.

yoruba: Mo fi dá ọ lójú pé tí o bá fi lè sọdá afárá náà tàbí rìn lẹba ọ̀nà lásìkọ òjò, gbogbo ara rẹ lo máa tutù.
english: You are guaranteed to get wet if you cross the bridge or walk along the trails winding near the Falls.

yoruba: Ní ìdàkejì, nítorí bí omi náà se pọ̀tọ́ ní yóò mun nira fún ọ láti rí bí omi se ń dà—pẹ̀lú gbogbo omi.
english: On the other hand, it is precisely because the volume of water is so high that your viewing of the actual Falls will be obscured—by all the water!

yoruba: Itẹ́ òkú Tutankhamun (KV62). KV62 lè jẹ́ itẹ́ tó gbajúmọ̀ jùlọ ní orí pẹ̀tẹ́lẹ̀, àwárí ìran Howard Carter's 192, tó jẹ́ ibi sàárè tó lọ́lá jùlọ fún ọba kékeré.
english: Tomb of Tutankhamun (KV62). KV62 may be the most famous of the tombs in the Valley, the scene of Howard Carter's 1922 discovery of the almost intact royal burial of the young king.

yoruba: Ní síse àfiwé rẹ̀ sáwọn ibi ìtẹ́ òkú àwọn ọlọ́lá sí, àmọ́ṣà, ìtẹ́ òkú Tutankhamun kò yẹ ní ohun tí à ń sàbẹ̀wò sí, níbí ó se kére jù pẹ̀lú ìdálọ́lá tí kò pọ̀.
english: Compared to most of the other royal tombs, however, the tomb of Tutankhamun is barely worth visiting, being much smaller and with limited decoration.

yoruba: Ẹni tí ó bá wù láti rí èrí ìpalára sí mummy ni ìgbà tí wọ́n ń gbìyànjú láti yọ ọ́ kúrò ní inú pósí, yóò banújẹ́, nítorí orí àti èjìká ni ó ṣe é rí.
english: Anyone interested in seeing evidence of the damage to the mummy done during attempts to remove it from the coffin will be disappointed as only the head and shoulders are visible.

yoruba: Ọrọ̀ ńlá inú ibojì kò sí níbẹ̀ mọ́, sùgbọ́n wọ́n ti gbe lọ sí ilé ìkó iṣẹ́ ọnà sí ni Cairo, ní ilẹ̀ Egypt.
english: The fabulous riches of the tomb are no longer in it, but have been removed to the Egyptian Museum in Cairo.

yoruba: Ó ma dáa kí àwọn àlejò tí o ní àkókò púpọ̀ lògbà wọ́n níbò miin.
english: Visitors with limited time would be best to spend their time elsewhere.

yoruba: Phnom Krom, kìlómítà méjìlà gúsù ìwọ ̀oòrun Siem Reap. Ibi Ìjọsìn tí wọ́n kọ́ sí òpin sẹńtúrì kẹsan, nígbàtí Ọba Yasovarman wà ní orí oy.
english: Phnom Krom, 12 km southwest of Siem Reap. This hilltop temple was built at the end of the 9th century, during the reign of King Yasovarman.

yoruba: Wíwò bàìbàì ojú òfúrufú tẹ́m̀pìlì àti ìwòye láti orí odò adágún ti Tonle Sap jẹ́ kí òkè gígùn náà seyebíye.
english: The gloomy atmosphere of the temple and the view over the Tonle Sap lake make the climb to the hill worthwhile.

yoruba: Àbẹ̀wò sí ààyè náà se é dàpọ̀ pẹ̀lú ìrìn-àjò ọkọ̀ ojú omi sí adágún láì sí ìnira.
english: A visit to the site can be conveniently combined with a boat trip to the lake.

yoruba: A nílò ìwé ìkọjá Angkoṛ láti wọnú ilé Ìsìn náà torínáà ma gbàgbé láti mú ìwé ìrìn àjò rę to bá n bọ̀ ní Tonle Sap.
english: The Angkor Pass is needed to enter the temple so do not forget to bring your passport along when heading to Tonle Sap.

yoruba: Ilu Jesusalemu je iya ilu si ile Isreli ti o tobi julo,amo awon orileede to seku ati Ajo Agbaye ko ri bee.
english: Jerusalem is the capital and largest city of Israel, though most other countries and the United Nations do not recognize it as Israel's capital.

yoruba: Ìlú ìgbàani tí ò wà ní Òke Juda ni ìtàn tó múnilọ́kàn tí ó sì ti ń gbòòrò láti bí ẹgbẹ̀rún ọdúnsẹ́yìn.
english: The ancient city in the Judean Hills has a fascinating history spanning thousands of years.

yoruba: Ilú náa jẹ́ mímọ́ nínú ẹ̀sìn mẹ́ta- Judaism,ọmọlẹ́yìn Kírísítì àti Ìsìláàmù, tí ó sì dúró gẹ́gẹ́ bí olú ìlú ẹ̀sìn, ìgbàgbọ́ àti àṣà.
english: The city is holy to the three monotheistic religions - Judaism, Christianity and Islam, and serves as a spiritual, religious, and cultural center.

yoruba: Látàrí bí ìlú náà se se pàtàkì fún ẹ̀sìn àti pàápàá jùlọ fáwọn agbègbè tó ti gbó níìlú, Jerusalem ló jẹ́ ibi ìrìnafẹ́ ní Isreal.
english: Due to the religious significance of the city, and in particular the many sites of the Old City area, Jerusalem is one of the main tourist destinations in Israel.

yoruba: ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ìtàn, àṣà, ìṣe, ìwádìí ìjìnlẹ̀ ni ó rọ̀ mọ́ Jerúsálẹ́mù, bákan náà ni ọ̀pọ̀ ilé ìtajà elérò púpọ̀, ilé oúnjẹ àti ti ohun mímu ni ó wà níbẹ.
english: Jerusalem has many historic, archeological and cultural sites, along with vibrant and crowded shopping centers, cafés, and restaurants.

yoruba: Ecuador fẹ́ kí àwọn ará ìlú Cuban gba lẹ́tà ìpeni ṣáájú wíwọlé sí Ecuador ní ibùdókọ̀ ọkọ̀ òfurufú tàbí ibí ìgbaniwọlé.
english: Ecuador requires that Cuban citizens receive an invitation letter prior to entering Ecuador through international airports or frontier admission points.

yoruba: Iléeṣẹ́ tó ń rí sọ́rọ̀ ilẹ̀ okèrè ilẹ̀ Ecuadorian, kí wọ́n sì tẹ̀lé àwọn àlàkalẹ̀ kọ̀ọ̀kan.
english: This letter must be legalized by the Ecuadorian Ministry of Foreign Affairs, and comply with certain requirements.

yoruba: Àwọn ohun tí a fẹ́ wọ̀nyí ni a gbé kalẹ̀ láti jẹ́ kí ìbáṣepọ̀ tí ó dá mọ́nrán wà láàárin orílẹ̀èdè méjèèj.
english: These requirements are designed to provide an organized migratory flow between both countries.

yoruba: Àwọn ọmọ orìlé-èdè Cuban tí wọ́n ní káàdì ìgbéèlú ní láti sàbẹ̀wò sí ọ́fíìsì asojú orìlé-èdè Ecuador láti gba ìyònda fún ètò ìmúlò yí.
english: Cuban citizens who are US green card holders should visit an Ecuadorian Consulate to obtain an exemption to this requirement.

yoruba: Ìwé ìrìnnà rẹ gbodò wà ní ìbámu o kéré tan osu mẹfa si àwọn ọjọ́ rínrin ìrin-àjò rẹ. Ìwé ìwọlé tọ péyẹ jẹ lílò làti sẹ èrí dídúró rẹ.
english: Your passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond your travel dates. A round/onward trip ticket is needed to prove the length of your stay.

yoruba: Ìrìn afẹ́ ò wọ́n fáwọn èrò tó bá pọ̀, torínà bí o bá ń dábọ̀ tàbí tí o bá wà pẹ̀lú ọ̀rọ́ rọ kanṣoṣo, gbìyànjú láti bá àwọn míìràn pàdé kí ẹ sì se àkójọ ẹgbẹ́ eléèyàn mẹ́rin sí mẹ́fà fúnrayín kí ẹ lè rí owó tó mọ níwọ̀n san léèyàn kọ̀ọ̀kan.
english: Tours are cheaper for larger groups, so if you're by yourself or with just one friend, try to meet other people and form a group of four to six for a better per-person rate.

yoruba: Síbẹ̀síbẹ̀, èyí kò yẹ kó pò ọ́ lọ́fun pọ̀, nítorí ọ̀pọ̀ ìgbà ní wọ́n má ń da àwọn arìnrìn àjò afẹ́ papọ̀ sínú àwọn ọkọ̀.
english: However, this shouldn't really be off your concern, because often tourists are shuffled around to fill the cars.

yoruba: Ń se ló dàbí ọ̀nà ati má a tan àwọn ènìyàn jẹ́, kíwọ́n lè gbàgbọ́ wípé wọ́n nílò àti má a sanwó sí i.
english: It seems actually to be more a way of tricking people into believing they have to pay more.

yoruba: Èyí tó gàbá lé ẹkùn àríwá ẹ̀yìn Machu Picchu ni òkè yíyọ̀, ó tún jẹ́ ìtàkùn fáwọn àwòrán.
english: Towering above the north end of Machu Picchu is this steep mountain, often the backdrop to many photos of the ruins.

yoruba: Ó jọ bí ìgbà tó le láti abẹ́, ó sì lọ lẹ̀ ó tún le láti gun òkè, ṣùgbọ́n àwọn tí wọ́n ní okun àti agbára lè gùn ní àárín ìṣẹ́jú márùnlélógójì.
english: It looks a bit daunting from below, and it is a steep and difficult ascent, but most reasonably fit persons should be able to make it in about 45 minutes.

yoruba: Wọn tó òkúta s’ẹ́gbẹ́ púpọ̀ ojú ọ̀nà náà àti pé níbi tó gajù okùn onírin pèsè réèlì ọwọ́ fún àtìlẹ́yìn.
english: Stone steps are laid along most of the path, and in the steeper sections steel cables provide a supporting handrail.

yoruba: Wọ́n sọ pé, kí a ma retí mímí húlehúle, kí a sì tajú ara wá ní ibi tí ó bá lọsílẹ̀,pàápàá nígbàtí ilẹ̀ bá tuntun, nítorí ó lè ní ewu kíákíá.
english: That said, expect to be out of breath, and take care in the steeper portions, especially when wet, as it can become dangerous quickly.

yoruba: Ihò kékeré kan wà ní ẹ̀gbẹ́ kan lókè tí a lè gbà kọjá, ó lọlẹ̀ ó sì fúnpa.
english: There's a tiny cave near the top that must be passed through, it is quite low and a rather tight squeeze.

yoruba: Fífi ọkọ̀ ojú omi wo ààyè àti awọn abẹ̀mí inú igbó Galapagos ló dára jùlọ, bí Charles Darwin se se ní ọdún 1835.
english: Seeing the sites and wildlife of the Galapagos is best done by boat, just as Charles Darwin did it in 1835.

yoruba: Àwọn ọkọ̀ orí omi tó ń rin odò Galapagos – wà láti ìwọ̀n elérò mẹ́jọ sí ọgọ́rùn-ún.
english: Over 60 cruise ships ply the Galapagos waters - ranging in size from 8 to 100 passengers.

yoruba: Ọ̀pọ̀ èèyàn ló ma ń gbààyè sílẹ̀ saájú (torí ọkọ̀ ojú omi ti ma ń kún fọ́fọ́ láwọn àkókò tó ga).
english: Most people book their place well in advance (as the boats are usually full during the high season).

yoruba: Mọ̀ dájú pé aṣojú tí o kọ sínú ìwé pàtàkì Galapagos pẹ̀lú ìmọ̀ kíkún lórí ọ̀kanòjọ̀kan ọkọ̀ ojú omi.
english: Be sure the agent through whom you book is a Galapagos specialist with a good knowledge of a wide variety of ships.

yoruba: Eleyii yio ri daju pe awon ohun pato ti o ni ife si ati/tabi idiwo bamu pelu oko oju omi tio dara siwon jul.
english: This will ensure that your particular interests and/or constraints are matched with the ship most suitable to them.

yoruba: Ki awon ara Spain to de ni odun 16th century, ariwa orile ede Chile wa ni abe isejoba Inca nigbati awon abinibi Araucanians (Mapuche) n gbe ni aringbungbun ati guusu Chile.
english: Before the Spanish arrived in the 16th century, northern Chile was under Inca rule while the indigenous Araucanians (Mapuche) inhabited central and southern Chile.

yoruba: Àwọn Mapuche náà jẹ́ ọ̀kan lára èèyàn Amẹrika tó gba òmìnira kẹ́yìn, tí wọn kò kó sínú ẹ̀yà tó ń sọ èdè Spain títí di ìgbà tí ilẹ́ Chile gba òmìnira.
english: The Mapuche were also one of the last independent American indigenous groups, that were not fully absorbed into Spanish-speaking rule until after Chile's independence.

yoruba: Ṣùgbọ́n ìlú Chile ní òmínira ní ọdún 1810 (ní àárín ogun napoleon tí ó jẹ́ kí Spain ní ìjọba tí kò ṣiṣẹ́ fún ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ̀ ọdún}, ìpinnu olúborí Spain kò wá sí ìmúṣẹ títí di 1818.
english: Although Chile declared independence in 1810 (amid the Napoleonic wars that left Spain without a functioning central government for a couple of years), decisive victory over the Spanish was not achieved until 1818.

yoruba: Olominira Dominikan (Ede Speeni: Republica Dominicana ) jẹ́ ilú Karibiani tó pẹ̀lú ìlú Haiti wà ní apá ìlà orùn irekusu Ispaniola.
english: The Dominican Republic (Spanish: República Dominicana) is a Caribbean country that occupies the eastern half of the island of Hispaniola, which it shares with Haiti

yoruba: Yàtọ̀ sí àwọn yẹ̀pẹ̀ funfun àti àlà ilẹ̀ orí òkè, orílẹ̀èdè ló jẹ́ ilé fáwọn ìlú ilẹ̀ yúròpù ní America, èyí tó ti wà lára Santo Domingo.
english: Besides white sand beaches and mountain landscapes, the country is home to the oldest European city in the Americas, now part of Santo Domingo.

yoruba: Àwọn ará Taínos àti Caribes ló kọ́kọ́ wà ní erékùsù. Àwọn Caribes ló má ń sọ èdè Arawakani tí wọ́n dé ní ǹkan bi ẹgbẹ̀rún mẹ́wàá BCE.
english: The island was first inhabited by the Taínos and Caribes. The Caribes were an Arawakan-speaking people who had arrived around 10,000 BCE.

yoruba: Láàrín ọdún díẹ̀ tó tẹ̀lé àwọn aṣàwákiri Europe, iye àwọn ènìyàn ní Tainos ti dínkù nípasẹ̀ àwọn tó ṣẹgun Spain
english: Within a few short years following the arrival of European explorers, the population of Tainos had significantly been reduced by the Spanish conquerors

yoruba: Níbàámu pẹ̀lú Fray Bartolomé de las Casas (Tratado de las Indias) láàrin ọdún 1492 àti 1498, àwọn aṣẹ́gun ilẹ̀ Spain sekúpa àwọn Taíno tíye wọ́n tó ọgọ́rùn ún ẹgbẹ̀rún.
english: Based on Fray Bartolomé de las Casas (Tratado de las Indias) between 1492 and 1498 the Spanish conquerors killed around 100,000 Taínos.

yoruba: Jardin de la Union. A kọ́ ààyè yí gẹ́gẹ́ bíi gbàgede fún ilé ìjọsìn okan ni orundun ketadinlogun, èyí tí tẹ́ḿpìlì de San Diego jẹ́ ilé kan soso tó wà di ìsinsìnyí.
english: Jardín de la Unión. This space was built as the atrium for a 17th-century convent, of which the Templo de San Diego is the sole surviving building.

yoruba: Òhun ni wọ́n ń lò gẹ́gẹ́ bí ilé ìtajà gbogbogbo lọ́wọ́lọ́wọ́, fún ìdí èyí, oríṣìíríṣìí nǹkan ni ó ń lọ níbẹ̀ tọ̀sán-tòru.
english: It now functions as the central plaza, and always has a lot of things going on, day and night.

yoruba: Àwọn ilé óúnjẹ́ yí ọgbà náà ká, ní ọ̀san àti ní alé wọ́n fún central gazebo ní àwọn orin kíkọ.
english: There are a number of restaurants surrounding the garden, and in the afternoons and evening there free concerts are often given from the central gazebo.

yoruba: Callejon del Beso (Titi Ifenukenu). Eyinkule meji ti 69 centimeters nikan lo wa laarin won je ile itan ife atijo.
english: Callejon del Beso (Alley of the Kiss). Two balconies separated by only 69 centimeters is home of an old love legend.

yoruba: Fun owo die awon omode kan a so itan naa fun e.
english: For a few pennies some children will tell you the story.

yoruba: Irukusu Boweni jẹ gbajumo ìrìn àjò ọjọ́ kan tàbí ìparí ọ̀sẹ̀, to pèsè kayaki, irin, ọjà, ilé ounje àti púpọ̀ si.
english: Bowen Island is a popular day trip or weekend excursion offering kayaking, hiking, shops, restaurants, and more.

yoruba: Ojúlówó agbègbè yìí la gbé kalẹ̀ ní apá gúúsù ti Howe Sound lẹ́gbẹ̀ẹ́ Vancouver, ó sì rọrùn láti débẹ̀ lórí omi tí ó la erékùsù Granville kọjá ní ìlú Vancouver.
english: This authentic community is located in Howe Sound just off Vancouver, and is easily accessed via scheduled water taxis departing Granville Island in downtown Vancouver.

yoruba: Fún àwọn tí wọn bá fẹ́ràn láti máa gbáfẹ́ ní ìta, rínrìn lọ sí òkun àti àárín òfurufú dár.
english: For those who enjoy outdoor activities, a trek up the Sea to Sky corridor is essential.

yoruba: A fọn fèèrè (ìwakọ̀ wákàtí kan ó lé márùn ún láti Vancouver) gbówó lórí à mọ́ ó gbajúmọ̀ látàrí ìdíje ìgbà òtútù Olympic lọ́dún 2010.
english: Whistler (1.5 hour drive from Vancouver) is expensive but well-known because of the 2010 Winter Olympics.

yoruba: Má a gbádùn àwọn eréje ìfò inú yìnyín tó dára jù nígbà òtútù ní ẹkùn àríwá America, àti wíwa alupùpù lórí àpáta to gbámúsé jù lákòókò ooru.
english: In the winter, enjoy some of the best skiing in North America, and in the summer try some authentic mountain biking.

yoruba: Àwọn ìwé ìgbanilááyè gbodọ ti wà ní ìsẹ̀tò síwáájú. O gbodò ní ìgbanilááyè láti dúró mọ́jú ní Sirena.
english: Permits must be reserved in advance. You must have a permit to stay overnight at Sirena.

yoruba: Sirena ni àgọ rénjì ṣoṣo tó ní àye ilé ìtura àti ounje gbígbóná pẹ̀lú ipago. Ipago láìsí ounje títà nìkan ni àwọn ìpàgọ́ La Liona, San Pedrilo ati Los Patos ma n ṣe.
english: Sirena is the only ranger station that offers dormitory lodging and hot meals in addition to camping. La Leona, San Pedrillo, and Los Patos offer only camping with no food service.

yoruba: Ó ṣe é ṣe láti rí àṣẹ ibi ìgbọ́kọ̀ sí gbà láti ilé Ranger ni Puerto Jiménez, ṣùgbọ́n wọn kìí gba kààdì ìpọwó
english: It is possible to secure park permits directly from the Ranger Station in Puerto Jiménez, but they do not accept credit cards

yoruba: Ise Ogba Idaraya (MINAE) o ki n fun eeyan ni iwe ase ju osu kan ki eyan to ma de.
english: The Park Service (MINAE) does not issue park permits more than one month in advance of anticipated arrival.

yoruba: Iléeṣẹ́ CafeNet El Sol ń gba ọgbọ̀n dọ́la US lórí ààyè ìgbásílẹ̀ tàbí dọ́là mẹ́wàá fún ọjọ́ kan; ẹ̀kúnrẹ́rẹ́ wà ní ìtàkùn Corcovado.
english: CafeNet El Sol offers a reservation service for a fee of US$30, or $10 for one-day passes; details on their Corcovado page.

yoruba: Cook Island jẹ island orílẹ́ èdè tó wà ní ẹgbẹ́ ọ̀fẹ́ pẹ̀lú ìlú New Zealand, tó wà ní Polynesia, ní àárín South Pacific Ocean.
english: The Cook Islands are an island country in free association with New Zealand, located in Polynesia, in the middle of the South Pacific Ocean.

yoruba: O je asipelago to ni irekusu 15 kaakiri osa fun km2 mimionu 2..
english: It is an archipelago with 15 islands spread out over 2.2 million km2 of ocean.

yoruba: Pẹ̀lú lílo àkókò kanáà pẹ̀lú Hawaii, àwọn erékùsù ma ń ro Hawaii gẹ́gẹ́ bí “Hawaii tó wà lábẹ́”.
english: With the same time zone as Hawaii, the islands are sometimes thought of as "Hawaii down under".

yoruba: Bó tilẹ̀ jẹ́pẹ́ o kéré́, o se ìrántí fún àwọn àlẹjò àtijọ́ ilẹ̀ Hawaii kí a tó sọ di ìpínlẹ,̀ láìsí gbogbo àwọn ilẹ́ ìtura nlanla arìnrìn àjò ìgbafẹ́ àti àwọn ìdàgbàsókè míràn.
english: Though smaller, it reminds some elderly visitors of Hawaii before statehood without all the large tourist hotels and other development.

yoruba: Kò sí ìlú Kankan ní erékùsù Cook ṣùgbọn erékùsù mẹ́ẹ̀ẹ́dógún ọ̀tọ̀ọ̀tọ̀ ni ó ní. Awọn tí wọ́n ṣe pàtàkì jùlọ ni Rarotonga àti Aitutaki.
english: The Cook Islands do not have any cities but are composed of 15 different islands. The main ones are Rarotonga and Aitutaki.

yoruba: Láwọn orílèèdè tó ni ìdàgbàsóké lónìí, pípèsè ibùsùn tó jíire àti oúnjẹ òwúrọ̀ ti di bí i iṣẹ̀ ọnà.
english: In developed countries today, providing deluxe bed and breakfasts has been raised to a sort of art-form.

yoruba: Ní ìparí lókè, B%Bs dára tí a bá ń wo ibùsùn àti óúnjẹ àárọ̀.
english: At the top end, B&Bs obviously compete mainly on two main things: bedding and breakfast.

yoruba: Bakanna, ni iru ibi ti o dara bayi, eniyan yio ni lero lati ri awon ibusun ti o rewa, boya bi awon ibusun ti a fi owo se loso tabi awon ibusun onise ona.
english: Accordingly, at the finest such establishments one is apt to find the most luxurious bedding, maybe a handmade quilt or an antique bed.

yoruba: Ó seése kí oùnje òwúrọ̀ kọ pẹ̀lú àwọn ìpèsè aládìídù ẹkùn náà tàbí àsè pàtàkì tí agbàlejò.
english: Breakfast may include seasonal delights of the region or the host's speciality dish.

yoruba: Ààtò rẹ̀ lè jẹ́ ilé tó ti pẹ́ tónítàn pẹ̀lú àwọn iṣẹ́ ọnà àti ààyè ìlúùwẹ́.
english: The setting might be an historic old building with antique furnishings, manicured grounds and a swimming pool.

yoruba: Wíwọ ọkọ̀ rẹ, pẹ̀lú lílọ sí ọ̀nà jínjìn ní àyò abínibí ní ọ̀nà tó rọrùn .
english: Getting into your own car and heading off on a long road trip has an intrinsic appeal in its simplicity.

yoruba: Yàtò sí àwọn ọkọ̀ ńlá, o seése kí o tí ní ìmọ̀lára ọkọ̀ rẹ , kí o sì mọ àwọn ìdiwọ̀n rẹ̀ .
english: Unlike larger vehicles, you are probably already familiar with driving your car and know its limitations.

yoruba: Síse àgbékalẹ̀ àgọ́ sórí ohun ìní ọlọ́danni tàbí ní ìlú le fa àfojúsí tí a kò fẹ́.
english: Setting up a tent on private property or in a town of any size can easily attract unwanted attention.

yoruba: Má fàá gùn lilo mọ́́tò ara eni dara fún irin ajo oju ona ṣùgbọ́́n fún ara re kii se ona lati fi “se ibud.”
english: In short, using your car is a great way to take a road trip but seldom in itself a way to "camp".

yoruba: Ibudo inuoko seese ti o ba ni oko nla mnifaani, SUV abi Station Wagon to ni ijoko ti o le sun sile.
english: Car camping is possible if you have a large minivan, SUV, Sedan or Station Wagon with seats that lay down.

yoruba: Àwọn ilé ìtura kan ma n ní ohun ìní láti àkókò iyebíye ti relúwé elérúkú àtì ọkọ̀ inú omi; ki Ogun Àgbáyé Kejì tó jà ní senturi 19th tàbí ìbẹ̀rẹ̀ senturi 20th.
english: Some hotels have a heritage from the golden age of steam railways and ocean liners; before the Second World War, in the 19th or early 20th centuries.

yoruba: Ilé ìtura yìí ní àwọn tó lówó tí wọ́n sì ní ọ́rọ̀ ma dúró si, wọ́n sì ní àyè ibi óúnjẹ́.
english: These hotels were where the rich and the famous of the day would stay, and often had fine dining and nightlife.

yoruba: Lára àwọn ìsesí wọn ni ẹ̀ṣọ́ aṣọ tó dúró ‘re ọlọ́jọ́ pípẹ́, àìní àwọn ohun amáyérọrùn tìgbàlódé àtàwọn ohun àkúwórí míràn.
english: The old fashioned fittings, the lack of the latest amenities, and a certain graceful agedness are also part of their character.

yoruba: Biotilejepe àdání ni wọ́n jẹ́, wọ́n ma n sábà gba àwọn olórí ìlú àti àwọn èyàn pàtàkì òmíràn wọlé.
english: While they are usually privately owned, they sometimes accommodate visiting heads of state and other dignitaries.

yoruba: Arìnrìnàjò tó lówó púpọ̀ lè rísí ìrìn àjò káà kiri ay.
english: A traveller with piles of money might consider a round the world flight, broken up with stays in many of these hotels.

yoruba: Ìgbàlejò tó ṣe pàṣípàra nẹ́tíwọkì jẹ ilé iṣẹ́ tí o so àwọn arinrìn àjò pọ̀ pẹ̀lú àwọn ìbílẹ̀ nínu àwọn ìlú tí wọ́n fẹ́ ṣe àbẹ̀wò sí.
english: A hospitality exchange network is the organization that connects travelers with locals in the cities they are going to visit.

yoruba: Láti darapọ̀ mọ́ irú àkójọ bí èyí, o kàn nílò láti buwọlu fọ́ọ̀mù lórí ìtàkùn ayélujára; lóòótọ́, irú àkójọ yìí nílò àyẹ̀wò míràn.
english: Joining such a network usually just requires filling out an online form; although some networks offer or require additional verification.

yoruba: Won a wa fun ni ni akosile gbogbo awon agbalejo to wa nile o le je loju iwe tabi loju intaneeti, nigbamiin won ma n fi ero awon onirinajo miin.
english: A listing of available hosts is then provided either in print and/or online, sometimes with references and reviews by other travelers.

yoruba: Won bere couchsurfing ni Sere 2004 leyin ti onimo komputa Kasi Fentoni ri tikeeti lati fo lo si Iselandi lofe sugbon ko ri bi duro s.
english: Couchsurfing was founded in January 2004 after computer programmer Casey Fenton found a cheap flight to Iceland but did not have a place to stay.

yoruba: Ó kọ̀wé ránṣẹ́ sí àwọn àkẹ́ẹ̀kọ́ ní ilé ẹ̀kọ́ gíga fásitì abẹ́lé, ó sì rí àgbàyanu ìfilọ̀ fún iléègbé ọ̀fẹ́ gbà.
english: He emailed students at the local university and received an overwhelming number of offers for free accommodation.

yoruba: Àwọn ilé ìtura ma ń tọ́jú àwọn ọ̀dọ́mọdé- àwọn àlejò wọn ló ma ń wà lọ́mọ ogún ọdún- àmọ́ o lè rí àwọn àgbàlagbà arìnrìnàjò náà níbẹ̀ pẹ̀lu.
english: Hostels cater primarily to young people – a typical guest is in their twenties – but you can often find older travellers there too.

yoruba: Ósọ̀wọ́n láti rí àwọn ìdílé pẹ̀lú ọmọ, ṣùgbọ́n àwọn iléègbé àwọn akẹ́ẹ̀kọ́ máa ń fàyè gbà wọn ní yàrá àdáni.
english: Families with children are a rare sight, but some hostels do allow them in private rooms.

yoruba: Ìlú Beijing ní orílẹ̀èdè China ni yío gba àlejò ìdíje Olympic nígbà òtútù lọ́dún 2022, èyí tí yíò jẹ́ ìlú àkọ́kọ́ tí yíò gbàlejò olympic ti àkókò ooru àti òtútù.
english: The city of Beijing in China will be the host city of the Olympic Winter Games in 2022, which will make it the first city to have hosted both the summer and winter Olympics.

yoruba: Beijing yíò gbà àlejò ayẹyẹ ìbẹ̀rẹ àti ìkásẹ̀nílẹ̀ pẹ̀lú àwọn eré ìdárayá yìnyín nínú ilé.
english: Beijing will host the opening and closing ceremonies and the indoor ice events.

yoruba: Àwọn ètò fífò míràn ni yíò wáyé ní ibi ìfò Zhangjiakou, ní ǹkan bi kìlómítà okòólénígba (ogóje máilì) látọ̀dọ Beijing..
english: Other skiing events will be at the Taizicheng ski area in Zhangjiakou, about 220 km (140 miles) from Beijing.

yoruba: Púpọ̀ nínú àwọn tempili lo ma n ṣe ayẹyẹ ọdún lati ìparí Belú dé àrín Èbìbí tí ó ma n yàtọ̀ síra torí kalenda tempili kookan.
english: Most of the temples do have an Annual festival starting from November end to Mid of May, which varies according to each temple's annual calendar.

yoruba: Ọ̀pọ̀lọpọ àwọn ayẹyẹ tẹ̀ḿpìlì ni wọ́n ń se gẹ́gẹ́ bí i àjọ̀dú tẹ́m̀pìlì tàbí ayẹyẹ ọjọ́ ìbí aládarí òriṣà tàbí àwọn ayẹyẹ gbòógì míràn tí wọ́n lọ́ mọ́ tẹ́ḿpìlì.
english: Most of the temple festivals is celebrated as part of temple's anniversary or presiding deity's birthday or any other major event associated with the temple.

yoruba: Àjọ̀dún Ilé ìjọsin Kerala ó dùnwò pẹ̀lú ìwòde àwọn erin tí a ṣe lọ́ṣọ̀ọ́, àwọn olórin ilé ìjọsìn àti àwọn ayęyę òmíràn.
english: Kerala's temple festivals are very interesting to see, with regular procession of decorated elephants, temple orchestra and other festivities.

yoruba: Ijade Aye Kan (ti a maa n pe ni Isipaya Aye tabi “Espo” lasan) is ni ayeye nla gbogbogbo nipa ara ati sayensi.
english: A World's Fair (commonly called World Exposition, or simply Expo) is large international festival of arts and sciences.

yoruba: Awon ilu to ko pa fi idan alara ati arikogbon han ni awon iyewu ile lati toka si awon oro agbaye tabi asa ati itan ilu won.
